[b][size=5]ENGLISH[/size][/b]

[b]Temporal Panic Button[/b] is an entertainment-focused mod. Please don't take it too seriously.
Multiplayer functionality has only been tested to a limited extent. If you encounter bugs or have suggestions, feel free to leave feedback.
When a player triggers a mine, turret-type trap, or presses the manual activation key, a Time Stop will be activated. During Time Stop, the activator can move, heal, throw items, and shoot, while the world, traps, creatures, explosive timers, and players without Time Stop permission are frozen.
[heading]Features[/heading]
[list]
[*]Time Stop can be triggered by mines, turrets / gun traps, or a manual hotkey.[/*]
[*]Intelligence Growth Mode enabled by default: higher Intelligence grants longer Time Stop duration and shorter cooldown.[/*]
[*]KrokMP multiplayer synchronization support. Multiple players can overlap their Time Stops, and only players currently granted Time Stop permission can move.[/*]
[*]Blue screen effect, shockwave, start/end visual effects, sound effects, attack poses, and an Observer acting as your stand.[/*]
[*]Prevents bleeding, unconsciousness progression, and body stat changes while Time Stop is active.[/*]
[*]Allows self-treatment during Time Stop and can optionally ignore pain and consciousness penalties affecting medical speed.[/*]
[*]Thrown objects freeze in mid-air and continue traveling after Time Stop ends.[/*]
[*]Firearms can be fired during Time Stop with delayed projectile effects; actual damage is applied when Time Stop ends.[/*]
[*]Prevents certain traps from continuing to trigger or track targets during Time Stop, including jump pads, bear traps, coils, spike traps, and sonic cannons.[/*]
[*]Includes a temporary Dynamite fuse-cutting feature. Dynamite timers pause during Time Stop, and fuses can be shortened to create near-instant explosions on the next use.[/*]
[/list][heading]Installation[/heading]
[list=1]
[*]Install BepInEx.[/*]
[*]Place TemporalPanicButton.dll into: BepInEx/plugins/[/*]
[*]Launch the game and configure the mod under: Settings -> Gameplay[/*]
[/list][heading]Optional Time Stop Sounds[/heading]
By default, the mod does not include Time Stop start/end sounds.
To use custom sounds, create (or use the automatically generated) folder:Â  BepInEx/plugins/TemporalPanicButtonAssets/
Place the following files inside:
Time Stop Start Sound:
timestop_start.wav
or
timestop_start.ogg
Time Stop End Sound:
timestop_end.wav
or
timestop_end.ogg
If bothÂ .wavÂ andÂ .oggÂ versions exist, the mod will load whichever valid file it finds first.
[heading]Settings[/heading]
Location:
[code]Settings -> Gameplay[/code][list]
[*][b]Temporal Panic Button[/b][i](Default: Enabled)[/i][/*]
[/list]Master switch. Disabling it completely turns off all Time Stop functionality.
[list]
[*][b]Intelligence Growth Mode[/b][i](Default: Enabled)[/i][/*]
[/list]Time Stop duration and cooldown scale with Intelligence.
[list]
[*][b]Time Stop Duration[/b][i](Default: 5 seconds)[/i][/*]
[/list]Fixed Time Stop duration when Intelligence Growth Mode is disabled.
[list]
[*][b]Time Stop Cooldown[/b][i](Default: 15 seconds)[/i][/*]
[/list]Fixed Time Stop cooldown when Intelligence Growth Mode is disabled.
[list]
[*][b]Trigger On Mines[/b][i](Default: Enabled)[/i][/*]
[/list]Activates Time Stop when stepping on a mine.
[list]
[*][b]Trigger On Turrets[/b][i](Default: Enabled)[/i][/*]
[/list]Activates Time Stop when triggering a turret or gun trap.
[list]
[*][b]Manual Time Stop[/b][i](Default: Enabled)[/i][/*]
[/list]Allows manual activation of Time Stop.
[list]
[*][b]Manual Time Stop Key[/b][i](Default: V)[/i][/*]
[/list]Hotkey used for manual activation.
[list]
[*][b]Steady Medical Hands[/b][i](Default: Enabled)[/i][/*]
[/list]Ignores medical speed penalties caused by pain or reduced consciousness.
[list]
[*][b]Time Stop Effect Intensity[/b][i](Default: 1, Range: 0â€“2)[/i][/*]
[/list]Controls the strength of visual Time Stop effects.
[list]
[*][b]Time Stop Sound Volume[/b][i](Default: 0.9, Range: 0â€“1)[/i][/*]
[/list]Controls the volume of Time Stop sound effects.

[heading]Intelligence Growth Mode[/heading]
Enabled by default.
When enabled, players cannot directly configure Time Stop duration and cooldown through the settings menu. Instead, these values scale with Intelligence.
Adds a little progression and replayability.
[list]
[*]Intelligence below 7: Time Stop has not yet been learned and cannot be activated manually.[/*]
[*]Intelligence 7: Unlocks Time Stop. Duration approximately 4 seconds, cooldown approximately 240 seconds.[/*]
[*]Intelligence 20: Maximum Time Stop level. Duration approximately 20 seconds, cooldown approximately 120 seconds.[/*]
[*]Progression from Intelligence 7 to 20 is gradual. The scaling curve is not completely linear, and later levels provide more noticeable improvements.[/*]
[/list]If Intelligence Growth Mode is disabled, the mod uses the fixed values configured in: Time Stop Duration andÂ  Time Stop Cooldown
[heading]Gameplay[/heading]
[b]Triggering Time Stop[/b]
Time Stop can be activated by:
[list]
[*]Stepping on a mine.[/*]
[*]Triggering a turret or gun trap.[/*]
[*]Pressing the manual activation key (default:V).[/*]
[/list]Manual activation is blocked while menus such as pause menus, trading screens, and container inventories are open.

[b]During Time Stop[/b]
[list]
[*]The activator can move and play normal movement animations.[/*]
[*]Most world objects, traps, creatures, and countdown timers are frozen.[/*]
[*]Body status progression is paused, including bleeding, dehydration, and unconsciousness.[/*]
[*]The medical interface can be opened for self-treatment.[/*]
[*]IfÂ  Steady Medical Hands is enabled, medical mini-game speed is calculated as if the player were in normal condition.[/*]
[*]Thrown items remain suspended in mid-air until Time Stop ends.[/*]
[*]Firearms create delayed projectile effects; actual hits and damage are resolved when Time Stop finishes.[/*]
[*]A status display appears at the top of the screen showing your remaining Time Stop duration and cooldown. If another player is currently stopping time and you are not allowed to move, their remaining Time Stop duration is displayed instead.[/*]
[/list][heading]When Time Stop Ends[/heading]
[list]
[*]End visual and sound effects are played.[/*]
[*]Delayed turret shots, gunfire, thrown items, and other queued actions are resolved.[/*]
[*]If no other player is currently stopping time, the world returns to normal.[/*]
[/list][heading]Multiplayer[/heading]
The mod works in single-player on its own and can optionally synchronize Time Stop effects when KrokMP is installed.
In multiplayer, Intelligence Growth Mode now follows the host setting. If the host enables it, clients follow it. If the host disables it, clients follow that as well, instead of using their own local setting.
Multiplayer behavior works roughly as follows:
[list]
[*]If Player A activates Time Stop, A can move while Player B is frozen.[/*]
[*]If Player B activates Time Stop during A's Time Stop, both A and B can move.[/*]
[*]If A's Time Stop ends first while B's is still active, A becomes frozen and B continues moving.[/*]
[*]Once all active Time Stops have ended, the world resumes normal behavior.[/*]
[/list]Turret and gun-trap triggers attempt to identify the actual triggering player to avoid duplicate Time Stop activations from different client perspectives.
Multiplayer synchronization depends on KrokMP and should still be considered experimental.
If KrokMP is not installed or not running, the mod behaves as a single-player mod.
[heading]Temporary Dynamite Feature[/heading]
This mod currently includes a temporary Dynamite fuse feature.
It may be removed later or moved into a separate mod.
[list]
[*]Lit Dynamite timers pause during Time Stop.[/*]
[*]While holding Dynamite, a CUT FUSE
button appears on the right side of the screen.[/*]
[*]Clicking it causes the next activation of that Dynamite to use a fuse length of approximately 0.1econds.[/*]
[*]The feature only affects Dynamite and is designed to avoid interfering with other items.[/*]
[/list]Basically, you can prepare an almost-instant explosion during Time Stop and watch it detonate the moment time starts moving again.
[heading]Compatibility[/heading]
If conflicts occur with other mods, first check whether those mods also modify:
[list]
[*]Trap behavior[/*]
[*]Player body status systems[/*]
[*]Medical mini-games[/*]
[*]Camera time scaling[/*]
[*]KrokMP synchronization logic[/*]
[/list]These areas are the most likely sources of incompatibility.
Author: MistalCry

Name: Cu-Stats
Description:
[b]CuStats[/b]

CuStats turns the things do in Casualties: Unknown into numbers. It watches block breaks, item pickups, weapon uses, food and medical consumption, layer descents, mob kills and trader encounters, then renders them in a tabbed panel grouped by category, with totals split between "all saves" and "this world".

CuStats æŠŠåœ¨ Casualties: Unknown é‡Œåšçš„æ¯ä»¶äº‹éƒ½ç¿»è¯‘æˆæ•°å­—ï¼šæ–¹å—ç ´åã€ç‰©å“æ‹¾å–ã€é£Ÿç‰© / åŒ»ç–—ç”¨é‡ã€ä¸‹å±‚æ¬¡æ•°ã€ç›¸é‡æ±‚ç”Ÿè€…ï¼›é¢æ¿æŒ‰ç±»åˆ«åˆ†é¡µå±•ç¤ºï¼Œå¹¶æŠŠ"æ‰€æœ‰å­˜æ¡£æ€»è®¡"ä¸Ž"å½“å‰ä¸–ç•Œ"ä¸¤æ¡æ•°æ®çº¿åˆ†å¼€ã€‚

[b]Tabs[/b]

- **General** â€” distance walked, layers descended, deepest layer, total blocks broken, items picked / used, mobs killed, survivors met.
- **Blocks broken** â€” every block id you ever cracked, with sprite icons.
- **Items picked up** â€” what you pulled from the world, sorted by count.
- **Food / Medical / Combat / Misc** â€” what you actually consumed, with a one-click toggle between "durability equivalent" (e.g. `1.02 items`) and "use count" (e.g. `3 times`).
- **Settings** â€” verbose log toggle, font scale slider, update check toggle, panel hotkey, UI language (auto / Chinese / English).
- **About** â€” version, links, credits, dependencies.

- **ç»¼åˆ** â€” ç§»åŠ¨è·ç¦»ã€ä¸‹é™å±‚æ•°ã€æœ€æ·±å±‚æ•°ã€æ–¹å—ç ´åæ€»æ•°ã€ç‰©å“æ‹¾å–ä¸Žä½¿ç”¨ã€ç›¸é‡æ±‚ç”Ÿè€…ã€‚
- **ç ´åæ–¹å—** â€” æ¯ä¸ªè¢«ä½ æŒ–æŽ‰è¿‡çš„ block idï¼Œé™„å¸¦ sprite å›¾æ ‡ã€‚
- **èŽ·å¾—ç‰©å“** â€” ä»Žä¸–ç•Œé‡Œæ¡åˆ°çš„æ‰€æœ‰ç‰©å“ï¼ŒæŒ‰æ•°é‡å€’åºã€‚
- **é£Ÿç‰© / åŒ»ç–— / æˆ˜æ–— / æ‚ç‰©** â€” çœŸæ­£æ¶ˆè€—æŽ‰çš„ä¸œè¥¿ï¼Œé¡¶æ ä¸€é”®åˆ‡æ¢"è€ä¹…ä»¶æ•°"ï¼ˆå¦‚ `1.02 ä»¶`ï¼‰ä¸Ž"ä½¿ç”¨æ¬¡æ•°"ï¼ˆå¦‚ `3 æ¬¡`ï¼‰ä¸¤ç§ç»´åº¦ã€‚
- **è®¾ç½®** â€” è¯¦ç»†æ—¥å¿—å¼€å…³ã€å­—å·ç¼©æ”¾ã€æ£€æŸ¥æ›´æ–°ã€é¢æ¿å¿«æ·é”®ã€ç•Œé¢è¯­è¨€ï¼ˆè‡ªåŠ¨ / ä¸­æ–‡ / è‹±æ–‡ï¼‰ã€‚
- **å…³äºŽ** â€” ç‰ˆæœ¬ã€é“¾æŽ¥ã€è‡´è°¢ã€ä¾èµ–ã€‚

[b]Two ways to open it[/b]

If CuSaveManager is installed, CuStats registers itself as a "Statistics" sidebar tab inside its panel; nothing extra appears on the main menu. If CuSaveManager is missing or its registration point is gone, CuStats injects its own button into the main menu and opens the panel as a standalone window. The default in-game hotkey is `U`; `ESC` closes the panel.

å¦‚æžœå®‰è£…äº† CuSaveManagerï¼ŒCuStats ä¼šæŠŠè‡ªå·±æ³¨å†Œæˆ"ç»Ÿè®¡"ä¾§æ åˆ†é¡µï¼Œä¸»èœå•ä¸Šä¸å†é¢å¤–åŠ æŒ‰é’®ï¼›å¦‚æžœ CuSaveManager ä¸åœ¨æˆ–è€…æ³¨å†ŒæŽ¥å£å–ä¸åˆ°ï¼ŒCuStats ä¼šåœ¨ä¸»èœå•æ³¨å…¥ä¸€ä¸ªç‹¬ç«‹æŒ‰é’®ï¼Œæ‰“å¼€è‡ªå·±çš„æµ®åŠ¨çª—å£ã€‚æ¸¸æˆå†…é»˜è®¤ `U` é”®å¼€å…³é¢æ¿ï¼Œ`ESC` å…³é—­ã€‚

Some data points come from observation, not authoritative game events. Food classification listens for `Body.Eat` inside a `Body.UseItem` window and reclassifies the active item into the food partition; mob kill credit is given when a `BuildingEntity` with `animal=true` dies within 1.5 seconds of a player melee strike (inside the strike's reach + 1m) or within 1.5 seconds of a gunshot (within 100m). These approximations are good enough for casual stats â€” open a GitHub issue if a count looks wrong.

éƒ¨åˆ†æ•°æ®æ¥è‡ªè¿è¡Œæ—¶è§‚å¯Ÿï¼Œä¸æ¥è‡ªæ¸¸æˆæƒå¨äº‹ä»¶ï¼šé£Ÿç‰©åˆ†ç±»é åœ¨ä¸€æ¬¡ `Body.UseItem` æœŸé—´ç›‘å¬ `Body.Eat` è°ƒç”¨ï¼Œå‘½ä¸­å³æŠŠå½“å‰ç‰©å“å½’å…¥é£Ÿç‰©åˆ†åŒºï¼›ç”Ÿç‰©åˆ¤å®šé  `BuildingEntity` çš„ `animal=true` æ­»äº¡é‚£ä¸€åˆ»â€”â€”è‹¥ 1.5 ç§’å†…æœ‰è¿‡çŽ©å®¶è¿‘æˆ˜ï¼ˆåœ¨æ”»å‡»è·ç¦» +1 ç±³å†…ï¼‰æˆ–å¼€ç«ï¼ˆ100 ç±³å†…ï¼‰ã€‚è¿™äº›è¿‘ä¼¼åœ¨æ—¥å¸¸ç»Ÿè®¡é‡Œè¶³å¤Ÿç”¨ï¼›æ•°å­—çœ‹èµ·æ¥ä¸å¯¹çš„è¯æ¬¢è¿Žåœ¨ GitHub å¼€ issueã€‚
[b]
Compatibility[/b]

Soft-depends on CuSaveManager for the sidebar embed (works without it). Doesn't touch save files.

Name: QoL Unknown Chinese Patch
Description:
QoL Unknown Chinese Patch
A Simplified Chinese localization patch for [b]QoL Unknown[/b], a quality-of-life mod for [b]Casualties: Unknown / Scav Prototype[/b].
This patch localizes QoL Unknown's menus, settings, module list, tooltips, dropdown entries, and several UI strings into Simplified Chinese.
This patch does [b]not[/b] include QoL Unknown itself.
Please install the original QoL Unknown mod first.
[heading]Supported versions[/heading]
[list]
[*]Game: Casualties Unknown Demo v7.0.1[/*]
[*]Original mod: QoL Unknown 1.0.4.5[/*]
[*]Mod loader: BepInEx 5.4.23.5[/*]
[*]Patch version: v0.10.14[/*]
[/list][heading]Installation[/heading]
[list=1]
[*]Install BepInEx 5.4.23.5.[/*]
[*]Install the original QoL Unknown mod.[/*]
[*]Download this patch.[/*]
[*]Extract the
[code]QoLChinesePatch[/code]folder into:[/*]
[/list][code]Casualties Unknown Demo/BepInEx/plugins/[/code]The final structure should look like this:
[code]Casualties Unknown Demo/
â””â”€â”€ BepInEx/
Â  Â  â””â”€â”€ plugins/
Â  Â  Â  Â  â”œâ”€â”€ QoL Unknown.dll
Â  Â  Â  Â  â””â”€â”€ QoLChinesePatch/
Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  â”œâ”€â”€ QoLChinesePatch.dll
Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  â”œâ”€â”€ QoLChinesePatch.Bootstrap.dll
Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  â”œâ”€â”€ translations.zh-CN.json
Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  â””â”€â”€ phrases.zh-CN.json[/code][heading]Known compatibility issue[/heading]
This version is currently not compatible with [b]KrokMP Chinese Name Fix 1.0.18.20[/b].
Reason: both mods patch global Unity text rendering methods such as
[code]UnityEngine.GUI[/code],
[code]GUILayout[/code],
[code]GUIStyle[/code],
[code]TMP_Text[/code], and
[code]UI.Text[/code]. If both are installed at the same time, some QoL Unknown IMGUI texts may fail to localize correctly.
Temporary workaround:
[list]
[*]Use this patch without KrokMP Chinese Name Fix; or[/*]
[*]Wait for a future compatibility update from either side.[/*]
[/list][heading]Notes[/heading]
This patch only contains localization files and patch code. It does not redistribute:
[list]
[*]QoL Unknown original DLL[/*]
[*]Casualties Unknown game files[/*]
[*]KrokMP files[/*]
[*]KrokMP Chinese Name Fix[/*]
[*]wiki translation JSON files[/*]
[*]fonts or Unity built-in resources[/*]
[/list][heading]Credits[/heading]
Original QoL Unknown mod by Jimmyking / Jimmyking9999999.
This is an unofficial Simplified Chinese localization patch.

Name: V for Whining with MP
Description:
[b][color=#ff7700][size=4][important!][/size][/color]
if you want to use this mod on the client side in multiplayer, the host must have the mod installed.
if only the host installs the mod, clients can still play without any issues, but the mod features won't function for them.[/b]

[color=#ff7700][size=4][b]Description[/b][/size][/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]This is a small add-on for "[b][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/197]V for Whining[/url]"[/b] that adds compatibility with "[b][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/67]KrokMP multiplayer[/url]"[/b].
Normally, the original mod only works locally, meaning other players cannot hear your whining. This add-on synchronizes the effect across the network, allowing other players to hear nearby players whining in multiplayer.
This mod is an add-on and does not replace or modify the original "[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/197]V for Whining[/url]" experience.[/color]

[color=#ff7700][size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size][/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]1. Install "[b]BepInEx[/b]" for "Casualties Unknown Demo" [/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]2. Install "[b]KrokMP[/b]"[/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]3.Â Install "[b]V for Whining"[/b][/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]4.Â Install this mod[/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]5.Â Put the mod things in here! : "Casualties Unknown Demo\BepInEx\plugins" [/color]

[color=#ff7700][size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size][/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]multiplayer support for V for Whining. [/color]

[b][size=4]Requirements[/size][/b]
[list]
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/197][b]V for Whining[/b][/url]Â ( Whining~ )[/*]
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/67][b]KrokMP[/b][/url]Â ( Online Multiplayer Co-op Mod )[/*]
[*][b][url=https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases/]BepInEx[/url][/b] For mods! [/*]
[/list][b][color=#ff7700][size=4]Known Issues[/size][/color][/b]
[size=3]I don't have many friends who play this game, so I haven't been able to test this mod with a large number of players. ;w;
So far, it has only been tested in a simple Host + 1 Friend setup.
If you run into any issues with larger groups or find any bugs, please leave a post and let me know! xd[/size]

[color=#ff7700][size=4][b]Shout outs[/b][/size][/color]

Also, thanks to the "KrokMP" developers for making multiplayer modding possible.[/color]

Name: MoreWatchInfo
Description:
[heading]MoreWatchInfo Plugin Description[/heading]
[b]MoreWatchInfo[/b]Â is a qualityâ€‘ofâ€‘life enhancement mod for the original watch item inÂ [i]Casualties Unknown[/i]. Of course,Â [b]BepInEx[/b]Â is required as a prerequisite.
By default, actively using the watch only reports its runtime and temperature. This plugin addsÂ [b]many vital signs that are not shown on the standard health panel[/b], allowing you to fully monitor your experimentâ€™s condition â€“ especially after using procoagulants or when you need to track hidden risks like blood viscosity.
New Information Added
When you actively use the watch, in addition to the runtime and temperature, the following data will be reported ([b]most entries appear only when their value is abnormal[/b]Â to avoid clutter):
[list]
[*][b]Blood Viscosity[/b]Â (always shown) â€“ Positive means thick blood (thrombosis risk), negative means thin blood (bleeding risk). Pay close attention after using clotting agents.[/*]
[*][b]Internal Bleeding[/b]Â â€“ Internal blood loss from damaged organs or vessels; reported when >0%.[/*]
[*][b]Hemothorax[/b]Â â€“ Blood accumulation in the chest cavity, affecting respiration and oxygen capacity; reported when >0.[/*]
[*][b]Fibrillation Progress[/b]Â â€“ Percentage of cardiac fibrillation; higher values mean greater risk of cardiac arrest; reported when >0%.[/*]
[*][b]Septic Shock[/b]Â â€“ Systemic infection reaching a critical stage; reported when >0%.[/*]
[*][b]Stroke Risk[/b]Â â€“ Chance of cerebral hemorrhage caused by high blood pressure or blood issues; reported when >0%.[/*]
[*][b]Pulmonary Embolism[/b]Â â€“ Blood clot blocking a pulmonary artery, which can lead to respiratory arrest; reported when present.[/*]
[*][b]Venom Level[/b]Â â€“ Blood toxin (e.g., from shade crawler bites); reported when >0.[/*]
[*][b]Limb Infection[/b]Â â€“ Lists all infected limbs with their infection percentage (only shown when infection >0 for that limb).[/*]
[/list]All extra information is seamlessly integrated into the original speechâ€‘bubble format (multiâ€‘line text). Infection details appear on a separate line and are omitted entirely if no infection is present.
Feedback & Suggestions
The current set of reported parameters already covers most survival monitoring needs. However, if you would like to see additional hidden stats, or if you have ideas for a better reporting format, please let me know.

In memory of the one who took too much procoagulant and couldn't keep the blood flowing while sleeping.

Name: Ultimate Sound Swap
Description:
[b]Ultimate Sound Swap[/b]
A lightweight framework for replacing nearly any sound in Casualties Unknown.
Simply place WAV files inside the Sounds folder and the mod will automatically replace matching in-game sounds. Sound names are matched directly against the game's internal audio clip names.

[b]Setup[/b]
[list=1]
[*]Install BepInEx 5.4.23.5 (or newer 5.x release) into your Casualties Unknown installation.[/*]
[*]This mod was developed and tested on Casualties Unknown v7.0.1. Ensure your version matches or expect weird behavior.[/*]
[*]Place the Ultimate Sound Swap DLL into:
[code]Casualties Unknown/
â””â”€â”€ BepInEx/
Â  Â  â””â”€â”€ plugins/
Â  Â  Â  Â  â””â”€â”€ UltimateSoundSwap.dll[/code][/*]
[*]Launch the game once. The mod will automatically create a Sounds folder inside the plugins directory if one does not already exist:
[code]Casualties Unknown/
â””â”€â”€ BepInEx/
Â  Â  â””â”€â”€ plugins/
Â  Â  Â  Â  â””â”€â”€ Sounds/[/code][/*]
[*]Enable HideManagerGameObject in BepInEx's config file. You'll find it in:
[code]Casualties Unknown/
â””â”€â”€ BepInEx/
Â  Â  â””â”€â”€ config/
Â  Â  Â  Â  â””â”€â”€ BepInEx.cfg/[/code]You should be able to open it in a text editor like notepad. Confirm that:
[code][Chainloader]
HideManagerGameObject = true[/code][/*]
[/list][b]Single Replacement Example[/b]
[code]Sounds/
â”œâ”€â”€ pain1.wav
â””â”€â”€ death1.wav[/code]Whenever the game plays "pain1" or "death1", your custom sound will be used instead.

[b]Randomized Replacements[/b]
Place multiple WAV files inside a folder named after the sound you want to replace:
[code]Sounds/
â””â”€â”€ death1/
Â  Â  â”œâ”€â”€ boom.wav
Â  Â  â”œâ”€â”€ owie.wav
Â  Â  â””â”€â”€ deez.wav[/code]Each time "death1" is played, a random file from the folder will be selected.

[b]Finding Sound Names[/b]
Hold Left Ctrl while sounds are playing to print their internal names and properties to the console. This makes it easy to identify which sounds you want to replace. Additionally, you can use the "playsound" command in the console and browse through the string ids there.

[b]Requirements[/b]
Replacement sounds can be MP3, WAV, OGG, etc. Let me know if an audio type you like doesn't work.

Author: Yraganchic

Name: CU.RemoteConsole
Description:
[b]CU.RemoteConsole[/b]

CU.RemoteConsole is a BepInEx 5 plugin for Casualties: Unknown that provides a local authenticated browser/API command console.

[b]Features[/b]

- Local browser console at http://127.0.0.1:8848/
- Bearer-token authentication
- Command output rendering
- Command history and receipt lookup
- Command catalog grouped by risk
- In-game config overlay opened with F8
- English / Simplified Chinese UI
- Conservative defaults: localhost only, auth enabled, dangerous commands denied

[b]Requirements[/b]

- Casualties: Unknown or Casualties: Unknown Demo
- BepInEx 5.4.x

BepInEx is not included and must be installed separately.

[b]Installation[/b]

1. Install BepInEx 5.4.x into the game folder.
2. Download CU.RemoteConsole-v1.0.0.zip.
3. Copy the BepInEx folder from this package into the game install directory.
4. Start the game once.
5. Open http://127.0.0.1:8848/
6. Copy the generated bearer token from:

BepInEx/config/cu.remoteconsole.cfg

[b]Safety[/b]

CU.RemoteConsole can execute game console commands. Keep it bound to 127.0.0.1, keep authentication enabled, do not share the generated bearer token, and do not expose the service directly to the public internet.

If remote access is needed, use a private network or tunnel such as Tailscale, WireGuard, SSH tunnel, or ZeroTier.

[b]Proton / Steam Deck[/b]

Normal Windows installs do not need launch options.

If BepInEx does not load when running the Windows build through Proton, try this Steam launch option:

WINEDLLOVERRIDES=winhttp=n,b %command%

[b]Source Code[/b]

https://github.com/foxderin/CU.RemoteConsole

[b][size=5]English[/size][/b]
A BepInEx/Harmony UI fix mod that keeps the inventory radial menu visible when the player reaches the bottom edge of a generated map layer.
[b]What It Fixes[/b]
In the vanilla game, the inventoryÂ RadialMenu follows the player's screen position. Near the bottom of the map, the whole inventory menu is placed too low, and the wearable equipment row can be clipped by the bottom of the screen.
This mod applies a smooth, configurable upward offset to the wholeÂ RadialMenuÂ only when the player is close to the bottom of the current map layer. It does not move individual equipment slots, change camera behavior, or rewrite inventory generation.
[b]Features[/b]
[list]
[*]Smoothly moves the full inventory radial menu upward near the bottom edge of the map.[/*]
[*]Restores the original menu position outside the bottom-edge area.[/*]
[*]Adds in-game settings under Settings -> GamePlay .[/*]
[*]Optional save/continue button separation to prevent overlap with larger inventory offsets.[/*]
[*]Uses Harmony patches only; no game files are overwritten.[/*]
[/list][b]Settings[/b]
[list]
[*]Inventory Edge Fix: default On, range On/Off. Enables the radial menu visibility fix.[/*]
[*]Inventory Edge Fix Threshold: default 2%, range 1%-5%. Bottom portion of the map where the upward offset begins.[/*]
[*]Inventory Edge Fix Offset: default 150 px, range 50-500 px. Maximum upward UI offset.[/*]
[*]Move Save/Continue Buttons: default Off, range On/Off. Moves the bottom-depth save and continue buttons left/right so they do not overlap the raised inventory UI.[/*]
[/list][b]Installation[/b]
[list=1]
[*]Install BepInEx for the game.[/*]
[*]Download InventoryVisibilityFix.dll from the release page.[/*]
[*]Place the DLL inÂ  BepInEx/plugins/.[/*]
[*]Launch the game and adjust the settings under Settings -> GamePlay.[/*]
[/list][b][size=3]Compatibility Notes
This mod is also compatible with [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/194]Wearable Array[/url]. When using multiple equipment rows, enableÂ  Move Save/Continue ButtonsÂ and adjustÂ  Inventory Edge Fix OffsetÂ to a suitable range.[/size][/b]
Author: MistalCry

Name: No Decay
Description:
[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]Prevents items from losing condition simply because time passes.[/color]
By default, this mod:
Â - Stops normal item time decay / spoilage
Â - Does not change normal item usage, damage, or other non-time-based condition loss
Â - Does not alter the game's original behavior when an item's condition actually reaches zero
Â -Â Battery-type items are not disabled by default. If an item uses the game's battery-style time drain, it will continue to work normally unless you changeÂ the config.Â Config file: BepInEx/config/com.el739.nodecay.cfg

[size=4][b]Installation[/b][/size]
1. Install BepInEx for the gameÂ  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â 
2. Place NoDecay.dll into BepInEx/plugins/

Name: Gamma Light Backport (port 6.1 to 7.x)
Description:
[color=#ff7700][size=4][b]Description[/b][/size][/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]Brings back how old lighting gamma works back in 6.1 as close as I can.[/color]
If you have [u]issues with the way how brightness is handled in 7.0.1[/u],
[u]The "ambient light" in run settings ruin immersion[/u] and[u] prefer the old gamma lighting in 6.1[/u], this is for you.

[b][color=#ff7700][size=4]Installation:[/size][/color][/b]
Download Bepinex (if you haven't already):
[url=https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases/download/v5.4.23.5/BepInEx_win_x64_5.4.23.5.zip]https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases/download/v5.4.23.5/BepInEx_win_x64_5.4.23.5.zip[/url]

install mod into plugin.


[color=#ff7700][size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size][/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]Reimplements Gamma Lighting back in v6.1 as an accessibility option.
adds the following:

2 option in the video settings, a gamma brightness slider, and a toggle.
1 option in controls to bind key and easily toggle on and off the mod. (default is f5 to toggle mod)[/color]


[color=#ff7700][size=4][b]Shout outs[/b][/size][/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]Thanks to moffie for telling me to go screw myself and forcing me to make a mod a possibility for everyone. I like your game, but like, I know it's a demo and it's unfinished, but we're your community words/feedback.[/color]

[s][color=#0000ff]NOTE[/color]: I will eventually turn this into Custom Game Settings and sync multiplayer who also have this mod Option to Align with "unfair advantage" from changing the brightness midway of the game.[/s]Â not planned anytime soon.

Name: Magazine Merge
Description:
[b][color=#ff7700][size=4][important!][/size][/color]
[size=3]if you want to use this mod on the client side in multiplayer, the host must have the mod installed.
if only the host installs the mod, clients can still play without any issues, but the mod features won't function for them.[/size] [/b]

[color=#ff7700][size=4][b]Description[/b][/size][/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]a simple quality-of-life mod that lets you combine compatible magazines into a single magazine, helping reduce inventory clutter and save time while managing ammunition.[/color]

[color=#ff7700][size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size][/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]1. Install "BepInEx" for "Casualties Unknown Demo"
2. Download "Magazine Merge.zip" Mod
3. Put the file here: "[/color][color=#d4d4d8]Casualties Unknown Demo\BepInEx\plugins"[/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]4. Play![/color]

[color=#ff7700][size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size][/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]â€¢ Combine compatible magazines with a simple drag-and-drop action.
â€¢ Preserves all rounds while transferring ammunition. [/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]â€¢ Supports both single-player and KrokMP multiplayer. [/color]

[color=#ff7700][size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size][/color]
[color=#D4D4D8][b]BepInEx For mods![/b][/color]

Name: Mushoo's Scuffed English Translation
Description:
[size=4][b]WELCOME TO THE MOST IMPORTANT MOD OF THE YEAR 2XXX![/b][/size]
[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]Finally, the [i]technically[/i]Â modified language file, "Scuffed English", is available for download to any who thinks I'm silly and funny enough to have my humor completely take over your game.[/color][/size]

[size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size]
[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]Adds some dialogue to your expie, most of which are references to other things, as well as changes a good number of descriptions in the game to things I find hopefully funny for people to see.[/color]
[/size]
Additionally, this "mod" requires no actual modding experience- you don't even need bepinex to use it! I'm just that good of a coder. It is a single file that you don't even need to modify your game in any way to use!

[b]Installation instructions[/b]
First: Locate your Casualties Unknown folder.
Now, stay with me here, because it's about to get serious.

Second: You're going to find "CasualtiesUnknown_Data" in your game files. This is scary, but don't worry, I'll be here with you the whole way.

Third: Find the "Lang" folder. The only thing you need to do now is, carefully, [i][b]very carefully[/b][/i], place the file in your "Lang" folder. It won't work if you put it in a different folder, or in a folder inside the Lang folder, it [i]has[/i]Â to be in the "Lang" folder and nowhere else.

Fourth: Open the game, and go to your settings. Navigate to the "Language" tab, and it should appear as an option for you to select!

Congratulations! You have successfully modified your game with my patented new virus-free language file (now with 12% less virus than before!)

[size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]A computer of some variety that is capable of playing Casualties Unknown.[/color][/size]

[size=4][b]Shout outs[/b][/size]
[color=#d4d4d8]Thanks to Orsonik for the fun game[/color]
Author: Mistamushoo

Name: Straightforward Health Panel
Description:
[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
Reworks the health panel to be more straightforward
Stats are now categorized by organ, and don't always show their number values, instead changing the organ they belong to
Body-wide stats also have their own fancy little bars

[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
This mod requires BepInEx
Once it's installed, place SFHealthPanel.dll and the SFHealthPanelTextures folder in the plugins folder
This mod is probably not compatible with any other mod that messes with the health panel

This mod was made in five days starting from quite literally zero modding knowledge as a result of a random burst of "i can do whatever the fuck i want"
I am aware that this removes some of the charm that the obtuse vanilla health panel has, as you have to figure out what to pay attention to while figuring out what's causing you to rapidly perish, but i personally don't like it and i doubt i'm the only one

If you wanna tell me anything, dm me on discord (@rosa_astraea, i'm in the main server)
Author: RosaAstraea

Author: winddramon

Name: Lore Reorganization
Description:
Main goal with this is to take the game's vent nature in a different direction. Instead of being "forced" to experience negative emotions, you voluntarily explore them for the benefit they provide.

Various aspects changed, most notably intro screen and pause screen splashes.

Pause splashes have the tonal range they do intentionally. Edit if disliked.

[u][b]The text is all very easy to edit, make changes to this or the original. I encourage you to reuse or copy ideas from this, without credit, assuming you find them good. Make them your own.[/b][/u]

This mod was last tested for Demo 7.0.1, and should be forward compatible. At minimum, you can copy and paste desired text directly.

[b]To install:[/b]

Add to [steam install]/CasualtiesUnknown_Data/Lang

Will show up in settings (blue vat on left) --> Language

Click it and reset game, should be changed.

[b]To edit:[/b]

The .json files can be opened in any text editor. Notepad++ is what I used for the better search function.

Intro text is at the 'startlorenote' syntax, a bit above the middle, and pause screen text at 'pauseQuotes', directly at the bottom.

Recommended to keep a backup of the original EN.json, reference the syntax from it.
Author: MetalFilingCabinet

Name: Species selection (BETA)
Description:
Patreon:Â [url=https://patreon.com/TheWanderer652?utm_medium=unknown&utm_source=join_link&utm_campaign=creatorshare_creator&utm_content=copyLink]Cool Stuff[/url]Â (not recommended as the mod update is being released soon.)

[heading]Main Features[/heading]
[list]
[*][b]Selectable Species[/b]: Adds a newÂ [b]Species[/b]Â dropdown settings option to your pre-run preset screen to choose betweenÂ [b]Experiment[/b]Â andÂ [b]Milky[/b].[/*]
[*][b]Visual Overhaul[/b]: Dynamically replaces player sprites (limbs, tail, heads, eyes, disfigured states) with Milky species textures.[/*]
[*][b]Tuned Physics[/b]:
[list]
[*]Player scale set toÂ 
[code]0.90f[/code]Â of normal.[/*]
[*]Fall damage force reduced toÂ 
[code]0.75x[/code]Â (netÂ 
[code]0.9375x[/code]Â fall damage).[/*]
[*]Colliders micro-tuned horizontally toÂ 
[code]0.85x[/code]Â to resolve infinite walljumping.[/*]
[*]Increased base speed and jump height to feel light and nimble.[/*]
[/list][/*]
[*][b]Balanced Stats & Leveling[/b]:
[list]
[*]Starts withÂ [b]STR = 5[/b],Â [b]RES = 5[/b], andÂ [b]INT = 17[/b].[/*]
[*]Strength is capped atÂ [b]9[/b]Â and levels up very slowly (
[code]0.1x[/code]Â XP).[/*]
[*]Resilience levels up slightly slower (
[code]0.75x[/code]Â XP) while Intelligence levels up fast (
[code]1.5x[/code]Â XP).[/*]
[*]Milky HP is effectivelyÂ [b]80[/b]Â (viaÂ 
[code]1.25x[/code]Â hazard damage scaling).[/*]
[/list][/*]
[*][b]Unique Dialogue & Voice Hook[/b]:
[list]
[*]custom dialogues forÂ [b]35+ state reactions[/b]Â (from stepping on glass, to heavy bleeding, to eating good food, to meeting other traders in a bad mood).[/*]
[*]Seamless native compatibility with theÂ [b]Animalese[/b]Â mod to automatically increase your player's voice pitch to match Milky traders (
[code]1.2f[/code]Â pitch).[/*]
[/list][/*]
[/list][line]
[heading]Installation Instructions[/heading]
[list=1]
[*]Download the mod files.[/*]
[*]Extract and place theÂ 
[code]Beta_RaceChanger.dll[/code],Â 
[code]README.txt[/code], and theÂ 
[code]Body[/code]Â folder directly into your game'sÂ 
[code]BepInEx/plugins/[/code]Â directory.[/*]
[*][b]Important[/b]: TheÂ 
[code]Body[/code]Â folderÂ [i]must[/i]Â be placed directly next to theÂ 
[code]Beta_RaceChanger.dll[/code]Â file.[/*]
[/list][line]
[heading]Requirements[/heading]
[list]
[*][b]BepInEx[/b]: Required to load the mod.[/*]
[*][b]Singleplayer Exclusivity[/b]: This mod is exclusively for singleplayer. Do not use it online/multiplayer as it will not work.[/*]
[*][b]Sprite Replacers[/b]: Currently incompatible with other sprite replacer mods. Please disable them to avoid visual glitching.Â [i](I will eventually fix this incompatibility in the future, but for now, I'm just a tired chud.)[/i][/*]
[*][b]Animalese Mod (Optional)[/b]: Optional but highly recommended for the custom player voice pitch![/*]
[/list][line]
[heading]Shout Outs[/heading]
[list]
[*][b]BeebiGirl[/b]: thanks for the sprite.[/*]
[/list]

[size=3][b]å·²çŸ¥é—®é¢˜: ç»´ç”Ÿä»“èƒŒæ™¯æ¶ˆå¤±ï¼ˆä¸ºäº†è¿™ä¸ªç ´ä¸œè¥¿æ•´åŠå¤©äº†ï¼‰ï¼ŒçŽ©å®¶ä¼ é€æ—©äºŽä¸–ç•ŒåŠ è½½ï¼ˆå»ºè®®æ˜¯å¤šæŒ‰ä¸¤ä¸‹loadï¼‰[/b][/size]

[b]Requirements:
BepInEx (prerequisite for all mods)
ScavLib (GUI related, version 0.6.1 or higher required)
Only tested in single-player mode.
Theoretically, multiplayer is unusable (too lazy to adapt it).
Press F5 to open the menu.

Author: Tatabaikani

Name: HARD MODE
Description:
[quote][size=4][font=Arial]Damn, it's almost like we're in some kind of... [/font][i][color=#ff0000][font=Arial Black]HARD MODE[/font][/color][/i][/size][/quote]A mod that is heavily inspired by UNDERTALE's Hard Mode difficulty
[left]This mod is not guaranteed to give a fair and enjoyable experience.[/left]
[left]Also multiplayer-compatible (I think, not tested)[/left]
[left][size=5][b]IMPORTANT[/b][/size][/left]
[left]All art assets are placeholders and there is plenty of content that is unchanged/unfinished[/left]
[left]Hard Mode is a preset that replaces Paradise (I think It's obvious why)[/left]
[left][/left]
[left]All Changes can be turned off in custom run settings[/left]
[left][size=3][font=Arial]Changes:[/font][/size]
[spoiler][size=3][font=Arial]
Jumppad velocity is buffed
Beartrap does more bleeding and damage
Spiders are more hostile and move way faster
Cave ticks have evolved into more dangerous versions.
Sound Cannon activates charges almost instantly
Landmines are instant and larger
Traders start with low reputation towards you
Stepping on Glass Shards does more bleeding
Spike traps do more bleeding
Barbed wire does more bleeding
Cactus sends you flying and does more damage
Barbed fences deal more damage, bleeding and damages clothing more
Oil pipes now leak oil at a consistent rate

MODIFIER CHANGES:
Cold is now freezing
Freezing is now even worse
Warm is now Hot
Hot is now even worse

Infested is really infested
There is now creatures in the fog.
Ionized has more barrels and less anti-rad

Other changes: Shower go bye bye

Optional: Starting out Hungry, Thirsty, Depressed, Underweight and with low stats[/font][/size][/spoiler]
[/left]
[left][size=4]also try out Desolate + Unchipped + Hardmode! :)[/size]
[size=1](pls record them if you do, i'd love to see your runs! You can submit your videos to the mod page in the media tab!)[/size][/left]
[left]
HOW TO INSTALL[/left]
[left]Drop the .ddl and folder into the plugins folder located at[/left]
[left]game root/BepinEx/plugins[/left]
[left][/left]
[left][/left]
[left][size=6][font=sans-serif]This mod is apart of the [color=#0000ff][b][i]Genesis Collection[/i][/b][/color][/font][/size][/left]
Author: TheBeginningGenesis

[b]Description[/b]
[b]A BepInEx/Harmony Mod for Casualties Unknown, which displays the explosion countdown for ignited Dynamites.[/b]

[b]Installation instructions[/b]
[list=1]
[*]DownloadÂ [b]BepInEx5Â [/b]fromÂ https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases and install it.[/*]
[*]Put the mod inÂ Casualties Unknown Demo/BepInEx/plugins,Â "DynamiteTimerCN.dll" is in Chinese, while "DynamiteTimerEN.dll" is in English. Please choose the one you are more familiar with. Only one can be installed!!![/*]
[*]This mod has only been tested on the v7.0.1 version released by Steam.[/*]
[/list]

[b]Main features[/b]
[list]
[*]The remaining explosion time is displayed above the explosive.[/*]
[*]Multiple explosives can be counted down simultaneously.[/*]
[*]The location of the explosive is shown, for example:
Right hand: 4.1s; Left hand: 3.2s; Mouth: 2.5s; Upper back: 1.8s; Middle back: 1.2s; Lower back: 0.7s[/*]
[*]The display color changes according to the remaining time:
> 3s: Green; <= 3s: Yellow[/*]
[/list][heading]The hands are displayed as the physical left and right hands, rather than "primary hand/secondary hand". This is because when the game switches the primary hand, the concept of the primary hand changes, but in the player's perspective, the items remain in the corresponding left or right hand positions, making it less likely for the display of the left and right hands to cause confusion.[/heading]

[b][size=4]I used AI to assist me in writing this mod. This mod is mainly for my own use.[/size][/b]
Author: MistalCry

Name: KrokMP Chinese Supplement - Simplified Chinese Translation
Description:
KrokMP Chinese Supplement - Simplified Chinese Translation
A Simplified Chinese supplement translation patch for [b]KrokMP / Krokosha_MP_CU / CO-OP MOD[/b] for [b]Casualties Unknown Demo[/b].
This mod provides additional Chinese translations for the multiplayer mod UI, server browser, rule menu, chat/status messages, tooltips, dynamic messages, and Battle Royale mode texts.
This is a translation supplement only. It does [b]not[/b] include the original KrokMP / CO-OP MOD files.
[line]
[heading]Description[/heading]
KrokMP Chinese Supplement is a BepInEx-based Simplified Chinese translation supplement for KrokMP / CO-OP MOD.
It is designed to fill in text that is not covered by the base Chinese language file, especially hardcoded multiplayer UI text and dynamic status messages.
It translates or supplements:
[list]
[*]Multiplayer main menu[/*]
[*]Server browser[/*]
[*]Lobby creation and connection menu[/*]
[*]Rule menu[/*]
[*]Rule search[/*]
[*]Tooltip text[/*]
[*]Chat and server announcements[/*]
[*]Last status messages[/*]
[*]Multiplayer in-game status panel[/*]
[*]Battle Royale mode texts[/*]
[*]TPS / FPS display text[/*]
[*]New KrokMP 3.1.0 language keys[/*]
[*]Location names and mood/status texts[/*]
[/list]This patch does not replace large wiki-style Chinese translation packs such as Wiki Chinese. It is only a supplement for KrokMP / CO-OP MOD multiplayer texts.
[line]
[heading]Supported Versions[/heading]
Tested with:
[list]
[*]Casualties Unknown Demo v7.0.1[/*]
[*]KrokMP / Krokosha_MP_CU / CO-OP MOD v3.1.0[/*]
[*]BepInEx 5[/*]
[/list]Older versions may work, but are not the main target of this release.
[line]
[heading]Requirements[/heading]
Required:
[list]
[*]Casualties Unknown Demo[/*]
[*]BepInEx 5[/*]
[*]KrokMP / Krokosha_MP_CU / CO-OP MOD v3.1.0[/*]
[/list]Recommended:
[list]
[*]A Simplified Chinese base translation / language file for the game[/*]
[/list]This translation supplement does not include the original KrokMP files, game files, or any decompiled KrokMP source code.
[line]
[heading]Installation[/heading]
[list=1]
[*]Install BepInEx 5.[/*]
[*]Install the original KrokMP / Krokosha_MP_CU / CO-OP MOD.[/*]
[*]Download this translation supplement.[/*]
[*]Extract the archive.[/*]
[*]Put the entire
[code]KrokMPChineseSupplement[/code]folder into:[/*]
[/list][code]BepInEx/plugins/[/code]The final structure should look like this:
[code]BepInEx/plugins/KrokMPChineseSupplement/
â”œâ”€ KrokMPChineseSupplement.dll
â”œâ”€ translations.zh-CN.json
â”œâ”€ phrases.zh-CN.json
â””â”€ README.md[/code][list=1]
[*]Start the game.[/*]
[/list][line]
[heading]Uninstallation[/heading]
Delete this folder:
[code]BepInEx/plugins/KrokMPChineseSupplement/[/code]If you want to fully clean the configuration file, also delete:
[code]BepInEx/config/casualtiesunknown.krokmpchinesesupplement.cfg[/code][line]
[heading]Main Features[/heading]
Multiplayer UI Translation
Translates the KrokMP multiplayer menu, server browser, direct connect menu, lobby settings, buttons, labels, and status messages.
Rule Menu Translation
Adds Chinese translations for multiplayer rules, rule names, rule tooltips, and rule search.
Dynamic Text Translation
Adds fallback translation for dynamic messages such as lobby status, Steam lobby type, player count, server messages, disconnect reasons, and status logs.
Battle Royale Translation
Adds translation support for Battle Royale mode texts, including gamemode activation, waiting messages, victory/death messages, play area warnings, and direction hints.
TPS / FPS Translation
Adds display translations such as:
[code]SERVER TPS â†’ æœåŠ¡å™¨åˆ»çŽ‡
LOCAL TPS â†’ æœ¬åœ°åˆ»çŽ‡
TPS â†’ åˆ»çŽ‡
FPS â†’ å¸§çŽ‡[/code]Performance-Safe Patch Strategy
The supplement uses targeted patches and JSON translation dictionaries. It does not modify KrokMP networking, player validation, Steamworks initialization, network packets, or world synchronization.
[line]
[heading]Notes[/heading]
This mod does not include:
[list]
[*]Original KrokMP DLL files[/*]
[*]Original KrokMP assets[/*]
[*]Game files[/*]
[*]Decompiled KrokMP source code[/*]
[*]Decompiled game source code[/*]
[/list]The original KrokMP / CO-OP MOD belongs to its author.
This project only contains an independent BepInEx translation supplement plugin and Simplified Chinese translation dictionaries.
[line]
[heading]Known Limitations[/heading]
[list]
[*]Rare dynamic messages may still be untranslated.[/*]
[*]Battle Royale mode itself may still have original KrokMP-side logic issues. This patch only translates its visible text.[/*]
[*]Discord IPC timeout messages are from KrokMP / Discord RPC and are not caused by this translation patch.[/*]
[*]Steam Chinese display names may still appear as question marks because this is handled by KrokMP itself. The experimental Steam display-name replacement was removed to avoid breaking nametag layout.[/*]
[/list]If you find untranslated text, please report it with:
[list]
[*]Screenshot[/*]
[*][code]BepInEx/LogOutput.log[/code][/*]
[*]Game version[/*]
[*]KrokMP version[/*]
[*]Steps to reproduce[/*]
[/list][line]

Name: Offhand QoL
Description:
[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]By pressing "E": It acts like if you'd opened your inventory and dragged the item from off hand to the middle to use it.[/color]
[color=#D4D4D8](Includes medicine, food, flashlights).[/color]

[color=#D4D4D8]Works on version Beta 7.0.1 - we'll see how it goes with updates :x.[/color]

[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]1. Install BepInExÂ [/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]2. Just unzip the [b]BepInEx folder[/b] right into your game directory :>
You can find it by going into Steam Library -> Casualities Unknown: Demo -> Right click, Properties -> Installed Files -> "Browse"[/color]

[size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]Pressing "E" acts like if you'd opened your inventory and dragged the item from off hand to the middle to use it.[/color]
[color=#D4D4D8][b][i]Fair warning - includes medicine, food, flashlights, so be mindful what you're carrying in the off-hand ;).[/i][/b][/color]

[color=#D4D4D8][i](You can find out how to change the key binding in "How to use mod.txt").[/i][/color]

[size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]You need to install BepInEx first[/color][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]([url=https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases]https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases[/url])[/color]

Name: ContainerTweaks
Description:
[center][url=https://github.com/Shinkudaisuki/ContainerTweaksPlugin][img]https://img.shields.io/badge/github-repo-blue?logo=github&link=https%3A%2F%2Fgithub.com%2FShinkudaisuki%2FContainerTweaksPlugin[/img][/url]Â  [img]https://img.shields.io/badge/Multiplayer-Verified-green?logo=%25F0%259F%258C%2590[/img][/center]
[b]Description[/b]

ContainerTweaks is a BepInEx plugin for [b]Casualties: Unknown Demo[/b] that improves the container UI.
It expands the container layout and adds scrolling support, making containers with many items easier to browse and use. It also adds a configurable quick-transfer hotkey for moving matching items between containers, quick liquid transfer between compatible water containers, and auto-highlights compatible items when dragging.

[b]Installation instructions[/b]

1. Install BepInEx for [b]Casualties: Unknown Demo[/b].Â 
2. Download or build [b]ContainerTweaks.dll[/b].Â 
3. Copy [b]ContainerTweaks.dll[/b] into the gameâ€™s BepInEx plugins folder:
[b]BepInEx/plugins/ContainerTweaks.dll[/b]
4. Launch the game.

[b]Main features[/b]

â€¢ Expands the container grid from 3 columns to 6 columns by default.
â€¢ Adds mouse wheel scrolling for containers with more items than the visible area.
â€¢ Keeps the expanded container area from closing unexpectedly while interacting with it.
â€¢ Adds a configurable quick-transfer hotkey (default: LeftControl) for moving matching items between containers.
â€¢ Supports quick liquid transfer between compatible water containers.
â€¢ Supports quick magazine-to-magazine transfers for the same ammo type.
â€¢ Supports quick loading rounds (in a magazine, including a 12gauge box) into a direct-feed gun (typically a shotgun).
â€¢ Supports quick loading rounds into a magazine, including a 12gauge box.
â€¢ Provides BepInEx configuration entries for grid layout, scrolling, drag threshold values, and enabling / disabling specific features.
â€¢ Auto-highlights compatible items in the container when dragging an item (water containers, ammo, and guns).
â€¢Â Supports multiplayer mod [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/67]Casualties Together[/url].

[b]Known issues[/b]

â€¢Â The background and volumn bar of the container view won't scale with the number of columns. Because I can't figure out how they are drawn.
â€¢Â The simi-transparent strip indicates closing inventory view won't scale for the same reasion above. But since the drop feature of the base game still works, I'd like to call this a feature ;) .

[b]Credits[/b]

The container scrolling implementation references code from [url=https://github.com/jimmyking9999999/QoL-Unknown]QoL-Unknown[/url].
Because QoL-Unknown does not support the latest current version (v7.0.1) of the game by the time this plugin was created, the container scrolling feature was ported into this plugin.
Thanks to the original author of QoL-Unknown. If the original author prefers, the related referenced or ported code can be removed from this project.
Author: shinkudaisuki

Name: Quick Pickup
Description:
[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]Use any hotkey (defaultÂ [b]Middle Mouse Button[/b]) to quick pickup the item on the ground or in your inventory!Â [/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]It also provides a container filter list to prioritize when picking up items (defaultÂ [b]Material pouch [/b]and[b] Belt[/b])Â . Else it will be put in [b]Best Containers (High Encumbrance reduction)[/b], then your first empty slot.[/color]

[color=#D4D4D8]This mod is already tested under co-op mod and works well. And you will not pointing when you want to pickup item but fail because of things like distance.[/color]

[color=#D4D4D8]Btw, I made this mod just because QoL Mod don't works at this moment and it don't have a filter list. This mod is also inspired by QoL Mod. And if you want, you can still use this mod and disable the function in QoL mod, because this mod have a bit more functions?[/color]

If you have any suggestions, please tell me.

Updated for the latest version. Requires RshLib 3.0.

Recipes have been simplified and are now easier to craft. The electric titanium machete texture has also been updated. Intelligence level requirements now match the vanilla titanium equipment requirements.

Multiplayer compatibility is experimental soft compatibility. I personally do not play multiplayer mode, so please report any issues promptly.
All multiplayer players need to install the same version of Electric Titanium Tools. Either the bundled version or the standard version can be used, but the RshLib version must be the same.

Have fun!

# Electric Titanium Tools

Electric Titanium Tools adds two late-game tool upgrades for Scav Prototype / Casualties Unknown:

- Electric Titanium Machete
- Electric Titanium Pickaxe

These two items are electric upgraded versions of the vanilla titanium machete and titanium pickaxe. The original items are not modified, and the vanilla titanium multi-tool is not changed.

## Features

- Keeps the original titanium toolsâ€™ damage values and tool properties.
- Supports removable batteries, including large batteries.
- Slightly increased attack range, making it easier to kite Elders.
- When powered:
Â  - Faster attack speed.
Â  - Lower stamina consumption.
Â  - Reduced risk of heatstroke during combat.
- When out of power or without a battery:
Â  - Still works normally with the base titanium tool stats.
Â  - Will not break just because it has no power.

These tools can be crafted, found while exploring, and purchased from traders. Trader prices are intentionally high.

## Crafting

Both recipes are located in the Tools category.

The recipes are designed for late-game progression. Since these tools upgrade titanium equipment into long-term electric gear, their crafting cost is higher than regular titanium tools.

## Requirements

- BepInEx 5
- RshLib 3.0

## Installation

Install BepInEx and RshLib first, then extract the archive into the game root folder.

The final path should look like this:

```text
BepInEx/plugins/ElectricTitaniumTools/ElectricTitaniumTools.dll
BepInEx/plugins/ElectricTitaniumTools/Assets/electrictitaniummachete.png
BepInEx/plugins/ElectricTitaniumTools/Assets/electrictitaniumpickaxe.png

[*]Inside the mod folder, create anÂ 
Items/Â directory to hold subfolders for each custom item.[/*]
[/list][b][size=4]Recommended structure example:[/size][/b]
[code]BepInEx/plugins/

[b]Step 3: Write Mod Identification File[/b]
Fill ICEnecoCustomItemFlag.json with the following content:
{
Â  "modName": "Your Item Mod Name",Â  // Required

}

[/list][b]Step 5: Create Normal Item Configuration ([/b]item.json[b])[/b]

item.jsonÂ file inside the item's folder.[/*]

[*][b]Use actions:[/b]Â addÂ attackÂ / eatÂ / splintÂ etc. in useAction.ï¼ˆThe "splint" actually refers to the action of drying one's body. The sound it makes is "splint". I will fix this issue in the next version.ï¼‰ (Search for "CustomItemUseData" in the .docx file.)
The format should be:"usable": true,"useAction": {"actionName (for now only have [eat][splint][attack][drawblood])": CustomItemUseData}[/*]

"capacity"Â (maximum milliliters)"autoFill"Â (whether to auto-fill)"drinkAmount"Â (amount consumed per drink)"defaultLiquid"Â (initial liquid fill, e.g.,Â {"blood": 250})Recommended use actions:Â "drink"Â orÂ "drawblood". Name: MULTIPLAYER_Sprite_Player_Customizer_ MULTIPLAYER
Description:
[size=6][b][color=#8e7cc3][font=Comic Sans MS]MULTIPLAYER[/font][/color] [color=#999999]S[/color][color=#b6b6b6]U[/color][color=#cccccc]P[/color][/b][/size][color=#d8d8d8][size=6][b]P[/b][/size][/color][color=#ffffff][size=6][b]O[/b][/size][/color][b][size=6][color=#f4f4f4]R[/color][color=#ffffff]T[/color]
[/size][/b][img width=413,height=320]https://i.postimg.cc/sgRH3qv6/Video-Project-9.gif[/img]
[size=6][b][color=#00ff00]New Esc-menu button [/color][/b][/size]
[img width=213,height=120]https://i.postimg.cc/TPVbfqn7/image.png[/img]

[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]Sprite Player CustomizerÂ is an expanded standalone fork ofÂ Player Sprite ReplacerÂ by Karlo PoljanÄiÄ‡ (dogederp).
It keeps the original real-time sprite switching concept and adds a full in-game pixel editor with live character preview.
You can now create, edit, and manage custom sprite sets directly in-game without restarting or manually replacing files every time.[/color]

[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
[list=1]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Make sure BepInEx is installed.[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Extract mod files into:[/color]

[code]...\steamapps\common\Casualties Unknown Demo\BepInEx\plugins\[/code][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Launch the game.[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Open the mod menu with F4 orÂ during the game press ESC and then the button in the top-left corner "sprite"[/color].[/*]
[/list][heading]HOW IT WORKS[/heading]
Inside theÂ CustomSpritesÂ folder, each character set is stored in its own directory (for exampleÂ st1,Â st2,Â st3, etc.).
Each set contains sprite files in theÂ Head andÂ BodyÂ subfolders.
When you open the mod menu in-game (F4), the mod scans these folders and shows all available sprite parts.
Clicking a PNG opens it in the built-in pixel editor.
In the editor:
[list]
[*]You can paint directly on sprite textures (pixel-by-pixel).[/*]
[*]Changes are previewed on the player model in real time.[/*]
[*]You can use tools like Brush, Eraser, Picker, Fill, Line, and Rect tools.[/*]
[*]Color can be adjusted with color wheel, Hex input, opacity, dark/light tone, and brush size.[/*]
[*]Undo/Redo and Save are available before committing file changes.[/*]
[*]You can also open the current PNG in an external editor (for example Paint).[/*]
[/list]Character model workflow:
[list]
[*]The model preview panel shows major body parts.[/*]
[*]Click a part to load its related variants list.[/*]
[*]Select a variant from the list to open and edit that exact PNG.[/*]
[/list]The mod keeps everything in-game, so you can create and test sprite edits quickly without restarting.

[heading]Media
[img]https://i.postimg.cc/bYL205G6/sasisku-(1).gif[/img]
[img]https://i.postimg.cc/6QQrnNcd/image-(7).png[/img]

Controls[/heading]
Global
[list]
[*]F4 â€” open/close mod UI.[/*]
[*]ESC [size=2]â€” closes current UI layer (fullscreen color picker -> dialogs -> editor).[/size][/*]
[*]NumPad/custom hotkeys â€” switch sprite set.[/*]
[/list]Main Window
[list]
[*]LMB + drag title bar â€” move window (attached preview/variants panels move together).[/*]
[*]Mouse wheel [size=2]over lists â€” scroll file/variant lists.[/size][/*]
[*]In model preview: click body part to load its variant group.[/*]
[/list]PNG Editor
[list]
[*]MMB + dragÂ â€” pan canvas.[/*]
[*]Mouse wheelÂ over canvas â€” zoom at cursor.[/*]
[*]LMB + dragÂ split bar â€” resize tools/canvas panels.[/*]
[*]LMB + dragÂ title bar â€” move editor window.[/*]
[/list]Color & Painting
[list]
[*]LMBÂ /Â LMB + drag on color wheel â€” continuous color picking.[/*]
[*]Fullscreen color wheel supports the same LMBÂ drag picking.[/*]
[*]Sliders + numeric fields set exact:
[list]
[*]opacity ( 0â€“100),[/*]
[*]tone ( 0â€“200, dark->light),[/*]
[*]brush size ( 1â€“16).[/*]
[/list][/*]
[/list]Workflow
[b]Editing variants opens on the variants panel (on the right).[/b]
TheÂ [b]"Show All"[/b]Â function toggles the full list of head and body variants.
[b]Main Features[/b]
[list]
[*]Fully standalone mod â€“ does not require the original mod to be installed.[/*]
[*]Real-time switching between sprite sets.[/*]
[*]Full in-game pixel editor for head and body PNG files.[/*]
[*]Real-time preview: changes are immediately shown on the player model.[/*]
[*]Tools: Brush, Eraser, Color Picker, Fill, Line, Rectangle Outline, Rectangle Fill.[/*]
[*]Color wheel + hex input.[/*]
[*]Sliders for opacity, tint (dark/light), and brush size.[/*]
[*]Undo / Redo / Save.[/*]
[*]Saved color presets.[/*]
[*]External edit button ("Open in Paint").[/*]
[*]Character model preview panel with a list of interactive parts and variants.[/*]
[*]Empty sprite detection in the file list (
[code][empty][/code]Â marker).[/*]
[*]UI input protection (prevents clicks from reaching the pause menu).[/*]
[/list][b]Requirements[/b]
[list]
[*]BepInEx 5.4.23.5[/*]
[*]Casualties Unknown demo (Scav prototype) installed via Steam.[/*]
[/list][b]Acknowledgements[/b]
[list]
[*]Thanks to Carlo PoljanÄiÄ (dogederp) for creating the original Player Sprite Replacer mod and releasing it under the MIT license, which allowed me to create this modified version.[/*]
[*]Original game assets provided by Orsoniks.[/*]
[/list]

Name: Cu-Hotbar
Description:
[b]CuHotbar[/b]

[u]A customizable Minecraft-style hotbar for Casualties: Unknown. Bind items from your inventory to slots and switch them to your main hand instantly.[/u]

ä¸ºã€ŠæœªçŸ¥ä¼¤äº¡ã€‹æ·»åŠ ä¸€æ¡å¯è‡ªå®šä¹‰çš„å¿«æ·ç‰©å“æ ï¼šæŠŠèƒŒåŒ…ç‰©å“ç»‘å®šåˆ°æ§½ä½ï¼Œéšæ—¶å¿«é€Ÿåˆ‡åˆ°ä¸»æ‰‹ã€‚

[b]Installation instructions[/b]

1. Install BepInEx.
2. Place the `CuHotbar` folder into `BepInEx/plugins/`.
3. Launch the game; open settings from the main-menu button, the in-game pause button, or the SaveManager tab.

1. å®‰è£… BepInExã€‚
2. æŠŠ `CuHotbar` æ–‡ä»¶å¤¹æ”¾å…¥ `BepInEx/plugins/`ã€‚

[b]Main features[/b]

- Drag items from the inventory onto slots to bind them.
- Switch to the main hand with number keys or the scroll wheel; right-click to use the held item.
- Adjustable position, scale, and background opacity.
- Bindings cached per character, persisting across sessions.
- Auto-refill and original-spot return when items are used or dropped.

Name: Dog things
Description:
Description
Adds a series of dog-themed items to Casualties: Unknown, allowing your Expie to enjoy the happy (?) life of an Earth dog.
Now supports game version DEMO v7.0.

Installation instructions
1. Make sure BepInEx and RshLib are installed
2. Place DogThings.dll and the img/ folder (contains all PNG files) into BepInEx/plugins/

Main features
Added items below:
- Dog Collar : Slowly increases happiness when worn (max 30)Â Â 
- Dog Bowl : Grants extra happiness when drinking from it (max 50)Â 
- Pet Ball : Grants happiness on pickup
- Elizabethan Collar : Prevents self-harm once while worn
- Dog Chew : Edible multiple times, restores small amounts of hunger and happiness
- Dog Carrier : A backpack big enough to carry 50u
- Poop Bag : A bag that can only hold poop. But a lot of it.

Requirements
- BepInEx (5.x)
- RshLib (3.0.0)

Shout outs
Thanks to rushellxyz (the author of RshLib) for the custom item framework.

Source code and more details can be found atÂ [url=https://github.com/winddramon/Dogthings]https://github.com/winddramon/Dogthings[/url]

Name: Sillies don't see textures

[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]Have you played the game for so long that you can identify every item by its texture alone? This mod lets you once again experience the silliness of being an Expie.
This mod replaces item textures with custom â€œunknownâ€ textures whenever you don't recognize the item.[/color]

[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
Place the "UnknownItemTextures" folder in BepInEx/plugins.

[size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size]
You can also replace textures within a folder "UnknownItemTextures/Textures". Each category of item should have a texture in this folder.

[size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]The mod requires BepInEx 5[/color]

[i]A custom map/gamemode ("Fungame") management system forÂ [b]Casualties Unknown[/b].[/i]

[i][b]Custom Fungame Pack[/b]Â is a BepInEx plugin that introduces a custom map/gamemode system called "Fungame" to Casualties Unknown. It allows players to load, manage, and create custom maps with unique features, waypoints, items, and game logic.[/i]

Name: Casualties Unknown Dev Menu 7.0.1
Description:
[center][b][i][u]DESCRIPTION[/u][/i][/b][/center]
[center]Adds an in-game developer and cheat menu to Casualties Unknown 6.1. Open the menu with F1 and get full control over your playthrough â€” spawn any item directly into your inventory, heal instantly, toggle pixelate effect, or run any console command manually.

[b][i][u] INSTALLATION INSTRUCTIONS
[/u][/i][/b]1. Download the archive
2. Extract it into your Casualties Unknown folder (where CasualtiesUnknown.exe is located)
3. Launch the game and press F1 to open the menu

[u][i][b]MAIN FEATURES[/b][/i][/u][/center]
[center]- Spawn any item directly into your inventory
- Instant heal
- Pixelate toggle
- Custom console command input
- 5 item categories: Medicine, Food, Weapons, Tools, Other
- Search bar for quick item lookup
- Clean purple gradient UI
- Press F1 to open and close

[b][i][u]REQUIREMENTS[/u][/i][/b][/center]
[center]BepInEx 5.4.23.5 (x64) for Unity â€” already included in the archive, no separate download needed.

[b][i][u]SHOUT OUTS
Thanks to the Casualties Unknown dev team for a great game. Built with BepInEx and Harmony.[/u][/i][/b][/center]

Name: Crafting Framework
Description:
[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8][size=3]This Mod (previously "More Craftables") loads .json files from the "CraftingFramework\recipes" folder.[/size][/color]
[color=#D4D4D8][size=3]A template is provided within the folder as a .txt file with brief possibly wrong understanding of the variables.[/size][/color]

[color=#D4D4D8][size=3]Recipes from "More Craftables" are now available as Optional FIles.Â [/size][/color]
[color=#D4D4D8][size=3]I am willing to add more at request while keeping in mind balancing like before.[/size][/color]

[color=#ff7700][size=3][b]WARNING [color=#ffffff]: [/color][/b][color=#ffffff]It seemÂ [/color][color=#ffffff]Liquids must have the full amount in 1 container. (400ml of liquid cannot be 200 and 200)[/color][/size][/color]
[color=#00ff00][size=3][b]MP CompatibilityÂ [color=#ffffff]:Â [/color][/b][color=#ffffff]Host can craft, clients cannot unless they share recipes with host.[/color][/size][/color]

If anyone knows how to handle recipe syncing I'm all ears but otherwise that is outside my ability.

[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]Drag the contents of "CraftingFramework.zip" into your "BepInEx > Plugins" folder.[/color][/size]

[size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8][size=3]Adds reading .json files within the plugins folder and adding them as recipes to craft within the game.[/size][/color]

[size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]Bepinex[/color][/size]

[size=4][b]Shout outs[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]Shout out to the "Razor blade" mod byÂ  RushellXYZ having it's source code so I could see how recipes are handled.[/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]Shout out to my friend Tesseffex for helping me along the way with converting the mod.[/color]
Author: Deltash

Name: Terraria Unknown
Description:
[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
Replaces sprites, tiles, and audio with Terrarian.
(This mod is still in development, expect bugs.)
The mod weighs a lot because there are a lot of sounds in the Audio folder

[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]Place the plugins folder in BepInEx.
The plugins folder should eventually contain two files: Replacer.dll and ResourcePack.[/color]

[size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]Replaces sprites, tiles, and audio [/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]Easy to customize[/color]

[size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]The mod requires BepInEx, any version[/color]

[color=#d4d4d8][b][size=4]How to set your custom textu[/size][size=4]res[/size][/b][/color]
[color=#d4d4d8][size=3]Put your files in the correct folder[/size][/color]

[color=#d4d4d8]Sprites, Backgrounds, and Tools - Sprites Folder[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8][size=3]Tiles and UI - Texture folder[/size][/color]
[color=#d4d4d8][size=3]Music and SFX - Audio folder[/size][/color]
[color=#d4d4d8][size=3]The mod supports png, ogg, mp3, and wav files[/size][/color]

[color=#d4d4d8][size=3]Also, duplicate the image in the Textures folder if it's a weapon[/size][/color]
Author: Kegluss

Name: LuckyBlocks
Description:
[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
[b]Lucky Blocks[/b]Â transforms every block you destroy into a random surprise! Instead of boring regular loot, you'll get random items, hostile mobs, or deadly traps.
The deeper you dig, the more dangerous it becomes. Will you find rare loot, or will a thornbackelder crawl out of the block you just broke?

[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
[list=1]
[*][b]Install BepInEx[/b]Â (if you haven't already)
[list]
[*]Download BepInEx 5.x for your game version[/*]
[*]Extract it to your game folder (where the .exe is located)[/*]
[/list][/*]
[*][b]Install the mod[/b]
[list]
[*]Copy LuckyBlocks.dllÂ to BepInEx/plugins/[/*]
[/list][/*]
[*][b]Run the game[/b]
[list]
[*]That's it! Every destroyed block will now drop random loot.[/*]
[/list][/*]
[/list][size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size]
[list]
[*]âœ…Â [b]Every block is lucky[/b]Â â€“ Any destroyed block triggers a random drop[/*]
[*]âœ…Â [b]Random items[/b]Â â€“ Over 300+ items from the entire game (weapons, medical supplies, food, tools, ammo, etc.)[/*]
[*]âœ…Â [b]Hostile mobs[/b]Â â€“ Spawns enemies like Shadecrawler, Cave Ticks, Thornback, Wallbiter, and more[/*]
[*]âœ…Â [b]Deadly traps[/b]Â â€“ Landmines, bear traps, turrets, barbed wire, and other dangers[/*]
[*]âœ…Â [b]Depth-based difficulty[/b]Â â€“ The deeper you go, the higher the risk[/*]
[*]âœ…Â [b]Random condition[/b]Â â€“ Items spawn with 50-100% durability[/*]
[/list][size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][b]BepInEx 5.x[/b][/*]
[/list]
Author: Plushkaa

Name: New Firearms
Description:
New firearms reworks all gun-related things in Casualties Unknown

[size=4][b]Key features[/b][/size]
[size=3]- Fire mode selector, some full-auto guns can be put into semi-auto mode to not waste ammunition[/size]
[size=3]- Different magazine types, m1911 mag, small magazine, smg magazine, etc.[/size]
[size=3]- Different ammunition types: 12gauge birdshot, buckshot, slugshot; 9mm round, hollow and venomus[/size]
[size=1]You can also choose what ammo fill magazine with when spawning by adding $ at a end of id[/size]
[size=3]- Magazine feed guns, tube feed guns, barrel feed guns (with display of all chambers!)[/size]
[size=3]- Custom UI sprites for guns, custom sounds too[/size]
[size=3]- Balancing rework: guns significly buffed to make them more useful[/size]
[size=3]- Shooting right over/under enemy stuns them[/size]
[size=3]- SLiders for configurabile damage/jaming/casings[/size]
[size=3]- Multiplayer compatability![/size]
[size=3]- Support for custom gun packs by .json editing![/size]

[size=1]TODO: Update media page[/size]

All guns:
[spoiler]Makeshift pistol:
Â  Â Early-game gun, can be easily crafted, and most lightweight. With a tradeoff of dealing least damage of all, being able to hold no more then 2 rounds at a time, and having bad durability
M1911:
Â  Â 9mm semi-auto handgun, fairly lightweight and durabile. Can only hold 8 rounds in specialized magazine, craftable tho recipes will take a while to learn (18 int)
Glock 17 (pistol):
Â  Â Very durabile, holds 17 rounds in small magazine and good damage/accuracy. Not craftable
Glock 18:
Â  Â Upgrade of glock 17 (16 int). Same in everything, except option for full-auto

UZI:
Â  Â Reuses assets from SMG in gunsaw
Â  Â Custom rack/unrack sounds and buttons
Â  Â Craftable (19 int), moderate damage of 9mm
Â  Â Lightweight (1u), uses small magazines or SMG magazines
Â  Â Not very durabile, have high recoil, inaccurate
Â  Â High RPM and only full-auto
MP5:
Â  Reuses assets from Mini-SMG in gunsaw
Â  1.25u, highest damage of 9mm
Â  Uses small magazines or SMG magazines
Â  Most relaible of 9mm, accurate, select-fire
Vector:
[size=1]Â  TODO i prob would repaint it[/size]
Â  Reuses assets from Prototype in gunsaw
Â  Heavy (1.5u), highest damage of 9mm
Â  Uses only SMG magazines
Â  Very accurate and have almost no recoil
Â  Low RPM and select-fire
P90:
Â  Reuses assets from PDW in gunsaw
Â  1.25u, highest damage of 9mm
Â  Uses P90 magazines (that are not craftable...)
Â  Low recoil, select-fire

Sawed off:
Â  Reuses assets from Sawedoff in gunsaw
Â  Lightweight (1u), lowest damage of 12gauge
Double barrel:
Pump-action shotgun
Bolt-action rifle[/spoiler]
[color=#00ff00][b]MP Status: C[/b][b]ompatible[/b][/color]
Even more, gun jamming is no longer dysnching and removing mag now makes you auto pick up

Other mods that do anything with firearms (including rusiber's new firearms loader and my old new firearms) are [b]Incompatible[/b]
[b][size=4]
Installation instructions[/size][/b]
If you mod your game for first time (dont have BepInEx):
[spoiler]Go to BepInEx download page
https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases/tag/v5.4.23.5
And download BepInEx_win_x64_5.4.23.5
Open steam, choose browse local file
Extract BepInEx contents into game folder's root (winhttp.dll should be in same folder as executable)
Open game once and close
Now folderÂ BepInEx/plugins should be created

Additional instruction for Linux users:
Â  - AddÂ  `WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp=n,b"Â  %command%` into launch arguments[/spoiler]

Download and extractÂ RshLib.3.0.1 into BepInEx/plugins(if you haven't before)
Â  You can verify that RshLib loaded by seeing "This is a modded beta build" on top
Download `NewFirearms` and extractÂ into BepInEx/plugins

[size=4][b]Known bugs[/b][/size]

[size=2]Dismembered by a gun causes no yellow flesh to be dropped[/size]
[size=2]In multiplayer, killing trader from a gun, makes host to apply mood penality?[/size]
[size=2]Do not try to load save without this mod with this mod, and vica verse[/size]
[size=2]Switching your main hand might mess up with aim (but this bug is present in vanila too!)[/size]
[size=2]Tube feed option behave differently from what you'd expect IRL[/size]
[size=2]After loading a save gun might have wrong sprite, this is only visual[/size]
[size=2]Yet again, other firearms-releated mods not supported[/size]

[b][size=4]Future plans[/size][/b]
Â - More standalone guns (and crosbow!)(add more assets to already existing, and add rifles and special)
Â - Tube feed with shift (like if you have tube-feed shotgun full of buckshots, and then instert a birdshot pump slider, a birdshot now will be in chamber)
Â - Revolver feed (idk, the reloading it is ass)
Â - Battery powered (chemcoilgun, railgun, laser)
Â - Maybe minigame (i saw that conceptÂ https://youtu.be/eKnXDqIYSiE might implement)
Â -Â MaybeÂ firearm handling course
Â - Maybe attachments

Documentation for creating new gun pack have moved into NewFirearms/doc.txt
Please report any bugs you find
