Name: Last Stand Custom Music
Description:
[b]Last Stand Theme[/b]
Lightweight mod that plays a song when last stand activates.
Simply place an audio file inside with the .dll in plugins and it'll be up and running!

[b]Setup[/b]
[list=1]
[*]Install BepInEx 5.4.23.5 (or newer 5.x release) into your Casualties Unknown installation.[/*]
[*]This mod was developed and tested on Casualties Unknown v7.0.1. Ensure your version matches or expect weird behavior.[/*]
[*]Place the Ultimate Sound Swap DLL into:
[code]Casualties Unknown/
â””â”€â”€ BepInEx/
Â  Â  â””â”€â”€ plugins/
Â  Â  Â  Â  â””â”€â”€ LastStandTheme.dll[/code][/*]
[*]Launch the game once. The mod will automatically create a config file inside the config directory if one does not already exist. You can modify this file to enable/disable muting the drone and looping the song using a text editor like notepad:
[code]Casualties Unknown/
â””â”€â”€ BepInEx/
Â  Â  â””â”€â”€ config/
Â  Â  Â  Â  â””â”€â”€ sebastian.laststandtheme.cfg[/code][/*]
[*]Enable HideManagerGameObject in BepInEx's config file. You'll find it in:
[code]Casualties Unknown/
â””â”€â”€ BepInEx/
Â  Â  â””â”€â”€ config/
Â  Â  Â  Â  â””â”€â”€ BepInEx.cfg[/code]You should be able to open it in a text editor like notepad. Confirm that:
[code][Chainloader]
HideManagerGameObject = true[/code][/*]
[/list]
Author: SomeIdi0t

Name: ChangeSkin-Multiplayer
Description:
[size=6][b]Casualties Unknown - ChangeSkin Multiplayer[/b][/size]

Multiplayer-compatible build of ChangeSkin, adapted for Krokosha Casualties Unknown Multiplayer. Replace your character's sprite skin and sync it to other players who also have the mod.

[size=5][b]Compatibility[/b][/size]

[list]
[*]Loader: [b]BepInEx 5.4.23.5[/b][/*]
[*]Required dependency: [b]Krokosha Casualties Unknown Multiplayer[/b] (latest release)[/*]
[*]Game version: [b]Casualties Unknown demo v6.1[/b][/*]
[/list]
The game is still in demo â€” file layouts and APIs can change between versions, which may break the mod until it's updated.

[size=5][b]Please report any bugs you find via our GitHub issues page or here on NexusMods![/b][/size]


[size=5][b]Permissions[/b][/size]

If the game creator [b]Orsoniks[/b] does not want this mod public, please contact me and I will take it down.


[size=5][b]Installation[/b][/size]

[size=4][b]1. Prerequisites[/b][/size]

[list=1]
[*]Install BepInEx 5 â€” official guide: [url=https://docs.bepinex.dev/articles/user_guide/installation/index.html]https://docs.bepinex.dev/articles/user_guide/installation/index.html[/url][/*]
[*]Install [b]Krokosha Casualties Unknown Multiplayer[/b] (required) following its own instructions.[/*]
[/list]
[size=4][b]2. ChangeSkinMP[/b][/size]

[list=1]
[*]Open the mod archive and grab the
[code]ChangeSkinMP[/code]folder.[/*]
[*]Drop it into your game's
[code]BepInEx/plugins[/code]folder â€” the same place where Krokosha Casualties Multiplayer lives. You should end up with
[code]BepInEx/plugins/ChangeSkinMP/ChangeSkinMP.dll[/code]and a
[code]resources[/code]folder next to it.[/*]
[*]Start the game.[/*]
[/list]

[size=5][b]Usage[/b][/size]

[list=1]
[*]Start a run and open the in-game console.[/*]
[*]Load a skin â€” local or remote:
[list]
[*][code]skin load-local robot[/code]â€” bundled robot skin (or any folder you've added to
[code]resources[/code]).[/*]
[*][code]skin load-remote https://skin.cat-bot.de/d/163[/code]â€” download from the skin portal.[/*]
[/list][/*]
[*]Turn on replacement:
[code]skin enable[/code].[/*]
[*]Done. Other players will see your skin only if [b]they also have the mod installed[/b] and [b]the host has it installed too[/b] (the host is what syncs skin data between clients).[/*]
[/list]

[size=5][b]Console commands[/b][/size]

[list]
[*][code]skin load-local <name>[/code]â€” load a local skin from
[code]resources/<name>/[/code][/*]
[*][code]skin load-remote <url>[/code]â€” load a remote skin from a URL[/*]
[*][code]skin enable[/code]â€” turn skin replacement on[/*]
[*][code]skin disable[/code]â€” turn it off[/*]
[*][code]skin ban <player>[/code]â€” block a specific player's skin from being shown to you[/*]
[*][code]skin unban <player>[/code]â€” undo a ban[/*]
[*][code]skin rule-set <rule> <true|false>[/code]â€” set a rule[/*]
[*][code]skin rule-get <rule>[/code]â€” read the current value of a rule[/*]
[/list]
Available rules:
[list]
[*][code]SkinUploading[/code]â€” whether your skin gets sent to other players[/*]
[*][code]SkinDownloading[/code]â€” whether you receive and display other players' skins[/*]
[/list]

[size=5][b]Creating your own skins[/b][/size]

[list=1]
[*]Back up your current local skin folder first.[/*]
[*]Duplicate the bundled
[code]robot[/code]folder and rename the copy to your skin's name.[/*]
[*]Replace the PNGs inside with your own art. [b]Keep the file names exactly the same[/b] â€” sprites are matched by file name, so renaming them will break replacement.[/*]
[*]Try to match the original image dimensions. I haven't tested what happens when they don't, so YMMV.[/*]
[/list]
Load it in-game with
[code]skin load-local <yourFolderName>[/code].


[size=5][b]Finding skins[/b][/size]

Main skin portal: [url=https://skin.cat-bot.de/]https://skin.cat-bot.de/[/url]

Browse community skins there, then load directly with
[code]skin load-remote <url>[/code].


[size=5][b]Notes[/b][/size]

[list]
[*]You do [b]not[/b] need to edit
[code]sharedassets1.assets[/code]for this mod.[/*]
[*]Both the host and any client whose skin needs to sync must have the mod installed.[/*]
[/list]

[size=5][b]Bug reports[/b][/size]

Something broken? Report it here on NexusMods or open an issue on GitHub: [url=https://github.com/05126619z/ChangeSkin-Multiplayer]https://github.com/05126619z/ChangeSkin-Multiplayer[/url]


[size=5][b]Credits[/b][/size]

[list]
[*][b]Mod by:[/b] dogederp (Karlo)[/*]
[*][b]Contributor / original ChangeSkin author:[/b] 05126619z[/*]
[*][b]Playtester:[/b] Dmonya[/*]
[/list]

[size=5][b]Contributing & support[/b][/size]

[list]
[*]GitHub (primary â€” gets updates first): [url=https://github.com/05126619z/ChangeSkin-Multiplayer]https://github.com/05126619z/ChangeSkin-Multiplayer[/url][/*]
[*]Discord: [url=https://discord.gg/aCBrFPYYjs]https://discord.gg/aCBrFPYYjs[/url][/*]
[*]Pull requests welcome.[/*]
[*]Open an issue for questions or bug reports.[/*]
[*]Got screenshots or videos of your skins? Feel free to add them to this page â€” I'd love to see them.[/*]
[*]Buy me a coffee: [url=https://buymeacoffee.com/dogederp]https://buymeacoffee.com/dogederp[/url][/*]
[/list]

[size=5][b]Looking for an alternative?[/b][/size]

If this mod doesn't fit your needs:
[list]
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/15]Player Sprite Replacer[/url] â€” simpler, single-player, Numpad-based skin swapper.[/*]
[/list]
Author: d0gederp

Name: WorldMap
Description:
[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
WorldMap â€“ Never get lost again!

Adds a fully functional minimap and fullscreen world map toÂ [b]Scav Prototype.Â [/b]Helps you navigate underground layers, spot valuable items and containers, and explore with confidence.

[b][size=4]Features:[/size][/b]
[list]
[*][b]Fog of war[/b]Â â€“ the map is hidden until you explore. Walk around to reveal terrain in a 10â€‘block radius (adjustable).[/*]
[*][b]Realâ€‘time terrain display[/b]Â â€“ different colors for different materials (stone, sand, soil, grass, metal, etc.).[/*]
[*][b]Player marker[/b]Â â€“ red dot with a white line showing your facing direction.[/*]
[*][b]Item markers[/b]Â â€“ yellow dots for loose items on the ground.[/*]
[*][b]Container markers[/b]Â â€“ blue squares for any container.[/*]
[*][b]Other crates[/b]Â â€“ brown squares for containercrate, foodbox, etc.[/*]
[*][b]Med crates & drop capsules[/b]Â â€“ special icons for medical crates and supply pods.[/*]
[*][b]Trader markers[/b]Â â€“ green squares for neutral NPC traders.[/*]
[*][b]Enemy markers[/b]Â â€“ red squares for spiders, crawlers, ticks, thornbacks, and other hostile creatures.[/*]
[*][b]Buildings & interactable objects[/b]Â â€“ grey squares with border / yellow circles.[/*]
[*][b]Fullscreen map[/b]Â â€“ rightâ€‘click on the minimap to open a large, draggable, zoomable map. Rightâ€‘click again to close.[/*]
[*][b]Smooth zoom[/b]Â â€“ mouse wheel zooms in/out on both minimap (2â€“500 blocks radius) and fullscreen map (0.2Ã— â€“ 20Ã—).[/*]
[*][b]Minimap window[/b]Â â€“ dragÂ [b]LMB[/b]Â anywhere on the minimap to move it. Player controls are automatically locked while dragging (no accidental movement or attacks).[/*]
[*][b]Configurable hotkey[/b]Â â€“ change the toggle key in BepInEx/config/com.purpleuser.worldmap.cfg (default: M).[/*]
[*][b]High refresh rate[/b]Â â€“ both maps update at ~60 FPS for smooth realâ€‘time tracking.[/*]
[*][b]Works on all layers[/b]Â â€“ explored fog of war is preserved when descending/ascending and after world regeneration.[/*]
[/list][size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
[list=1]
[*]Install BepInEx 5 (if not already installed):
[list]
[*]Download from:Â [url=https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases]https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases[/url][/*]
[*]Extract to your game folder[/*]
[*]Run the game once, then close it[/*]
[/list][/*]
[*]Install the mod:
[list]
[*]Download MinimapMod.dll[/*]
[*]Copy it to: [Game Folder]\BepInEx\plugins\[/*]
[/list][/*]
[*]Launch the game â€“ the minimap will appear in the topâ€‘right corner.[/*]
[/list][b][size=4]Controls[/size][/b]
[list]
[*]M (customizable) â€“ toggle minimap on/off[/*]
[*]Mouse wheel (over the minimap) â€“ zoom in/out[/*]
[*]LMB drag on the minimap â€“ move minimap window (player controls locked while dragging)[/*]
[*]Rightâ€‘click on minimap â€“ open fullscreen map[/*]
[*]LMB drag on fullscreen map â€“ pan the map[/*]
[*]Mouse wheel on fullscreen map â€“ zoom in/out[/*]
[*]Rightâ€‘click on fullscreen map â€“ close fullscreen map[/*]
[/list][left][b][size=4]Configuration[/size][/b]
After first launch, editÂ BepInEx/config/com.USER.worldmap.cfg:[/left]
[left]ToggleKey = M[/left]
[left][/left]
[size=4][b]Requirements:[/b][/size]
BepInEx 5 (any 5.x version)
Author: Plushkaa

Name: Quick Medical Automation
Description:
Quick Medical AutomationÂ is aÂ BepInExÂ medical automation mod made forÂ Scav Prototype / Casualties Unknown Demo.
It automatically evaluates the player's current condition and uses accessible medical supplies in priority order, focusing first on critical life signs, severe blood loss, major bleeding, glass / shrapnel, fractures / dislocations, infection / sepsis, and severe pain or opiate withdrawal.
The goal of this mod is not to â€œplay the game for you,â€ but to reduce repetitive medical micromanagement so you can focus more on exploration, survival, and combat decisions.
[b]Main Features[/b]
[list]
[*]Automatically evaluates the player's current vitals and injuries[/*]
[*]Automatically uses accessible medical supplies for treatment[/*]
[*]Recursively scans inventory slots and container contents for medical items[/*]
[*]Prioritizes severe blood loss, open wounds, and critical conditions[/*]
[*]Automatically removes glass / shrapnel[/*]
[*]Automatically handles dislocations and fracture splinting[/*]
[*]Automatically handles infection, sepsis, and local disinfection[/*]
[*]Automatically manages pain relief and opiate withdrawal when needed[/*]
[*]Supports both Chinese and English[/*]
[*]Includes an in-game standalone configuration panel[/*]
[*]Supports notification mode, scan interval, panel scale, and opacity adjustment[/*]
[/list][b]How to Use[/b]
[b]Default Hotkeys[/b]
[list]
[*]F8: Toggle auto treatment on / off[/*]
[*]F9: Run one immediate smart treatment pulse[/*]
[*]F11: Open / close the config panel[/*]
[/list][b]Interface[/b]
[list]
[*]Open the standalone settings panel in-game[/*]
[*]Settings can be changed live without restarting the game[/*]
[/list][b]Configurable Options[/b]
[list]
[*]Master automation toggle[/*]
[*]Language switch (Chinese / English)[/*]
[*]Treatment mode (Conservative / Balanced / Aggressive)[/*]
[*]Notification mode[/*]
[*]Scan interval[/*]
[*]Panel scale[/*]
[*]Panel opacity[/*]
[*]Whether specific medicine, dressing, and splint categories may be used automatically[/*]
[/list][b]Currently Supported Major Item Types[/b]
[list]
[*]Combat Pen[/*]
[*]Blood bag / Human blood bag[/*]
[*]Blood coagulant[/*]
[*]Various bandages, dressings, and suturing items[/*]
[*]Tweezers[/*]
[*]Splint / Carcass splint[/*]
[*]Wrench / Makeshift wrench[/*]
[*]Antibiotics / Ceftriaxone / Antiserum[/*]
[*]LRD / Makeshift LRD[/*]
[*]Disinfectant[/*]
[*]Pain cream / Wound glue / Painkillers[/*]
[*]Morphine / Opium / Fentanyl / Naloxone[/*]
[*]Saline / Ringer solution[/*]
[*]Clotting mush / Purple herb / Bulbskin[/*]
[*]Chest drain / Ice pack[/*]
[*]Antiseptic mush / Glowplant fruit / Xaloris sponge[/*]
[/list][b]Installation[/b]
[list=1]
[*]Make sureÂ BepInExÂ is installed[/*]
[*]PlaceÂ QuickMedicalAutomation.dllÂ into:
BepInEx/plugins/[/*]
[*]Launch the game[/*]
[/list][b]Notes[/b]
[list]
[*]Human blood bagÂ is not used automatically by default[/*]
[*]Combat PenÂ is reserved for last-stand emergency rescue[/*]
[*]Pain-relief logic tries to avoid use during low blood pressure, low oxygen, cardiac arrest, or high opiate load[/*]
[*]Glass / shrapnel removal is prioritized above routine wound dressing[/*]
[*]For balance reasons, automatic glass / shrapnel removal and automatic bone-setting are not instant, and usually take aroundÂ 15-30 seconds[/*]
[/list]

Name: Random Layer
Description:
[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
Tired of the same linear progression? This mod randomizes your journey through the layers! Start on a random layer and each time you go deeper, you teleport to another random layer.Â 

[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
[list=1]
[*]Install BepInEx (if not already installed)[/*]
[*]Copy RandomLayer.dll to BepInEx/plugins[/*]
[*]Launch the game[/*]
[/list][size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size]
[list]
[*]Random spawn on any layer (0-4)[/*]
[*]Random transitions when going deeper[/*]
[*]No layer repeats in a row[/*]
[*]Fresh experience every playthrough[/*]
[/list][b][size=4]Configuration[/size][/b]

After launching the game once with the new version, a configuration file will appear at: BepInEx/config/com.purpleuser.randomlayer.cfg
Open it with any text editor. You can change the following settings:

Setting: StartLayer
Values: -1 (random), 0, 1, 2, 3, 4
Layer you start on. -1 = random (original behaviour).

Setting: RandomTransitions
Values: true / false
If true, every time you go down you teleport to a random layer. If false, normal linear progression (0â†’1â†’2â†’3â†’4).

Setting: ExcludeLayers
Values: commaâ€‘separated numbers, e.g. 4 or 3,4
Layers you want to skip during random transitions. Useful for avoiding unbalanced or disliked layers.
Example â€“ always start on layer 1, go random, but never go to layer 4 (overgrown):
StartLayer = 1
RandomTransitions = true
ExcludeLayers = 4

After editing the file, save it and restart the game.

[size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[list]
[*]BepInEx 5[/*]
[/list]
Author: Plushkaa

Name: Infinite Batteries
Description:
[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
This mod makes all batteries and battery-powered items last forever. No more searching for batteries or worrying about your flashlight dying.

[b][size=4]Works on:[/size][/b]
[list]
[*]Small, Medium, Large batteries[/*]
[*]Flashlights & emergency light[/*]
[*]AED & manual defibrillator[/*]
[*]Drills (laser, heavy, plasma)[/*]
[*]Terrain scanner, MP3 player, and more[/*]
[/list][size=3][b]Configurable[/b]Â [/size]â€“ enable/disable via config file.

[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
[list=1]
[*]Install BepInEx[/*]
[*]Copy InfiniteBatteries.dll to BepInEx/plugins[/*]
[*]Launch the game[/*]
[/list][b]To disable:[/b]Â editÂ BepInEx/config/com.purpleuser.infinitepbatteries.cfgÂ and setÂ InfiniteBatteries = [b]false[/b]

[size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size]
[list]
[*]Batteries never drain[/*]
[*]Emergency light works forever[/*]
[*]Configurable on/off[/*]
[*]Works with existing saves[/*]
[/list][heading][size=4]Configuration[/size][/heading]
After launching the game once, a config file will appear at: [size=4]BepInEx/config/com.purpleuser.infinitepbatteries.cfg[/size]

Open it with any text editor and change:

InfiniteBatteries = [b]trueÂ  [/b]â†’ batteries never drain (default)
InfiniteBatteries = [b]false [/b]â†’ mod disabled, batteries drain normally

[b]Multiplier[/b]Â (only works when InfiniteBatteries = false):Â 

BatteryDrainMultiplier = 0.5Â  â†’ 2x slower drain
BatteryDrainMultiplier = 2 â†’ 2x faster drain

Save the file and restart the game for changes to take effect.

[size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[list]
[*]BepInEx 5[/*]
[/list]

Name: Bren's Makeshift Guns
Description:
[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
[color=#d4d4d8]Adds a handful of firearms to Casualties Unknown. Compatible with most other firearm mods too.[/color]


[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]Make sure to download/install BepInEx, RshLib, and New Gun Loader if you haven't already.

Links:
[url=https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases/download/v5.4.23.5/BepInEx_win_x64_5.4.23.5.zip]Direct download to BepInEx[/url]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/32?tab=description][/url][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/32?tab=description]RshLib[/url]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/57?tab=description][/url][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/57?tab=description]New Gun Loader mod[/url]

Follow the instructions provided by the previously mentioned mods too.


Once that is done, either copy-paste or drag the contents in MakeshiftGunsMod>NewGun to BepInEx>plugins>NewGun using "Browse local files" on Steam (or by finding Casualties Unknown's game files yourself).


If that doesn't work, try again and/or check another gun mod for better instructions.[/color]


[size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size]

[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]Adds five improvised-looking or otherwise old-fashioned weapons to C:U that can be found in the console (and MAYBE in normal gameplay, I haven't checked):[/color][/size]

[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]Makeshift Semi Automatic Rifle - A semi automatic rifle loaded with up to 12 individual 9mm rounds.
Makeshift Pistol - A semi-automatic pistol loaded with up to 7 individual 9mm rounds.[/color][/size]
[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]Makeshift Shotgun - A double-barrel shotgun loaded with 12 gauge.[/color][/size]
[color=#d4d4d8]Makeshift SMG - A simplistic SMG that uses 9mm pistol magazines. Features a decent firerate and a magazine capacity of 20.[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]Makeshift Bolt Action - A bolt-action rifle that can be loaded with up to 4 individual 5.56mm rounds.[/color]


[size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]Requires BepInEx, RshLib, and New Gun Loader to function.[/color][/size]


[size=4][b]Shout outs
[/b][/size][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/profile/7432547877542Al]RushellXYZ[/url] for making RshLib
[color=#D4D4D8]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/profile/rusiber]rusiber[/url]Â for making the New Gun Loader


[size=4][b]Known Issues[/b][/size][/color]
- [b](MP ONLY)[/b] These guns do not appear for clients who do not have the mod. Despite this, they still get damaged by most of the weapons (see above for exception).
- They're uncraftable. Use the console if you want to play around with them, or gamble by looking for traders.
Author: BrenGun aka John Broken Paradise

Description:
â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•

This mod makes all equipment completely indestructible.
Every weapon, tool, armor, backpack, and container lasts forever at 100% condition. No more repairing, no more searching for replacements.[/b]
â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•â•

[heading]ðŸ› ï¸ Configuration[/heading]
After first launch, the config file is created at: [b]BepInEx/config/infinity_durability.cfg[/b]
Open it with Notepad to customize everything.
Available settings:
[b][size=4][General][/size][/b]
Enabled = true â€“ turn mod on/off
Debug = false â€“ enable console logging
[size=4][b][Durability][/b][/size]
Tools = true â€“ infinite tools
Weapons = true â€“ infinite weapons
Armor = true â€“ infinite armor
Backpacks = true â€“ infinite backpacks
Containers = true â€“ infinite containers
Clothing = true â€“ infinite clothing
[b][size=4][Consumables][/size][/b]
Food = true â€“ food decays or infinite
Medicine = true â€“ medicine used or infinite
Batteries = true â€“ batteries drain or infinite
Ammo = true â€“ ammo consumed or infinite
[b][size=4][Exceptions][/size][/b]
Liquids = true â€“ liquids normal or infinite
[Advanced]
Mult = 1 â€“ durability multiplier (0 to 10)
[b][size=4]How to change settings:[/size][/b]
[list=1]
[*]Close the game[/*]
[*]Open infinity_durability.cfg in Notepad[/*]
[*]Change true to false or false to true[/*]
[*]Save the file[/*]
[*]Launch the game[/*]
[/list]Changes take effect after restart.

Name: DG-Lab EXP BodySync
Description:
[center][size=5][b]DG-Lab EXP BodySync[/b][/size]
BodySync-style DG-Lab feedback for [i]Casualties Unknown[/i][/center]

[line]

[size=4][b]Overview[/b][/size]

[b]DG-Lab EXP BodySync[/b] adds a BodySync-style DG-Lab layer to [i]Casualties Unknown[/i].

Instead of working as a simple damage trigger, it follows the character's body state more closely. Local injuries, body-part changes, pain, bleeding, fractures, dislocations, infection, shock, hypoxia, temperature, consciousness, and related conditions can all become part of the feedback logic.

The result is a more connected DG-Lab experience where feedback can follow both [b]where[/b] something happens and [b]what kind[/b] of condition the character is experiencing.

[line]

[size=4][b]What BodySync Does[/b][/size]

BodySync reads the game's body-state system and sends feedback through [b]DG-Lab Socket v2[/b]. Local effects can be routed by body part, while systemic conditions can be mixed into the background output.

For example, you can bind the head, upper torso, and arms to one DG-Lab channel, while assigning the abdomen and legs to another. Direct injuries can then lean toward the matching channel, while broader physical states may affect both.

This makes the feedback less like a single on/off reaction and more like a layered response to the character's current condition.

[line]

[size=4][b]Main Features[/b][/size]

[list]
[*]BodySync-style DG-Lab support for [i]Casualties Unknown[/i].[/*]
[*]DG-Lab Socket v2 connection support.[/*]
[*]Embedded Socket backend with in-game QR code generation.[/*]
[*]External Socket backend support for custom setups.[/*]
[*]Configurable A/B channel strength limits.[/*]
[*]Configurable body-part binding for local injury routing.[/*]
[*]Feedback based on injuries, pain, bleeding, fractures, dislocations, infection, shock, hypoxia, temperature, consciousness, and related states.[/*]
[*]In-game DG-Lab menu opened with [b]F10[/b].[/*]
[*]Status overlay, waveform monitor, and debug display.[/*]
[*]Offline status and waveform preview for checking the setup before connecting a device.[/*]
[/list]
[line]

[size=4][b]Getting Started[/b][/size]

Install the mod with [b]BepInEx 5[/b], then launch the game and press [b]F10[/b] to open the DG-Lab menu.

If you use the embedded backend, keep your phone and the PC running the game on the same local network. Select the correct LAN address in the menu, refresh the QR code, and scan it with the DG-Lab app.

Before testing, set low A/B strength limits and check the connection state, current strength, and waveform output with the status overlay or waveform monitor.

[line]

[size=4][b]Safety Note[/b][/size]

This mod maps in-game injuries and body states into simulated DG-Lab waveform and strength changes.

Start with low A/B strength limits. Keep the DG-Lab app or device stop controls within reach. Stop immediately if you feel pain, numbness, dizziness, panic, skin irritation, or anything unusual.

Name: Unknown Enough Items
Description:
[quote][b]Github:[url=https://github.com/MAakber/Unknown-Enough-Items]Github[/url][/b][/quote]
[b][color=#ff7700]Description[/color][/b]

Unknown Enough Items, or UEI, is a JEI-style helper mod for Casualties Unknown Demo. It adds an in-game item, liquid, and recipe browser beside the native inventory and backpack UI, without replacing the original crafting system.

UEI reads runtime game data directly, so it can show item sources, recipe results, usage recipes, generic ingredient candidates, and related crafting chains.

[color=#ff7700][b]Installation instructions[/b][/color]

1. Install BepInEx for Casualties Unknown Demo.
2. Download `UEI.build.dll`.
3. Put the file here:
[code]BepInEx/plugins/UEI/UEI.build.dll[/code]4. Launch the game.
5. Open the native inventory or radial backpack UI to show UEI.

[b][color=#ff7700]Main features[/color][/b]

[list]
[*]Browse runtime items and liquids.[/*]
[*]Search items and filter by category.[/*]
[*]Favorite entries and pin useful recipes.[/*]
[*]Left-click an entry to view source recipes.[/*]
[*]Right-click an entry to view usage recipes.[/*]
[*]Click recipe materials or results to jump through recipe chains.[/*]
[*]Jump to the original crafting panel and select the matching recipe.[/*]
[*]Hover an inventory item and press `U` to open its UEI usage panel.[/*]
[*]Chinese and English UI support.[/*]
[*]KrokMP multiplayer compatibility.[/*]
[*]Cheat mode respects multiplayer command/admin permissions and disables itself when unavailable.[/*]
[/list]
[b][color=#ff7700]Requirements[/color][/b]

- Casualties Unknown Demo
- BepInEx
- TextMeshPro runtime dependencies from the game
- KrokMP is optional and only required for multiplayer compatibility features

[color=#ff7700][b]Shout outs[/b][/color]

Inspired by JEI-style item and recipe browsers. Thanks to the Casualties Unknown community for testing, feedback, and multiplayer compatibility reports.
Author: Aakber

---
Since game version 7.0.1 has added a very comprehensive menu function compared to 6.1, and MenuMod is not compatible with ScavLib versions after 0.5.0, MenuMod is currently only a archive file and is not updated or maintained.


[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]

[color=#D4D4D8][b]WARNING: Currently in the testing and rapid updating/iterating phase.[/b]
Please expect potential bugs and frequent updates. We highly recommend backing up your save files before playing. Feel free to report any issues or feedback in the comments![/color]

[b]MenuMod[/b] adds a fully featured, elegant, and functional in-game Pause and Settings Menu to the game. Activated by pressing your pause key (ESC by default), it safely freezes game time and provides a centralized interface to customize your gameplay experience.

[b]Supports Simplified Chinese and English only.[/b]

[size=4][b]Installation Instructions[/b][/size]

[list=1]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Download and install [b]BepInEx 5[/b] and [b][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/83?tab=description]ScavLib API[/url] 0.4.x[/b][/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Download the MenuMod archive and extract it.[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Move [b]MenuMod.dll[/b] into your game folder at [b]BepInEx/plugins/[/b].[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Run the game, load a world, and press [b]ESC[/b] to open the Pause Menu![/color][/*]
[/list][size=4][b]Main Features[/b][/size]

[color=#D4D4D8][b]Pause Menu[/b][/color]

[list]
[*][color=#D4D4D8][b]Time Freeze:[/b] Automatically pauses game physics and timers (Time.timeScale = 0) when open. Can be toggled off in General Settings.[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8][b]Run Timer:[/b] Displays your current run time directly in the pause menu.[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8][b]In-Game Save:[/b] Save your progress at any time with a single click.[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8][b]Safe Exit:[/b] "Save and Return to Main Menu" and "Quit to Desktop" (auto-saves before quitting).[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8][b]Abandon Run:[/b] A dedicated "Abandon Run and Quit" button that exits [i]without[/i] saving, protected by an inline confirmation prompt to prevent accidents.[/color][/*]
[/list][b]Advanced Settings Tabs[/b]

[list=1]
[*][b]General Settings:[/b]
[list]
[*][b]Background Auto-Save:[/b] Toggle periodic background auto-saving while in-game.[/*]
[*][b]Custom Auto-Save Intervals:[/b] Choose between 1 min, 5 min, 10 min, 30 min, or 1 hour.[/*]
[*][b]Smart Auto-Save:[/b] Automatically skips saving if the player is dead or unconscious to prevent corrupted saves.[/*]
[*][b]ESC Auto-Pause Toggle:[/b] Choose whether pressing ESC should freeze the game world.[/*]
[*][b]Quick Save Hotkey:[/b] Bind a dedicated key (default: F6) for instant in-game saving without opening any menu.[/*]
[*][b]Run Timer (In-Settings):[/b] Your current run time is also displayed at the top of the General tab while in-game.[/*]
[*][b]Save Management:[/b] Open your save folder or keybind file directly from the menu. Includes a protected Delete Save button with an inline confirmation prompt.[/*]
[*][b]Run Parameters:[/b] Adjust loot rarity, trap rarity, per-layer loot decay, per-layer trap increase, radiation line toggle, and unchipped mode â€” all written directly to the game's PlayerPrefs and applied on the next layer.[/*]
[/list][/*]
[*][b]Video Settings:[/b]
[list]
[*][b]Resolution Control:[/b] Cycle through all monitor-supported resolutions.[/*]
[*][b]Fullscreen Modes:[/b] Switch between Exclusive Fullscreen, Windowed, and Borderless Window.[/*]
[*][b]Pending Apply System:[/b] Resolution, fullscreen mode, quality level, and render scale changes are staged and only applied when you click the corresponding Apply button â€” no accidental mid-session disruptions.[/*]
[*][b]Render Scale:[/b] Expose the hidden URP render scale slider (0.50Ã— â€“ 2.00Ã—, step 0.05) for performance tuning or supersampling.[/*]
[*][b]Quality Preset:[/b] Cycle through Unity quality presets with a single Apply click.[/*]
[*][b]VSync Toggle:[/b] Enable or disable vertical sync instantly.[/*]
[*][b]FPS Limiter:[/b] Set a custom frame rate cap when VSync is off. Automatically disabled when VSync is on.[/*]
[*][b]Gamma Slider:[/b] Adjust brightness in real time, synced with the game camera's post-processing profile. Supports values above 1.0 via direct volume override for darker environments.[/*]
[*][b]Pixel Filter / Line of Sight / Reduce Visual FX:[/b] Toggle visual filters and effect intensity on the fly.[/*]
[*][b]Environment Effects:[/b] Independently suppress rain, fogs.[/*]
[*][b]Camera Zoom:[/b] Adjust the in-game orthographic zoom level in real time (0.05Ã— â€“ 2.00Ã—, default ~0.25Ã—). Resets with a single button.[/*]
[*][b]Scrollable Layout:[/b] The entire Video tab scrolls, so all options remain accessible regardless of window size.[/*]
[/list][/*]
[*][b]Audio Settings:[/b]
[list]
[*][b]Independent Volume Sliders:[/b] Fine-tune Music, Master (SFX), and Ambient volumes individually â€” displayed as clean percentages.[/*]
[*][b]Mute All:[/b] Instantly silence all game audio with a single toggle, without changing your volume settings.[/*]
[*][b]Smart Ambient Control:[/b] The Ambient slider is only available in-game where world audio is actually loaded.[/*]
[*][b]Now Playing:[/b] Displays the name of the currently playing music track while in-game.[/*]
[/list][/*]
[*][b]Keybind Rebinding:[/b]
[list]
[*][b]In-Game Rebinds:[/b] Interactively rebind all movement and action hotkeys (jump, speed multipliers, inventory, crafting, and more).[/*]
[*][b]Friendly Key Names:[/b] Displays human-readable labels (e.g. "LMB", "R.Shift", "L.Ctrl", "Num Enter").[/*]
[*][b]Failsafe Lock:[/b] The Pause/Menu key is locked and cannot be accidentally rebound.[/*]
[/list][/*]
[*][b]Language Settings:[/b]
[list]
[*][b]Separate Mod UI Language:[/b] Switch MenuMod's own interface between English and Simplified Chinese instantly â€” no scene reload required.[/*]
[*][b]Game Language Selector:[/b] Browse and apply all installed game language files from a scrollable list.[/*]
[*][b]Reload Confirmation:[/b] Game language changes prompt a confirmation dialog before reloading the scene.[/*]
[/list][/*]
[/list][size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][color=#D4D4D8][b]BepInEx 5.x[/b][/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8][b][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/83?tab=description]ScavLib API 0.4.x[/url][/b][/color][/*]
[/list][size=4][b]Shout Outs[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Developed by [b]Kanisuko[/b] and [b]QinShenYu[/b].[/color][/*]
[/list]
Author: QinShenYu

Name: ScavLib API
Description:
[b][size=6]ScavLib â€” Casualties Unknown Mod API Library[/size][/b]

[b]Authors:[/b] Kanisuko / QinShenYuÂ 
[b]Required by:[/b] Any mod built on ScavLibÂ 
[b]Source (DLL repository): [url=https://github.com/Kanisuko/ScavLib-API-DLL-Repository]github.com/Kanisuko/ScavLib-API-DLL-Repository[/url]Â [/b]
[b][color=#ffffff]Source Code:Â  [url=https://github.com/Kanisuko/ScavLib-API]github.com/Kanisuko/ScavLib-API[/url][/color][/b]
[b]Wiki:Â [url=https://github.com/Kanisuko/ScavLib-API-DLL-Repository/wiki]github.com/Kanisuko/ScavLib-API-DLL-Repository/wiki[/url][/b]
[line]
[b][size=5]Description[/size][/b]

ScavLib is a foundational developer-focused API and utility library designed to simplify mod development for [b]Scav Prototype (a.k.a. Casualties Unknown)[/b]. It streamlines common tasks such as building in-game menus, managing configurations, registering console commands, handling custom events, interacting safely with the game state, and working with player vitals, skills, and items.

[quote][b]Note for players:[/b] This is a core dependency library. It does not add any content on its own, but is required by other mods to function.[/quote][line]
[b][size=5]Installation[/size][/b]
[list=1]
[*]Ensure you have [b]BepInEx 5[/b] installed for Scav Prototype.[/*]
[*]Download [font=Courier New]ScavLib.dll[/font].[/*]
[*]Place [font=Courier New]ScavLib.dll[/font] into your game directory under [font=Courier New]BepInEx/plugins/[/font].[/*]
[*]Launch the game. To verify it loaded correctly, open the developer console and type:
[code]scavlib status[/code]You should see ScavLib's version, authors, and all registered mods printed to the console.[/*]
[/list][line]
[b][size=5]Regarding multiplayer support[/size][/b]
[b]Multiplayer games are theoretically supported, both the client and server need to have ScavLib installed.[/b]
[line]
[b][size=5][b]Requirements[/b][/size][/b]
[list]
[*][b][b]BepInEx 5.x[/b] (64-bit version recommended)[/b][/*]
[*][b]Scav Prototype game client (Steam)[/b][/*]
[/list][line]
[b][size=5]For Developers[/size]

You can see wiki onÂ [url=https://github.com/Kanisuko/ScavLib-API-DLL-Repository/wiki]Github wiki page[/url]Â or you can see source code on [url=https://github.com/Kanisuko/ScavLib-API]ScavLib API[/url],Â thanks.[/b]

[b]Of course, if you discover any new issues, we recommend that you submit an issue report to the DLL repository on GitHub.[/b]

[b](I sincerely apologize for the bugs that appeared, as I was the only tester and was unable to conduct comprehensive testing.)[/b]
[line]
[b][size=5][b]Shout-outs[/b][/size]

Developed by [b]Kanisuko[/b] and [b]QinShenYu[/b].[/b]
Author: Team Ethertaco

Name: Cu-SaveManager
Description:
[b][u]SaveManager[/u][/b]

æŽ¨èé€‰æ‹©ç”Ÿæˆä¸–ç•Œçš„æ–¹æ³•ä½¿ç”¨æœ¬modçš„ï¼ŒKrokMPå½“å‰ä¿å­˜ä¸–ç•Œä¼šå›ºå®šä¿å­˜åˆ°ä¸‹ä¸€å±‚çº§ï¼Œè€Œä¸æ˜¯ä¿å­˜å½“å‰å±‚çº§

Fixed-world multi-save for *Casualties: Unknown*â€”rollback brings back the same layer, terrain, and position. **Save & Exit** writes your run and returns to the main menu; your EXP dog does not die on exit. Timed auto-backups, manual slots, death rollback. Soft-compatible with QoL Unknown and KrokMP (host-driven rollback in multiplayer).
[b]
Installation[/b]

Install to:

`<GameFolder>/BepInEx/plugins/SaveManager/SaveManager.dll`

If you use a mod manager, create a `SaveManager` folder inside `plugins` and move this mod into it.
[b]
How to use[/b]

- Main menu: click **Save Manager** (panel is English / Chinese).
- **Saves**: snapshot the current run into slots; load or delete later.
- **Save & Exit**: save and quit to the menu without killing your EXP dog.
- **Rollback**: rewind to a recent slot; optional auto-rollback on death.
- **Settings**: timed backups, fixed-world engine (QoL or this mod), seeds, position mode, hotkeys.

CuHotbar merges its settings as an extra tab when SaveManager is installed.
[b]
Links[/b]

- GitHub: [[url=https://github.com/huanxin996/Cu-SaveManager]huanxin996/Cu-SaveManager[/url]]
- Details: see the GitHub README.

Name: Auto's Arsenal
Description:
[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]just adds some guns, nothing more, nothing less.[/color][/size]

[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
[color=#d4d4d8]installÂ [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/122]New Firearms[/url][/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]Install this[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]insert the contents of "Auto's-Arsenal.zip" into their respective folders in theÂ  "NewFirearms" folder[/color]
[color=#d4d4d8]done[/color]

[size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size]

Revolver: a six shooter that does a lot of damage, can fire pretty much as fast as you can click. uses .357 Magnum rounds

MP7: an SMG that fires rather fast, however doesn't deal much damage. uses small mags or SMG mags (From New Firearms).

M1918 Tankgewehr: Very heavy, one shot, huge damage. uses 13.2mm TuF rounds

Karabiner 98k: A good in-between of the Rifle and Anti-Tank Rifle, rather high amount of damage but needs to be racked manually. uses 5.56 rounds

AA-12: Fully automatic shotgun, a LOT of damage, a lot of ammo. uses Shotgun mags

[size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/122]New Firearms[/url]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/32]RshLib[/url]

[b][size=4]Shout-Outs[/size][/b]
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/profile/rusiber][b][size=3]rusiber[/size][/b][/url], for making this possible (Pre 2.0)
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/profile/7432547877542Al][b]RushellXYZ[/b][/url], for making this possible (post 2.0)
Author: AutogenMp4

Name: Guns Plus
Description:
[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8][size=3]This little mod introduces four new firearms that can be found in your struggles as a sort of complement to the vanilla firearms, filling in certain niches that I thought were either missing, or could be expanded upon. I made it mostly for myself, and as such are somewhat balanced in respect to their real-life counterparts.[/size][/color]

[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]First: Ensure you have BepInEx all sorted out. It's necessary for any mod here. To deal with this, download it from its repository [url=https://github.com/bepinex/bepinex]here[/url], extract it in your 'Casualties Unknown Demo' folder, and run the game once to let it generate all its associated folders and stuff.[/color]

[color=#D4D4D8]Second: Get RshLib, which is a prerequisite library that enables custom items, and the Gun Loader relies upon it. You may find it [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/32]here[/url], and just plop and extract this in the 'plugins' folder within the 'BepInEx' folder. [/color]

[color=#D4D4D8]Third: Get the New Gun Loader mod. You can find it [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/57?tab=description]here[/url], and just extract it in the same way as RshLib in the 'plugins' folder.[/color]

[color=#D4D4D8]Fourth: Download this mod and overwrite (or just add) its contents into the 'NewGun' folder by the Gun Loader mod.[/color]

[color=#D4D4D8]Fifth: Happy hunting![/color]

[size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size]
[b][color=#D4D4D8][size=3][u]Guns Featured:[/u][/size][/color][/b]

[list]
[*][color=#D4D4D8][size=3]Desert Tech MDRx Micron '[i]Interceptor[/i]': An exceptionally compact, yet powerful semi-automatic rifle that makes it a good all-rounder for any solo incursion. While this would be tiny in a regular human's hands, it fits perfectly for an expie to wield with little issue. Be sure to still consider its somewhat bulky nature however, as the polymer body does take up a good chunk of storage regardless.[/size][/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8][size=3]KRISS Vector '[i]Prototype[/i]' (Moniker shamelessly ripped from Gunsaw): A 9mm submachine gun with a lightweight stock and a unique design that lets it mitigate the emitted recoil significantly, so much so that you can use it one-handed without too much trouble. Its only limiting feature is that it shares its magazines with the 17-round pistol ones, which could fare a detriment in more 'prolonged' engagements.[/size][/color][/*]
[*][size=3]SIG MCX Raptor '[i]Rabid[/i]': A fast-firing automatic carbine, intended to just get as much lead out the barrel as fast as possible. While it's somewhat lightweight and packs quite a punch, its recoil is borderline uncontrollable under full blast even with the stock. It's still however, a great option if you happen to have way too much rifle ammunition sitting around that you want to burn through.[/size][/*]
[*][size=3]KelTec KSGÂ '[i]Oppressor[/i]': A notoriously unusual pump-action shotgun, with the distinct ability to house a whopping fifteen shells in a relatively tight gun, split across two barrels. This lets you fire for much longer, and more accurately thanks to the choked design of the shotgun. It is however, a bit too large for an expie to comfortably wield, and as such is cumbersome to both store and use.[/size][/*]
[/list]
[size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8][size=3]BepInEx[/size][/color]
[color=#D4D4D8][size=3]RshLib[/size][/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]New Gun Loader [/color]

[size=4][b]Shout outs[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]Big thanks to [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/profile/rusiber]rusiber[/url] who could make such gun mods possible and very easy to make.[/color]

Author: Angly

Name: Multiplayer Sprite Replacer
Description:
[color=#fafafa][size=4]Multiplayer Sprite Replacer[/size][/color]
This is a fork of [b]Player sprite replacer[/b] by d0gederp, specifically modified to be compatible with [b]Krokosha Casualties Unknown Multiplayer[/b].
[quote][b]Support & Feedback:[/b] For bug reports and test/latest builds, join our [url=https://discord.gg/EkgMFcPskE]Discord server[/url]. [i](I am more active on Discord!)[/i][/quote]Main Features
[list]
[*][b]Instant Sprite Switching:[/b] Press [b]Numpad 0 through 9[/b] to instantly switch between different character sprites in-game.[/*]
[*][b]Custom Folders:[/b] The mod looks for your sprites in folders ranging from
[code]CustomSprites/st0[/code]up to
[code]CustomSprites/st9[/code][/*]
[*][b]Automatic Skin Syncing:[/b] Skins automatically sync to all users running this mod (provided the host also has it installed). No manual uploads are requiredâ€”the mod streams your skin files directly to other players![/*]
[*][b]In-Game UI Manager:[/b] Use the newly added [b]MSR tab[/b] in the in-game settings menu to equip slots, reload skins, download remote skins via URL, and edit your keybinds.[/*]
[*][b]Live Auto-Refresh:[/b] The mod will automatically refresh your skin in-game the moment you alter the current slot's PNG files on your disk.[/*]
[*][b]Persistent Saving:[/b] Your last selected slot, recent URLs, downloaded skin URLs, and hidden/banned player settings are saved between sessions.[/*]
[/list]Requirements
You [b]MUST[/b] have the [url=https://github.com/Krokosha666/cas-unk-krokosha-multiplayer-coop]Krokosha Casualties Unknown Multiplayer[/url] mod installed in order to use this mod (though you are able to have the multiplayer mod itself disabled).
Installation Instructions
[b]If BepInEx is already installed:[/b]
[list=1]
[*]Open this repository's folder.[/*]
[*]Drag/copy its contents into your game's
[code]BepInEx/plugins[/code]folder.[/*]
[*]Start the game.[/*]
[/list][b]If BepInEx is not installed yet:[/b]
[list=1]
[*]Install BepInEx by following their [url=https://docs.bepinex.dev/articles/user_guide/installation/index.html]official instructions[/url].[/*]
[*]Once BepInEx is installed, follow the steps above.[/*]
[/list]Console Commands
Version 1.2.0 introduces the msr console command to give you granular control over your sprites and multiplayer experience:
[list]
[*]msr equip <slot>[size=2][font=Arial, Inter, "Inter Fallback", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif] Equips a local slot (e.g.,msr equip st1[/font][/size][size=2][font=Arial, Inter, "Inter Fallback", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif]).[/font][/size][/*]
[*]msr download <slot> <url>[b]:[/b] Downloads a remote skin to a specific slot (e.g. msr download st1 https://skin.cat-bot.de/d/423).[/*]
[*]msr reload[b][size=2][font=Arial, Inter, "Inter Fallback", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif]:[/font][/size][/b][size=2][font=Arial, Inter, "Inter Fallback", ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif] Reloads the current slot from your local disk.[/font][/size][/*]
[*]msr ban <username>[b]:[/b] Locally hides a specific player's custom skin.[/*]
[*]msr unban <username>[b]:[/b] Restores a previously hidden player's custom skin.[/*]
[*]msr bans[b]:[/b] Displays a list of currently banned player skins.[/*]
[*]msr verbose <true/false>[b]:[/b] Toggles verbose logging on or off for debugging purposes.[/*]
[/list]Finding Sprites
Looking for custom sprites? Check out these links containing custom sprite databases:
[list]
[*][url=https://skin.cat-bot.de/]Cat-Bot Skin Database[/url][/*]
[*][url=https://github.com/Paili-16/Scav-Prototype-The-Characters-Skins]Scav-Prototype Characters Skins GitHub[/url][/*]
[/list]Known Issues
[list]
[*]None! (for now.)[/*]
[/list]Changelog - Version 1.2.0
[list]
[*][b]Updated[/b] compatibility for Krokosha Multiplayer 4.0.0.[/*]
[*][b]Added[/b]
[code]st0[/code]skin slot support with a default
[code]Keypad0[/code]keybind.[/*]
[*][b]Added[/b] the
[code]msr[/code]console command structure.[/*]
[*][b]Added[/b]
[code]msr equip <slot>[/code]to equip a local slot such as
[code]st1[/code][/*]
[*][b]Added[/b]
[code]msr download <slot> <url>[/code]for remote skins.[/*]
[*][b]Added[/b]
[code]msr reload[/code]to reload the current slot from disk.[/*]
[*][b]Added[/b]
[code]msr ban <username>[/code]and
[code]msr unban <username>[/code]to locally hide or restore another player's custom skin.[/*]
[*][b]Added[/b]
[code]msr bans[/code][code]msr list[/code]and
[code]msr verbose <true|false>[/code][/*]
[*][b]Added[/b] an MSR tab to the in-game settings menu.[/*]
[*][b]Added[/b] slot equip, reload, and URL download controls in the settings menu.[/*]
[*][b]Added[/b] a settings-menu URL textbox for remote skin downloads.[/*]
[*][b]Added[/b] in-game keybind editing for
[code]st0[/code]through
[code]st9[/code][/*]
[*][b]Added[/b] automatic refresh when the current slot's PNG files change on disk.[/*]
[*][b]Added[/b] persistent saving for the last selected slot, downloaded slot URLs, recent URLs, and banned players.[/*]
[*][b]Added[/b] a verbose logging setting for debugging.[/*]
[*][b]Fixed[/b] an issue where non-MSR players would sometimes receive mismatched
[code]st1[/code]limb sprites.[/*]
[/list]Credits & Shout outs
[list]
[*][b]d0gederp[/b] for creating the original mod! [i](Note: All donation points go to d0gederp. If d0gederp requests that this mod be taken down, I will do so immediately).[/i][/*]
[/list]

Name: Item Spawner
Description:
[quote][b]Github: [url=https://github.com/MAakber/ItemSpawner/releases/tag/v1]Github[/url][/b][/quote]
[b][color=#ff7700]Description[/color][/b]

Item Spawner is an in-game item spawning plugin for Casualties Unknown Demo V6.1.

It adds a draggable item spawner panel that lets you search registered game items, filter by category or favorites, and spawn items with configurable quantity, durability, and spawn mode.

This mod is intended for local testing, debugging, sandbox play, and single-player convenience.

[b][color=#ff7700]Compatibility[/color][/b]

Tested only on Casualties Unknown Demo V6.1.

Compatibility with other game versions is unknown.

[b][color=#ff7700]Installation instructions[/color][/b]

1. Install BepInEx for the game.
2. Download the mod archive.
3. Extract the archive into the game root folder.
4. Confirm the DLL is located at:
[code]BepInEx/plugins/ItemSpawner/ItemSpawner.build.dll[/code]5. Launch the game.
6. Press `LeftControl + I` to open or close the panel.

[b][color=#ff7700]Main features[/color][/b]

[list]
[*]Â Search and spawn registered in-game items.[/*]
[*]Â Category filter.[/*]
[*]Â Favorites filter.[/*]
[*]Â Persistent favorites.[/*]
[*]Â ä¸­æ–‡ / English UI.[/*]
[*]Â Draggable and scalable panel.[/*]
[*]Â Passthrough mode, allowing player controls while the panel is open.[/*]
[*]Â Native item tooltip when hovering item icons.[/*]
[*]Â Spawn to inventory, at player position, or at mouse position.[/*]
[/list]
[b][color=#ff7700]Requirements[/color][/b]

[list]
[*]Â Casualties Unknown Demo V6.1[/*]
[*]Â BepInEx installed for the game[/*]
[/list]
[b]Â Shout outs[/b]

Thanks to the Casualties Unknown Demo community and BepInEx modding ecosystem.
Author: Aakber

=======================
Some unbalanced items have been added. The list is as follows:
galaxybag: Super Galaxy Backpack, with a weight of 0 and a capacity of 9999, a maximum single-item limit of 9999, and a negative load-bearing coefficient. It cannot be equipped.
galaxybelt: Super Galaxy Belt, with the same attributes as the Galaxy Backpack, but it can be equipped. The equipment is located in the same position as the original transparent equipment.
galaxylegpouch: Super Galaxy Leg Backpack, with the same attributes as the Galaxy Backpack, but it can be equipped. The equipment is located in the same position as the original transparent equipment.
galaxymaterialpouch: Super Galaxy Material Backpack, with the same attributes as the Galaxy Backpack, but it can be equipped. The equipment is located in the same position as the original transparent equipment.
galaxypack: Super Galaxy Duffle Bag, with the same attributes as the Galaxy Backpack, but it can be equipped. The equipment is located in the same position as the original transparent equipment.
galaxycanteen: A water bottle with a capacity of 10000ml, which can be consumed. It can be consumed in one 100ml portion.
galaxyjug: A water bottle with a capacity of 5000ml, which can be consumed. It can be consumed in one 100ml portion and the liquid inside can be seen.
galaxybloodbag: Like the water bottle, it can now input liquid. The maximum speed is 100ml/s.
galaxylaserdrill: 30-meter range, 100 damage, a laser excavation tool that requires a medium-sized battery.
galaxybandage: Can wrap around 1000 times and simultaneously restore skin, muscles, bleeding, fractures, and sprains.
galaxymultitool: A powerful and terrifying multi-functional toolkit that can be used for hammering, cutting, igniting, heating... etc. It can also automatically recharge and repair. With it, you can discard all other processing tools.
Galaxy Waterball: Edible water ball, +50 water content, +5 satiety
Galaxynutrientbar: Nutritional bar, +50 satiety

[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
0. [b]If you have previously installed my mod, please delete the old version files before updating.[/b]
1. Download and add RshLib.dll to BepInEx/Plugin
2. Download andÂ Extract the files to BepInEx/Plugin

ex.If you want to add an Disabled items, you can move it to the "items" folder.
You can obtain additional information about the items in the Item list.
[size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
BepInEx
RshLib
ICEnecoCustomItemAPI

[b][color=#fafafa][size=4]Future Goals[/size][/color][/b]
[list=1]
[*][b]Refine the underlying system[/b]Â â€“ eventually make it a standalone item-creation API that connects withÂ [b]RshLib[/b]Â (probably rename it by then).[/*]
[*][b]Build a small program for custom items[/b]Â â€“ so you won't need to write JSON manually.[/*]
[*][b]Integrate with MossLib for localization[/b]Â â€“ once that's done, I'll provide proper localizations.[/*]
[/list]
Author: ICEneco

Name: Killer's Guns
Description:
[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
[color=#d4d4d8]Adds a handful of guns, using sprites I made for a ss14 fork called FarHorizons (SS14 sprites and Casualties Unknown's are, interestingly, very compatible with eachother)[/color]

Mostly a personal mod.
[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]Install New Gun Loader [/color]
Install this mod
Put the contents of Killer_Guns.zip into the NewGun folder
Enjoy :D
[size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size]
[color=#d4d4d8]This mod adds

Shotguns -
- Sentinel (24 round semiautomatic, has a 0.5s delay between shots)
The SS14 version of this is mag-fed but pump action. As funny as this would be, no shotgun magazines so you're spared.. for now.

- Combat Shotgun (12 round semiautomatic)
Holds more, with more recoil.

- Krammer5001 (8 round semiautomatic, low recoil)
Better shotgun, not much to say.

LMGs -
- M429 (Manually loaded like a shotgun, 100 rifle rounds. Does a bit more damage than a rifle.)
I would have LOVED to give this thing magazines, but, making items by json isn't a thing. Yet. Best I could do.

Rifles -
- Lecter Type-T (Low recoil)
Just a better rifle. Added stock makes the gun actually controllable.

- Lecter Type-F (Low recoil, higher damage than a rifle. 0.5s delay between shots)
An advanced Lecter, same concept. Shoots slower though.

Pistols -
- P91 (Machine pistol, extremely fast RoF but almost no accuracy)
Hold down left trigger and watch the utter obliteration of something two feet in front of you.

- N1984 (.44 Magnum, extremely high damage/recoil, uses pistol magazines and has a 0.5s delay between shots)[/color]
Solid sidearm if you can find it. Salad doesn't stand a chance against someone who can aim.

Railguns -Â 
- N2461 (Pistol / 2k damage per shot / High recoil / 1s delay between shots / Loud)
Just a good sidearm. Will deafen you.

- IFV-2461 (Rifle / 6k damage per shot / Ridiculous recoil / 3s delay between shots / Loud / Heavy)
The recoil generated by this weapon is high enough to perform gun-jumps, but the game is weird about it. If you aim too far up you will be launched upwards, and if you aim too far down you will be crushed into the ground. Consider this your warning before you accidentally end your run. Do gun-jumps at an angle.

Originally was going to use shotgun rounds to round out advanced weapons, but then I couldn't do magazine states which is part of the appeal of me doing high-tech guns.Â

[color=#ff7700][size=5][b]Feature List:[/b][/size][/color]
Steam Lobbies
Real voice chat with in-game effects.
Middle Mouse Button to point at a location.
Right click to interact and heal other players.
Stay close to others to share body temperature.
Spectator mode when dead.
Sleeping is disabled.
Hunger and thirst is slowed down.
Carrying incapacitated players.
Unchipped option is individual.
Most of these features can be tweaked in the in-game console.
Also this mod can run as a dedicated server. (its not real headless)

There's no client prediction.
There's no network ownership.
There's no deterministic RNG.
There's no anticheat.
This mod only adds a simple co-op experience to the game.

Â  Selfharm cooldown set to 120 if was below

Name: Comprehensive revision
Description:
Description
æœ¬ DLL è¡¥ä¸æ·±åº¦é‡å†™äº†ã€ŠCasualties Unknownã€‹çš„æŠ€èƒ½ä¸Žç”Ÿå­˜ç³»ç»Ÿã€‚åŽŸç‰ˆæ¸¸æˆæŠ€èƒ½è¶…è¿‡ 31 çº§æˆ–æ›´é«˜çš„ç­‰çº§ä¼šå¯¼è‡´ç»éªŒå€¼æº¢å‡ºå¹¶å¡æ­»ï¼Œè§’è‰²æˆé•¿ä¸Šé™ä½Žï¼Œå¤§é‡å±žæ€§ï¼ˆå¦‚æ™ºåŠ›ï¼‰ç¼ºä¹å­˜åœ¨æ„Ÿã€‚æ­¤æ¨¡ç»„å½»åº•ä¿®å¤æº¢å‡ºé—®é¢˜ï¼Œå°†æ‰€æœ‰å±žæ€§ä¸Šé™å®‰å…¨æ‰©å±•è‡³ 1000 çº§ï¼Œå¹¶ä¸ºåŠ›é‡ã€éŸ§æ€§ã€æ™ºåŠ›æ–°å¢žäº†å…¨é¢ä¸”ç¬¦åˆç›´è§‰çš„åŠ æˆâ€”â€”é«˜åŠ›é‡èƒ½å¼ºåŒ–è£…å¤‡æ•ˆæžœï¼Œé«˜éŸ§æ€§èŽ·å¾—æ›´å¼ºæ¢å¤å’Œé˜²å¾¡ï¼Œé«˜æ™ºåŠ›åˆ™åŠ é€Ÿå…¨å±€ç»éªŒèŽ·å–ã€‚è§’è‰²å°†éšç€ç­‰çº§æå‡æ„Ÿå—åˆ°çœŸæ­£çš„æ•°å€¼ç¢¾åŽ‹ã€‚
è¯¦ç»†æ”¹åŠ¨åˆ—è¡¨å·²éšé™„åœ¨ä¸‹è½½æ–‡ä»¶ä¸­ã€‚

Description
This DLL patch deeply rewrites the skill and survival systems of Casualties Unknown. In the original game, skills exceeding level 31 or higher cause experience overflow and freeze, with a low growth ceiling and many attributes (like Intelligence) lacking presence. This mod completely fixes the overflow issue, safely extending all stat caps to level 1000, and adds comprehensive, intuitive bonuses for Strength, Resilience, and Intelligence â€“ high Strength enhances equipment effectiveness, high Resilience grants stronger recovery and defense, and high Intelligence accelerates global experience gain. As you level up, you will truly feel the numerical domination.
A detailed change log is included with the download.

Installation instructions

Main features
- Break through the level 30+ experience limit: all skill caps extended to level 1000, experience no longer overflows, goodbye freeze bug.
- Comprehensive skill effect rework:
Â  - Strength: boosts equipment armor efficiency.
Â  - Resilience: improves blood clotting, blood regeneration, wound healing, body temperature stability, consciousness recovery, and almost all other bodily recovery abilities, while also providing unarmored damage reduction.
Â  - Intelligence: exponentially accelerates all experience gain based on level, making Intelligence a true core growth stat.
- Fixed official overflow bugs: negative pain values and self-damage from recoil have been corrected.
- Minimal invasive changes: only formula values were modified; the core game architecture remains untouched.
- Supports existing save files, no new game required. (Probably?)

Name: New Gun Loader (super beta)
Description:
[list]
[*][color=#666666][size=5]ve[/size][/color][color=#999999][size=5]r[/size][/color][color=#b6b6b6][size=5]s[/size][/color][color=#cccccc][size=5]i[/size][/color][color=#efefef][size=5]o[/size][/color][color=#ffffff][size=5]n[/size][/color] [color=#6aa84f][size=5]2.0.0[/size][/color][/*]
[/list]Major update: the mod has been rewritten for the current game version (7.0.1) and the new RshLib 3.0.0.
Compatibility
[list]
[*]Full rewrite for the new RshLib 3.0.0 API. Previous builds no longer loaded due to changes in how items are registered â€” this is now fixed.[/*]
[*]Verified against all current in-game weapon parameters.[/*]
[/list]Multiplayer (KrokMP)
[list]
[*]Custom guns now sync correctly in multiplayer â€” weapons are visible and functional for all players.[/*]
[*]Note: every player in the session must have the mod and identical gun configs installed.[/*]
[/list]Fire sound
[list]
[*]Fixed the long-standing issue â€” custom fire sounds now fully work. The sound plays natively on every shot (correct volume, positioning and fire rate).[/*]
[*]Added optional rack/unrack sounds:Â 
[code]rackSound[/code]Â andÂ 
[code]unrackSound[/code].[/*]
[*]Reworked WAV loader â€” files with non-standard headers are now supported.[/*]
[/list]New config options (all optional)
[list]
[*][code]baseItem[/code]Â â€” base prefab the item is built from.[/*]
[*][code]tags[/code]Â â€” item tags.[/*]
[*][code]slotRotation[/code]Â â€” sprite rotation in the inventory slot.[/*]
[*][code]muzzle[/code]Â â€” muzzle flash:Â 
[code]pistol[/code],Â 
[code]shotgun[/code]Â orÂ 
[code]none[/code].[/*]
[*][code]rackSound[/code]Â /Â 
[code]unrackSound[/code]Â â€” custom bolt sounds.[/*]
[*][code]startChambered[/code]Â â€” round in the chamber on spawn.[/*]
[*][code]startWithMag[/code]Â â€” spawn with a magazine.[/*]
[*][code]startRounds[/code]Â â€” rounds in the magazine on spawn.[/*]
[*][code]startSafe[/code]Â â€” spawn with the safety on.[/*]
[/list]Backwards compatibility
[list]
[*]Existing configs work unchanged â€” all new fields are optional.[/*]
[/list]

[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]New Gun Loader (Beta). Adds custom guns via subfolders and JSON config files. No coding required. Already includes the FN SCAR assault rifle. Custom fire sounds are currently not supported and will be fixed in a future update. [/color]

[img]https://i.ibb.co/YBgt82c1/Video-Project-5.gif[/img]

[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
[list=1]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Install BepInEx 5 and RshLib.[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Extract the mod into BepInEx/plugins.[/color][/*]
[/list][size=4][b]Adding a new gun:
[/b][/size]
[list=1]
[*][size=4]Go to BepInEx/plugins/NewGun/ and create a new folder (e.g., MyPistol).[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]Place four PNG sprites inside (weapon must face RIGHT):[/size]

[list]
[*][size=4]normal.png (default view, magazine in, bolt closed)[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]racked.png (bolt open after firing)[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]normal_nomag.png (magazine removed, bolt closed)[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]racked_nomag.png (magazine removed, bolt open)[/size][/*]
[/list][/*]
[*][size=4]Create config.json in the same folder. Copy the example from the FN_SCAR folder and edit values.[/size][/*]
[*][size=4]Launch the game, open console, type: spawnÂ my_weapon cursor[/size][/*]
[/list][size=4][b]How to use config.json[/b][/size]

[code]{
Â  "itemId": "unique item ID (e.g., myscar.scar)",
Â  "fullName": "displayed weapon name in-game",
Â  "description": "in-game description text",
Â  "category": "inventory category (usually 'utility')",
Â  "weight": "item weight",
Â  "value": "trader value (integer)",
Â  "recognition": "stealth rating (higher = less visible)",
Â  "ammoType": "ammo type: Pistol, Rifle, Shotgun",
Â  "firingMode": "fire mode: Auto, SemiAuto, Pump",
Â  "feedType": "feed type: Mag, Direct, revolver",
Â  "magCapacity": "magazine capacity (rounds)",
Â  "knockBack": "recoil strength",
Â  "structureDamage": "damage to structures",
Â  "animalDamage": "damage to creatures",
Â  "loudness": "gunshot loudness",
Â  "desiredGasTime": "time between shots in seconds",
Â  "shotsPerFire": "projectiles per shot (e.g., 8 for shotgun)",
Â  "verticalSpread": "bullet spread",
Â  "conditionLossPerShot": "condition loss per shot (0 to 1)",
Â  "pixelsPerUnit": "sprite scale (higher = smaller in hand)",
Â  "fireSound": "custom fire sound file name without extension (currently unsupported)"
}[/code][size=4]
[b]Main features[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Add custom guns without programming.[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Four sprite states: normal, racked, no magazine, racked no magazine.[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]All weapon stats configured via JSON.[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Supports Pistol, Rifle, Shotgun ammo types.[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Auto, SemiAuto, and Pump firing modes.[/color][/*]
[/list][size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]BepInEx 5, RshLib. [/color]
Author: rusiber

Name: Player Sprite Replacer _Numpad_
Description:
[center][quote][b][size=4]ORIGINAL MOD -Â [/size][/b][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/15?tab=description][b][size=4]https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/15?tab=description[/size][/b][/url][/quote][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/15?tab=description]
[/url]
[left][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/15?tab=description][/url][/left]
[left][/left]
[/center]
[left][size=4][b]Description[/b][/size][/left]
[color=#D4D4D8]This is a modified fork of the original Player Sprite Replacer mod by Karlo PoljanÄiÄ‡ (dogederp), distributed under the MIT License.

You do NOT need the original mod. This version works completely standalone â€“ just install it as described and it will function on its own.

The original mod allowed replacing the player sprite by manually swapping files. This version adds the ability to switch between multiple custom sprites directly while playing, using the NumPad keys 1 through 9 â€“ no need to leave the game.

HOW IT WORKS:
Inside the CustomSprites folder, there are subfolders named st1, st2, ... st9. Each of these folders contains two subfolders: "head" and "body", which hold the corresponding sprite parts for that slot. The mod comes with pre-loaded sprites in these folders so you can test the functionality immediately. You can replace the contents of st1â€“st9 with your own sprites as long as you keep the head/body structure.

The code edits were done with the help of an AI neural network. I have no experience in C# programming, so the implementation may not be perfect, but it works. All credit for the core functionality and original assets belongs to Karlo PoljanÄiÄ‡.

NOTE: If you don't have a NumPad but really want to use the mod, I can make a separate version with different key bindings. Let me know in the comments.[/color]

[size=4][b]Media
[img]https://i.ibb.co/PvzjkW0B/Video-Project-3.gif[/img]
[youtube]tRlZNJFllXo[/youtube]
Installation instructions [/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]1. Make sure BepInEx is installed for Casualties Unknown Demo.
2. Extract the contents of the ZIP archive into the folder:
Â  â€¦\steamapps\common\Casualties Unknown Demo\BepInEx\plugins
3. The folder structure should look like:[/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]
[i]Â  BepInEx\plugins\body_sprite_replacer.dll
Â  BepInEx\plugins\CustomSprites\[/i][/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]
Â  Â  Â  â”œâ”€â”€ st1\
Â  Â  Â  â”‚Â  â”œâ”€â”€ head\
Â  Â  Â  â”‚Â  â””â”€â”€ body\
Â  Â  Â  â”œâ”€â”€ st2\
Â  Â  Â  â”‚Â  â”œâ”€â”€ head\
Â  Â  Â  â”‚Â  â””â”€â”€ body\
Â  Â  Â  â””â”€â”€ ... (up to st9)
4. The mod already includes test sprites inside st1â€“st9, so you can try it out right away.
5. Launch the game.
6. While playing, press NumPad keys 1 through 9 to switch between the sprite sets instantly.[/color]

[size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8][b]- Fully standalone â€“ does not require the original mod to be installed.[/b][/color]
- Switch between 9 different sprite sets in real time using NumPad 1-9 keys.
- Each set is stored in a dedicated folder (st1 to st9) with "head" and "body" subfolders.
- Pre-loaded with test sprites so you can see the mod in action immediately.
- Easy customisation: just replace the images inside st1â€“st9/head and body with your own.
- No need to replace files manually or restart the game.
- Preserves the original sprite replacer functionality.
- Works with the Casualties Unknown Demo (Scav Prototype).
- If you lack a NumPad, a custom keybinding version can be provided upon request (ask in comments).

[size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]- Casualties Unknown Demo (Scav Prototype) installed via Steam.
- BepInEx mod loader configured for the game.
- (No other mods are required.)[/color]

[size=4][b]Shout outs[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]Thanks to Karlo PoljanÄiÄ‡ (dogederp) for creating the original Player Sprite Replacer mod and releasing it under the MIT License, which allowed me to make this modified version.

Also thanks to the AI tools that helped me edit the code despite my lack of C# knowledge.

Original game assets by Orsoniks.[/color]

[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]

[color=#D4D4D8]Use in inventory yo get a cigarret[/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]Then use cigarret to lit it and put it in your mouth slot[/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]Detailed description:[/color]
[spoiler][color=#D4D4D8]You must have a lighter in inventory or a campfire nearby to lit it
One cigarrete lasts 50 sec noramly
25 sec in rain
or 1 sec if wet (wet decays even if unlit)

The effects of 50 sec are following:
Â 2.4 mood
Â 3.5 sickness (actually 6.5 sickness, but you reduce 3 sickness in 50 sec)
Â 1.8 hemothorax (actually 4.8 hemothorax, for comparasion you see moodle at 40)
Â Unable to harm yourself while smoking
Â Decreases heart rate and gives adrenaline in some amount[/color][/spoiler]

MP Status: Compatible, both client and host must have it


[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
If you mod your game for first time (dont have BepInEx):
[spoiler]Go to BepInEx download page
https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases/tag/v
And download BepInEx_win_x64_5.4.23.5
Open steam, choose browse local file
Extract BepInEx contents into game folder's root (winhttp.dll should be in same folder as executable)
Open game once and close
Now folderÂ BepInEx/plugins should be created

Additional instruction for Linux users:
Â - AddÂ  `WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp=n,b"Â  %command%` into launch arguments[/spoiler]
Download and extractÂ into BepInEx/plugins RshLib.2.1.5 (if you haven't before)
Â  You can verify that RshLib loaded by seeing "This is a modded beta build" in top
Download `Cigarrets` and extractÂ into BepInEx/plugins

Name: Project Mechanism
Description:
[center][size=5][b]Mechanism Prototype Preview 1.0.8[/b][/size]
[i]A hostile mechanism preview build for Casualties: Unknown[/i][/center]

[size=4][b]Overview[/b][/size]

Mechanism Prototype Preview is an early BepInEx prototype mod for [i]Casualties: Unknown[/i].

It turns your Sawian into a hostile mechanism unit equipped with experimental built-in weapons, protocol tools, diagnostic UI changes, and prototype target/NPC systems. This is not a finished alternate campaign or polished gameplay overhaul yet. It is a preview build meant for footage, testing, and feedback on the core weapon/mechanism concept.

[size=4][b]Current Features[/b][/size]

[b]Mechanism Weapon Selector[/b]
The mechanism has its own built-in weapon selector separate from normal held items. Scroll the mouse wheel to select a mechanism weapon, then use left click to fire, charge, aim, or activate the selected system.

[b]Protocol Laser[/b]
Protocol Laser is a charge weapon with multiple release levels. Select Protocol Laser with the mouse wheel, then hold left click to charge. A quick tap fires a small finger shot; a longer hold releases a sustained palm beam; a full charge releases a much larger two-hand termination beam. Larger beams can keep applying damage while sustained, push through bodies, tear into blocks/buildings, and apply heavier trauma or dismember pressure.

[b]Beam Clash[/b]
Beam Clash is a debug-target interaction for testing opposing Protocol Laser beams. Spawn a debug target with
[code]mechanismbody cursor[/code], then use
[code]mechanismbody clash[/code]or the beam commands to make it fire back. If both beams meet, they can lock into a shared clash point with camera framing and beam-struggle audio. Spam click during the clash to push harder and overpower the opposing beam.

[b]Protocol Scan[/b]
Protocol Scan identifies debug targets before the full protocol flow. Select Protocol Scan with the mouse wheel and left click a target, or use
[code]mechanismbody scan[/code]for testing. Scan tells the mechanism whether a target is chipped, unchipped, unverified, cleared, or valid for protocol escalation.

[b]Protocol Command / Compliance[/b]
Protocol Command lets you order a scanned target to comply instead of immediately executing it. Select Protocol Command and left click after a successful scan, or use
[code]mechanismbody command[/code]. Depending on trust, fear, suspicion, pain, distance, and chip status, the target can submit, resist, flee, panic, become cleared, or become a valid protocol target.

[b]Protocol Evidence HUD[/b]
A diagnostic protocol panel shows scan readiness, aim/range failures, chip verification, trust-floor checks, and target behavior.

[b]Protocol Scan Pulses / Laser Legality Colors[/b]
Scan success/refusal produces a small colored world-space pulse, and Protocol Laser visuals distinguish unverified, cleared, and valid protocol targets.

[b]Head Turret[/b]
Head Turret fires fast head-mounted shots without needing a held gun. Select Head Turret with the mouse wheel and left click, or press [b]G[/b] for the direct debug key.

[b]Sound Cannon[/b]
Sound Cannon is a charge-and-release sonic blast. Select Sound Cannon and hold/release left click, or press [b]H[/b] for the direct debug key. It is a forceful area hit rather than a precise projectile, with pressure-wave distortion and color separation.

[b]Spike Hand[/b]
Spike Hand extends a blade from your arm. Select Spike Hand and hold/release left click, or press [b]J[/b] for the direct debug key. Stab a wall to pin yourself there until you release, which can be used to hang from surfaces or stop movement. Stab a body to impale it and keep it attached to the blade until click/release. While a target is pinned, keep aiming the arm to drag, lift, or turn the body with the spike.

[b]Rocket Hand[/b]
Rocket Hand fires an explosive rocket from the mechanism arm. Select Rocket Hand, hold left click to raise the arm and project a red laser aim line, then release to fire. Rockets travel at medium speed, leave a trail, explode on impact or timeout, damage bodies/buildings/blocks, and shove ragdolls with the blast.

[b]Offhand Grapple[/b]
Offhand Grapple fires a hook from your secondary arm. Select Grapple and left click, or press [b]K[/b] for the direct debug key. Hold after latching to pull yourself toward the latch point or drag against it, then release to retract.

[b]Jump Pad Punch[/b]
Jump Pad Punch is a launch-force melee hit. Select Jump Pad Punch and left click, or press [b]L[/b] for the direct debug key. It can throw you or targets upward and can sometimes trigger Black Flash.

[b]Black Flash[/b]
Black Flash is a high-impact strike with a brief impact-frame effect. Press [b]E[/b] for a guaranteed Black Flash punch. Jump Pad Punch also has a 1-in-6 chance to trigger it.

[b]Perception Module[/b]
Perception Module highlights threats and adds danger projections. Use
[code]mechanismperception 0[/code]through
[code]mechanismperception 3[/code]to change levels. At higher levels the mechanism can respond to incoming hazards more aggressively, including ducking or planking under horizontal turret fire and using head-turret recoil as an airborne emergency dodge when available.

[b]Mechanism Health Panel[/b]
The player identity and several stat labels have been adjusted toward the mechanism theme. The Install Bay/loadout work is where experimental built-in systems are being organized, and the health panel now uses a chip-style mechanism button.

[b]Debug Target Body[/b]
Debug Target Body spawns a passive cloned test body for demonstrating weapons, protocol systems, beam clashes, ragdoll recovery, speech reactions, and targetable limbs. Use
[code]mechanismbody cursor[/code]to spawn one at the cursor, or
[code]mechanismbody[/code]to remove spawned debug bodies.

[b]Gunsaw Sawian Test Bodies[/b]
Gunsaw Sawian Test Bodies are experimental Gunsaw-style debug bodies for visual and combat testing. Use commands such as
[code]gunsawbody base cursor[/code],
[code]gunsawbody all cursor[/code], or
[code]gunsawbody roza cursor armed[/code]. Some have imported sprites, tails, blood colors, and weapon profiles, making them useful for testing impales, rockets, sonic blasts, lasers, and ragdoll behavior.

[b]NPC 9mm Handling[/b]
NPC 9mm Handling lets debug bodies be armed for combat testing without inheriting normal player control. Use the armed Gunsaw/debug body commands or related mechanismbody testing commands to stage weapon interactions.

[b]Testing Tools[/b]
Includes commands for god mode, Gunsaw visual debugging, perception levels, protocol testing, heavy diagnostics, health UI tuning, headless perception tests, Gunsaw runtime prefab tests, and navigation descent self-tests. Most of the prototype's best scenes come from spawning a body, arming it, then combining scan/command/beam clash/spike/rocket/sound cannon interactions.

[size=4][b]Basic Controls[/b][/size]

[list]
[*]Scroll Wheel - select mechanism weapon[/*]
[*]Left Click - fire/use selected weapon[/*]
[*]E - guaranteed Black Flash[/*]
[/list]
[b]Direct debug keys still work for quick testing:[/b]

[list]
[*]G - Head Turret[/*]
[*]H - Sound Cannon[/*]
[*]J - Spike Hand[/*]
[*]K - Offhand Grapple[/*]
[*]L - Jump Pad Punch[/*]
[/list]
Mod keybinds are ignored while the in-game console is open.

[size=4][b]Console Commands[/b][/size]

[b]Spawn or remove the main debug target body:[/b]
[code]mechanismbody cursor[/code]
[b]Run without cursor to remove spawned debug bodies:[/b]
[code]mechanismbody[/code]
[b]Show or hide simple debug target thoughts:[/b]
[code]mechanismbody thoughts on
mechanismbody thoughts off[/code]
[b]Beam clash testing:[/b]
[code]mechanismbody clash
mechanismbody beam
mechanismbody beam big[/code]
[b]Protocol testing:[/b]
[code]mechanismbody scan
mechanismbody command
mechanismbody execute
mechanismbody status
mechanismbody chipped
mechanismbody unchipped
mechanismbody resetprotocol[/code]
[b]Heavy debug diagnostics:[/b]
[code]mechanismbody debug
mechanismbody debug on
mechanismbody debug off[/code]
[b]Perception module:[/b]
[code]mechanismperception 0
mechanismperception 1
mechanismperception 2
mechanismperception 3
mechanismperception status
mechanismperception debug[/code]
[b]Gunsaw-style debug bodies:[/b]
[code]gunsawbody base cursor
gunsawbody all cursor
gunsawbody roza cursor armed[/code]
[b]Gunsaw visual debugging:[/b]
[code]gunsawdebug dump
gunsawdebug tail
gunsawdebug rig on
gunsawdebug rig off
gunsawdebug autofit on
gunsawdebug autofit off[/code]
[b]Testing god mode:[/b]
[code]mechanismgod on
mechanismgod off
mechanismgod status[/code]
[size=4][b]Installation[/b][/size]

This mod requires BepInEx 5.

Extract the archive into your game folder so that the DLL ends up here:

[code]BepInEx/plugins/HeadTurret.dll[/code]
The included weapon sounds should end up here:

[code]BepInEx/plugins/HeadTurretSounds/[/code]
The included Gunsaw/Sawian visual assets should end up here:

[code]CasualtiesUnknownMods/Assets/GunsawExtracted/Sprites/[/code]
[size=4][b]Important Notes[/b][/size]

This is a prototype preview build, not a polished final release.

Expect rough edges, debug behavior, balance issues, unfinished systems, and possible weirdness. It is being shared for footage, testing, and feedback on the core weapon/mechanism concept.
Author: Sebastian

Name: Configurable Casualties
Description:
[size=6]WORKS ON 7.0[/size]
[size=4]should be compatible with coop but its preferrable that you and the one you are playing with have the same configs[/size]

[size=4]Makes the game much easier (or harder, if you want to make it so) without the need to constantly type console commands, aswell as includes some personal changes to the game[/size]
[size=4]includes a GUI for everything, heres the overview:[/size]

[list]
[*]ability to boost and lower health regen (multipliers for skin/muscle healing, the amount of blood you lose and the speed at which bleeding clots, grow limbs back over time, disable concussion or brain damage and MUCH, MUCH more.)[/*]
[*]ability to edit body parts that certain wearable armor covers, aswell as the amount of protection/insulation they give and the amount of damage they take[/*]
[*]ability to change the amount of hp a limb has, its damage resist, how fast is the bandaging on it and etc[/*]
[*]ability to make certain items regenerate their durability, basically making them last indefinitely, highly configurable which means you can make dressings and one-use items regen over time, such as crystals[/*]
[*]dirtyness now decays over time without the need to dip yourself in water or use a pod shower (can be disabled)[/*]
[*]ability to control the amount of wetness that decays over time[/*]
[*]ability to control body temperature and default it to the value you want, preventing hyperthermia/hypothermia[/*]
[*]ability to control immunity positive/negative values to either buff or debuff it[/*]
[*]ability to control mood and its gain[/*]
[*]ability to control liquids and what they do (you can make water work like morphine when injected and like oil when drank, highly customizable)[/*]
[*]ability to control items and their mechanics[/*]
[*]ability to control body metabolism[/*]
[/list]to change the values, press F9 in game, by default values are are unchanged

installation: install BepInEx into the game's main folder (i use the steam release), run the game once after installing it and then drop the contents of ConfigurableCasualties folder into the game's folder (where the .exe is)
