if things dont work list them in the comments[/size][/b]

[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]If you mod your game for first time (dont have BepInEx):[/color]
[spoiler][color=#D4D4D8]Go to BepInEx download page
https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases/tag/v
And download BepInEx_win_x64_5.4.23.5
Open steam, choose browse local file
Extract BepInEx contents into game folder's root (winhttp.dll should be in same folder as executable)
Open game once and close
Now folderÂ BepInEx/plugins should be created

Additional instruction for Linux users:
Â - AddÂ  `WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp=n,b"Â  %command%` into launch arguments[/color][/spoiler]
[color=#D4D4D8]Download and extractÂ into BepInEx/plugins RshLib.3.0.0 (if you haven't before)[/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]Â  You can verify that RshLib loaded by seeing "This is a modded beta build" in top[/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]Download `NewClothing` and extractÂ into BepInEx/plugins[/color]
It's also recomended to downloadÂ [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/194]Wearable Array[/url]Â too, it prevents wearables from going-off screen



[size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size]
Adds:
Â - Shirt (different from torn shirt)
Â - T-Shirt
Â - Crop shirt
Â - Crop t-shirt
Â - Thigh highs
Â - Jeans (have a little pocket store things)
Â - ShortsÂ (have a little pocket store things)
Â - Socks
Â - Boots
Â - Long shirt

You can spawn them in any color, just add $<HEX CODE OF COLOR> In the end
Like this: spawn armwarmers$F09000

You also can add your own clothing by .json edits!
Doc:
[spoiler]{
Â  Â  "baseItem": "string", // Advanced only, enter only vanilla items here, remove if you're unsure on what you are doing
Â  Â  "notWearable": false, // i guess we are doing items now
Â  Â  "notPaintable": false, // Allows usage of FF00FF
Â  Â  "limbs": [ "string", ], // All limbs beside "desiredWearLimb" affected by clothing
Â  Â  "fullname": "string", // Displayed name
Â  Â  "description": "string", // Displayed description
Â  Â  "category": "string", // "utility" droppable as utility item, "custom" only traders, "unobtainable" only console commands
Â  Â  "decayMinutes": 0.0, // Set to 0 to not decay
Â  Â  "scaleWeight": false,
Â  Â  "weight": 0.0,
Â  Â  "desiredWearLimb": "string", // Main limb it worn on, dismembrent bans wearing. If not wearable only defines file name
Â  Â  "wearSlotId": "string", // Player cant put on 2 wearables with same slot id
Â  Â  "wearableIsolation": 0.0, // Isolation from temperatures provided by wearable, multiply by 100 to get in %. Doesn't affected by "limbs"
Â  Â  "wearableArmor": 0.0, // Armor provided to "desiredWearLimb" and "limbs", multiply by 100 to get in %
Â  Â  "wearableHitDurabilityLossMultiplier": 0.0, // Multiplier of damage wearable takes
Â  Â  "wearableVisualOffset": 0, // Lower values makes wearable be visually putted below ones with highers value
Â  Â  "rippableAmount": 0, // Defines rippable quantity
Â  Â  "value": 0,
Â  Â  "recognition": 0,
Â  Â  "decayInfo": 6, // 6 by default
Â  Â  "isBulletproof": false, // Used by New Firearms 1.5.0+. No effect if no new firearms

Â  Â  "container": false, // Conflicts with "bottle"
Â  Â  "maxWeight": 0.0,
Â  Â  "maxWeightPerItem": 0.0,
Â  Â  "encumberanceMult": 0.0, // Weight of items in container multiplier by this value
Â  Â  "itemsVisible": false,
Â  Â  "tagRestriction": [ "string", ], // Leave empty/null to not have restrictions

Â  Â  "weightReduction": false, // Requires "container"
Â  Â  "weightReductionSelf": -0.0, // If operational, this value will get ADDED on top of current enumberance

Â  Â  "maxBatterySize": 0, // not 0 makes clothing battery powered, 1 - small, 2 - medium, 3 - large

Â  Â  "explodeAtZero": false, // Explode similar to gravbag when condition reaches below 0.5% and (if battery powered then also have battery)

Â  Â  "bottle": false, // Confilicts with "container",
Â  Â  "liquidCapacity": 0.0,
Â  Â  "defaultLiquid": "string",
Â  Â  "autoFill": "string",
Â  Â  "fillmentSpritesPath": "string",

Â  Â  "autoZoomBehaviour": false,
Â  Â  "climbingClawsBehaviour": false, // doesnt drain condioton on walljumps
Â  Â  "headlampBehaviour": false,
Â  Â  "scubaGearBehaviour": false,
}[/spoiler]

Name: Meow Sound Swap
Description:
[url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/221][b][size=5]THIS MOD HAS ENTIRELY BEEN SUPERSEDED BY ULTIMATE SOUND SWAP[/size][/b] [/url]

(if you want the voyager sound, just take the voymeow1.wav from this mod with you)

Replaces several player vocal sounds with custom meowing clips:

- B key bark sounds -> meow clips
- Pain whimper sounds -> hiss clip
- Depressed growl -> custom growl clip

Requires BepInEx 5 x64.

## Installation

1. Install BepInEx 5 x64 for the game.
2. Download this mod and extract the zip into the game folder.
3. The final layout should look like this:

`CasualtiesUnknown.exe`
`BepInEx/plugins/VoySoundSwap.dll`
`BepInEx/plugins/VoySoundSwapSounds/voymeow1.wav`

Name: Prosthetics
Description:
[b]Prosthetics mod, allows you to replace a missing limb with a prosthetic, that changes gameplay, visuals both as in world items, sprites on your character and in Wound UI. (works with changeskin and spritereplacer mods)[/b]

[b][color=#00ff00]MP STATUS:[/color] Works if both host and client have the mod and requirements (there will be bugs, report them here pls)
[/b]
[b][color=#ff0000]Balancing[/color]: the balancing is WIP I would love to hear your opinion on what can be improved, or changed.[/b]

[b][color=#ff0000]ITS WIP[/color], For the future ideas go to the end of description, heres what's currently in the mod:[/b]

[b][color=#783f04]Wooden [/color]prosthetics:[/b]
[list]
[*]Get +25% damage from all sources;[/*]
[*]Deals 80% damage to environment;[/*]
[*]Slow you down if you have em as legs;[/*]
[*]Wooden arms cant hold anything besides containers (theese are hooks bro);[/*]
[*]Upon breaking without wrench you recieve glass shards (splinters) on your nearest normal limb;[/*]
[*]Can be healed via applying bio-chem, wood scraps, wood panels, sticks in medical ui.

[size=4]New sprites for mini games made byÂ  Il_Disabile[/size][/*]
[/list][b][color=#999999]Scrap [/color]Prosthetics[/b]
[list]
[*][b]prty much the same as a normal limb[/b][/*]
[*]they dont bleed, infect or get a broken bone[/*]
[*]can be repaired with: scrap metal, panel, bundle of wires.[/*]
[/list][b][color=#1155cc]TITANIUM [/color]limbs:[/b]
Buffs are influenced by hp of the limbs and how wet they are, they can give you debuffs if limbs are damaged or wet.
[b]Arms[/b]:
[list]
[*]Deal more damage with claws, tools, melee weapons[/*]
[*][s]When you put something in the titanium arm slot it halves the weight of that item..[/s][/*]
[*]Act as hammering tools when you have em equipped (you can finally make juice with hands)[/*]
[/list][b]Legs[/b]:
[list]
[*]Make you go faster and jump higher[/*]
[*]if you have 2 titanium legs it will prevent fall damage unless you are falling while being ragdolled[/*]
[/list][b]Also[/b]:
[list]
[*]Titanium limbs can be repaired with titanium sheets they restore both "skin" and "muscle"[/*]
[*]Titanium limbs cant be fully destroyed unless eaten by an elder.[/*]
[*]Extremely rare and expensive if you find them on traders.[/*]
[*]You cant take em off unless they have > 80% hp[/*]
[*]Have 2 mod slots each.
[/*]
[/list][b][color=#ff7700]Copper Jaw prosthetic[/color][/b]:
[list]
[*]Can be apllied when you lose a jaw[/*]
[*]Gives 25% dmg resistance to the head[/*]
[*]Defends against shrapnel and dislocations[/*]
[*]Broken bone on head event will destroy it tho, no need to fix the "bone"[/*]
[*]Items in the mouth slot will not be dropped when you are unconscious[/*]
[*]You gain only half of the mood from food sources[/*]
[/list][color=#00ff00]Prosthetic [b]EYE[/b][/color]:

Â â€” a one-shot surgical tool that opens an augment socket directly in a biological arm or leg. Now you can run Powergrip, Steady Hand, Multi-Hacking, or Powersteps without committing to a full prosthetic.

[list]
[*]Powered by small,medium batteries[/*]
[*]Restores sight if its powered[/*]
[*]Can be your flashlight if you hit F, and if you have both prosthetic eyes the light will be stronger! but at a cost of more energy consumption.[/*]
[*]You can take out the battery while its in your head via wound ui, or pull it out into inventory and do it there[/*]
[*]Battery in the prosthetic eye will take damage instead of the eye if it comes to that, if theres no battery the prosthetic eye may be broken same way a normal eye can.[/*]
[*]Prosthetic eyes can break. If a battery is loaded when it would break, the battery gets destroyed instead and the eye survives â€” so a charged eye is its own shield.[/*]
[/list]
[b]Bonesaw[/b]:
[b]Added:[/b] Two new bonesaws â€” a Makeshift Bonesaw you can craft, and a Medicinal Bonesaw that must be found in the world.
Both allow you to sever a limb regardless of its current state, and do so more efficiently than any other tool.

[b]Plastic fork[/b]: AllowsÂ  you to get rid of your "useless" biological eye out to make space for an upgrade.

[color=#9900ff][b]NEW CHALLENGE MODE:[/b][/color]
You start with wooden limbs and an empty prosthetic eye, but just to make it a little more fair you start with 13 int and a flimsy knife at your feet.

[b][color=#00ffff][size=5]NEW UI[/size][/color][/b]
[list]
[*]Press V to activate[/*]
[*]You can equip/unequip augments and mods via this interface.[/*]
[*]You can see the statuses of your augmentations and mods (battery charge, uses left ext.)[/*]
[/list][heading]Brain Mods[/heading]
[list]
[*][b]Pain Blocker[/b]Â â€” Halves all incoming pain.Â [i]Ejection: brain flash + vomiting.[/i][/*]
[*][b]Trap Detector[/b]Â â€” Red glow on beartraps, mines, coils, turrets, spikes.Â [i]Ejection: brain flash + vomiting.[/i][/*]
[*][b]Sonic Screamer[/b]Â â€” Active. 25-unit sonic burst sends nearby creatures fleeing for 2s.Â [i]3 uses, safe to eject.[/i][/*]
[*][b]Maso chipÂ [/b]â€” Pain hurts 10% more, but mood rises the more you suffer.Â [i]Ejection: 5 brain damage, brain flash, 15 head pain.[/i][/*]
[*][b]Adrenaline Surge[/b]Â â€” Active. Stand up from any ragdoll, even mid-air. Hurts like hell.Â [i]3 uses, safe to eject.[/i][/*]
[*][b]Sandivistian[/b]Â â€” Active. Military reflex booster, slows perceived time. Severe sickness after 10s.Â [i]3 uses, can't eject while active, auto-breaks on last use.[/i][/*]
[*][i][b]Scanning Chip [/b]â€” hold an item, activate, 3 s scan reveals the recipe and permanently marks the item as recognized (saved across runs)
[/i]
[color=#a4c2f4]Notes:
- brain mods will now be in their own category of items
- brain mods can be found in new Neuromod boxes (1-3 spawn on each layer, you'll need to find em)
- Milkies are more likely to have one brain mod in their catalogue[/color][/*]
[/list]
[color=#ff0000][b]Augmenting Biological Limbs[/b][/color]

[list]
[*][color=#ffffff][b]Augment Implant Tool[/b]Â â€” a one-shot surgical tool that opens an augment socket directly in a biological arm or leg. Now you can run Powergrip, Steady Hand, Multi-Hacking, or Powersteps without committing to a full prosthetic.[/color][/*]
[*][color=#ffffff]Use it in the wound view on a Shoulder or Thigh.[/color][/*]
[*][color=#ffffff]The socket dies with the limb â€” if the limb gets cut off, the socket is gone.[/color][/*]
[/list]
[color=#9900ff][size=5][b]Limb Augments[/b]Â [/size][/color]
Battery-powered modifications you install into prosthetic limbs (or into a biological limb if you've used an Implant Tool â€” see below). All come with their own sprites and sound effects. Toggle them and check status from the cybernetics panel (V by default).
[list]
[*][b]Steady Hand[/b]Â â€” wrist gyroscope. Cuts gun recoil in half while powered, and shows a live ammo count next to your held item. Small or medium battery.[/*]
[*][b]Powergrip[/b]Â â€” power-assisted palm grip. Held items feel half as heavy, you hit harder, you throw farther, and you keep your grip even if you pass out. Small or medium battery.[/*]
[*][b]Multi-Hacking[/b]Â â€” skips lock-picking minigames, keypads, and blood terminals at a battery cost per use. Small battery can't crack blood terminals; medium can.[/*]
[*][b]Powersteps[/b]Â â€” servo pistons in the thigh. Run faster and jump higher while active: +12.5% with one leg, +25% with both. Small, medium, or large battery. One hotbar key fires both legs at once.[/*]
[/list][heading]Grappling Hook (Arm Prosthetic Mod)[/heading]
[list]
[*]Installs into aÂ [b]scrap[/b]Â orÂ [b]titanium[/b]Â arm prosthetic via wound ui or the aug interface panel (press V)[/*]
[*]Battery-powered â€” each fire drains charge; hook becomes inert when depleted[/*]
[*]Live condition % shown in the cybernetics panelÂ [/*]
[*]1-second cooldown between shots so it can't be spammed.[/*]
[/list][line]
[b]Prosthetics[/b]
[list]
[*]They are craftable and visible as world items[/*]
[*]Visually: Seen on your character when applied both in UI and in game.[/*]
[/list][b]How They Work[/b]
[list]
[*]Apply to any missing limb directly from your inventory[/*]
[*]Restores full limb function â€” no movement penalty (wooden exception)[/*]
[*]The wound view updates to show the prosthetic in place of the missing limb[/*]
[/list][b]Repair[/b]
[list]
[*]Use the correct materal to repair skin and muscle damage of a prosthetic[/*]
[*]Medical items (bandages, bruise kits, LRDs, etc.) do not work on prosthetics[/*]
[/list][b]Prosthetics Don't...[/b]
[list]
[*]Bleed or drip blood[/*]
[*]Get infected[/*]
[*]Cause pain[/*]
[*]Heal passively over time[/*]
[*]Get broken bones[/*]
[*]Cant get injected with venom by all kinds of vile creatures[/*]
[*]Can not be targeted by self-harm event[/*]
[*]Dune doesnt bite em, if you dont have any normal limbs He wont bite you even if you ask him.[/*]
[/list][b]Breaking[/b]
[list]
[*]If skin or muscle health reaches 0, most prosthetics breaks apart[/*]
[*]Any impact that would normally break a bone destroys it instead[/*]
[*]On destruction: drops material at your feet[/*]
[/list][b]Ripped Dressings and Old Rags[/b]:
- You can fight off wetness with ripped dressings and old rags by using them on yourself

KeyBinds:
You can change the three keybinds of my mod game settings in ControllsÂ 

Main menu ui:
[list]
[*]its a button that alerts you if you need to update.[/*]
[*]When you click it aÂ  pop up appears that gives you more info and a link to download page.[/*]
[/list]
[line]
[b]Installation[/b] Unpack into:
[code]Game/BepInEx/plugins/[/code][b]
[img]https://staticdelivery.nexusmods.com/mods/8391/images/40/40-1781212506-2020377369.png[/img]
Thats how your plugins folder will look with the mod installed correctly.

Requirements[/b] BepInEx 5.4 Â· RshLib 2.1.2. ( idk 2.1.3. doesnt work without mp mod)



[b]IDEAS (Future Content):[/b]

[b]Augmentations / Modifications
[/b]
[list]
[*][b]Shields, you can carry in hand or equip them to your prosthetic, they take the damage from the direction to themselves and prevent your bodyparts from getting cut or shrappnelled, but you recieve blunt damage through them instead, which is reduced. As a con: climbing debuff (covers Arm on which is equipped, Torso, Abdomen, Head, Thighs)[/b][/*]
[*][b]RTG [/b]or a nuclear battery that yo can attach to your chest via implant tool to make all prosthetics and their mods recharge passively all the time at the cost of having a permanent 2 radiation sickness all the time. Or you can carry it and when it charges batteries it emits radiation 0.1 rad per second when activated (mb you should leave it somewhere then come back or risk the exposure?).[/*]
[/list]
[b]Minigames:[/b]
Change the "Hand sprite" in minigames to the current prosthetic sprite.
I'm thinking about making a brain chip install/eject mini game. (if you fuck up it dmgs brain integrity)
Maybe: Prosthetic installation.
Maybe: Cybernetic installation (could be cool to have an insert eye minigame)

If you want to contact me, or stay updated abt the mod via discord you can do that on this server:Â  [url=https://discord.gg/aCBrFPYYjs]https://discord.gg/aCBrFPYYjs[/url]

P.S. BIG Thanks to everyone who supported and supports me, and YOU for scrolling down here ;)

Name: Re-Growth Serums
Description:
[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]Tested and made on Steam version 6.1

Adds 2Â Craftable and Findable Serums to regrow lost Eyes or Limbs!

It hurts regenerating eyes, and quite violent to your body for limbs though, so make sure you are prepared.

Fully works in Steam MP with latest Rshlib of 2.0.0
Also basically compatible out of the box withÂ [url=https://next.nexusmods.com/profile/YOU1ARE0IC][/url][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/profile/YOU1ARE0IC]YOU1ARE0IC[/url]'s wonderful [url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/40]Prosthetics[/url] mod[/color]

[size=4][b]Installation instructions[/b][/size]
[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]Just install like other Item mods that use RshLib, right into your "/BepInEx/plugins" folder
Should have regrowthserums_data folder, and ReGrowthSerums.dll next to the RshLib.dll[/color][/size]

[size=4][b]Main features[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8][b][u][i]Eye Growth Serum:[/i][/u][/b]
- Regrows one eye per use[/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]- Used like a Neural Booster
- Similar side effects as if using a second NeuralBooster (Much much lighter though, mostly just some pain, minor head tissue damage, drop in hunger, and jump in body temp)

- Can even use after losing both eyes from the NeuralBooster! (Weakens the boosters effects though, and causes new and stronger side effects, you got a bigger brain so...)
- Rough effects If used after having used any Neural Booster:Â 
50 sickness
10 brain damage
Vomit
Undo part of the effect of the booster by 10% (Boosters Increase by 25%, so getting one eye back would result in the booster only boosting stats by 15%, and both eyes would be only a 5% boost.)

-Also, same mindwipe and hollow effect if used under the effects of brain grow sickness.[/color]
[color=#D4D4D8]
[b][i][u]Limb Re-Growth Serum:[/u][/i][/b][/color]
- Used like an L.R.D. on the health panel. (But Sir, how can I apply to the missing limb?)
- Use on an existing limb! Regrows an adjacent limb! (If you got no arms, use on your chest and a new stubby bicep will grow, just the bicep though, need multiple usages to regrow a whole arm or leg)
- Very violent, so does a bunch of pain and tissue damage to the host limb, breaks their bones, and leaves open and internal wounds on both limbs. (so be prepared to patch it up)
- Fresh new limb starts with 0 usability, you need to let it heal/regrow after.
- Some strong disinfectant is also applied with the usage, but not enough to fully prevent infection if you have low immunity

- Restrictions: Cannot use on a very damaged limb, you need to have at least 80 skin and muscle health on the donor limb. (Broken bones are fine!)

[size=4][b]Requirements[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8][size=3]RshLib and its requirements[/size][/color]

[size=4][b]Shout outs[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8]RushellXYZ for their RshLib and their other mods with their source code so I could see how it was done.[/color]

[/b][/size]Made and tested on the latest 6.1 version on steam.

Author: RushellXYZ

Name: RshLib
Description:
[b][size=4][font=Arial]Installation instruction[/font][/size][/b]
Download `RshLib.3.0.2` and put RshLib.dll into BepInEx/plugins
You can ensure that RshLib is loaded by seeing "This is a modded beta" in top of screen

[size=4][b]If you're installing mods for first time (dont have BepInEx)[/b][/size]
Go to BepInEx download page
https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases/tag/v5.4.23.5
And download BepInEx_win_x64_5.4.23.5
Open steam, choose browse local file
Extract BepInEx contents into game folder's root (winhttp.dll should be in same folder as executable)
Open game once and close
Now folderÂ BepInEx/plugins should be created

Additional instruction for Linux users:
[spoiler]AddÂ  `WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winhttp=n,b"Â  %command%` into launch arguments[/spoiler]
[b][size=4]Multiplayer support![/size][/b]
Both client and server should have RshLib and all other mods

[b][size=4]Known bugs[/size][/b]
None


[b][size=4]For developers[/size][/b]
[spoiler]Why are you even need RshLib?
In a nutshell: If two different mods try to implement code for custom items, only one will work. RshLib implements this code and other mods now tells RshLib to add they're item into that code

Building example
Put RshLib.dll into BepInEx/plugins/
In RshLibExample.csproj replace all /home/rushell/Documents/CU/ to your path to game
then `dotnet build`
Put RshLibExample.dll from bin/Debug/netstandard2.1/ to BepInEx/plugins/
And put neuraldestroyer.png to BepInEx/plugins/ too

Writing plugin for custom item:
There's source code of example, you might wanna check it
Firstly add reference to RshLib.dll into csproj
Add com.rushellxyz.rshlib to BepInExDependency
Create new ItemInfo, you can see how it works in game's code
Give your item fullname, description
ALWAYS call SetTags() for every ItemInfo
And call RshLib.Plugin.RegisterItem(itemId, new RshItem
{
Â  sprite = Sprite,
Â  info = ItemInfo
})
There's also onSpawn option in case if you wanna add component to item and baseItem if you wanna fix to shitty colliders

P.S. The thumbnail is supposed to be like a gears, but im not an artist to draw >< if someone could upload something better i will make it a thumbnail[/spoiler]

Bleach heals sickness and makes you a lil happy.
Bandages, bruise kits no longer heal your muscles or skin, just stops bleeding

Author: YOU1ARE1GA

Name: Time Limit Unknown
Description:
[size=4][b]Description[/b][/size]

[i]Note:Â  To my knowledge, Time Limit: Unknown is fully compatible with the latest Steam Demo / Beta release (v6.x)[/i]

[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]I created Time Limit: Unknown, to fix a simple problem I had with the built-in Time Limit in Casualties: Unknown.[/color][/size]

[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]Although you can toggle the Time Limit On / Off using the built-in difficulty settings, there is no way to configure the actual Time Limit itself. This mod aims to fix that problem by allowing you to adjust the Time Limit using a configuration file.[/color][/size]

[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]Do you want to double the Time Limit, and set it to something like 2 Hours? You can do that.[/color][/size]

[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]Trying to speed run? You want to make the Time Limit even less forgiving, like 20 or 30 minutes? You can do that.Â [/color][/size]

[size=3][color=#D4D4D8]Do you absolutely hate yourself? Do you hate having fun? Do you want the Rad Line to start immediately? You can do that. Just set it to 0 or 1.[/color][/size]



[b][size=4]Requirements[/size][/b]

You will need BepInEx / Harmony in order for Time Limit: Unknown. If you don't know how to install or use BepInEx with this game, see the installation instructions.



[size=4][b]Installation and Usage Instructions
[/b][/size]
[color=#D4D4D8][size=3]Full Installation Instructions:[/size][/color]
[spoiler][color=#D4D4D8][size=3][i]Note for Linux users:Â  You will likely need to use something like Wine or Proton to run the game when using a Linux based Operating System. Modded or not.
[/i][/size][/color]
[list=1]
[*]Download BepInEx from the official repository:Â Â [url=https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases/download/v5.4.23.5/BepInEx_win_x64_5.4.23.5.zip]https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases/download/v5.4.23.5/BepInEx_win_x64_5.4.23.5.zip[/url]Â ([url=https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases]https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases[/url]). Make sure to specifically download the win_x64 version.[/*]
[*]Go to the folder / directory where the Casualties: Unknown game is located / installed (specifically, the folder containing CasualtiesUnknown.exe).[/*]
[*]Extract / Copy all of the contents in the BepInEx archive directly into the folder (the one containing CasualtiesUnknown.exe)[/*]
[*]Launch the game once in order for BepInEx to generate any necessary files, then close the game. A BepInEx folder should now exist under the game's folder / directory.[/*]
[*]Download the Time Limit: Unknown mod (TimeLimitUnknown.dll)[/*]
[*]Go to [Game Directory] > BepInEx > plugins, and place TimeLimitUnknown.dll in the plugins folder.[/*]
[*]Launch the game (again) in order to generate the configuration file for Time Limit: Unknown, then close the game (again)[/*]
[*]If done correctly, the configuration file for Time Limit: Unknown (com.chaofanj.timelimitunknown) should now exist under [Game Directory] > BepInEx > config, and you can now change the Time Limit using the provided configuration file.[/*]
[/list][/spoiler]

Mod Only Installation Instructions (If you are already using BepInEx / other mods with Casualties: Unknown:Â 
[spoiler][list=1]
[*]Download the Time Limit: Unknown mod (TimeLimitUnknown.dll)[/*]
[*]Go to [Game Directory] > BepInEx > plugins, and place TimeLimitUnknown.dll in the plugins folder.Â [/*]
[*]Launch the game in order to generate the configuration file for Time Limit: Unknown, then close the game.[/*]
[*]The configuration file for Time Limit: Unknown (com.chaofanj.timelimitunknown) should now exist under [Game Directory] > BepInEx > config, and you can now change the Time Limit using the provided configuration file. [/*]
[/list][/spoiler]

[size=3]How to use Time Limit: Unknown:[/size]
[spoiler][list]
[*]If you have not already done so, you must load the game with the Time Limit: Unknown mod installed, then close the game. This generates the configuration file for the mod.[/*]
[*]To set / change the Time Limit, go to [Game Directory] > BepInEx > config and open Time Limit: Unknown's configuration file (com.chaofanj.timelimitunknown.cfg), and set the TimeLimit value to whatever you want (in seconds), save the changes to the configuration file, then launch the game.[/*]
[*]The Time Limit value is loaded / set when the game first starts up. If you want to change the Time Limit to something else, you will need to close the game, change the TimeLimit value, then launch the game again[/*]
[/list][/spoiler]



[b][size=4]Compatibility[/size][/b]

[size=3]Due to the simple nature of this mod, it should generally work with any Casualties: Unknown mods, unless said mod specifically alters the Time Limit value.[/size]

[size=3]Time Limit: Unknown has also been tested with the QoL: Unknown mod and should work fine, since Time Limit: Unknown only alters the Time Limit.[/size]

[size=3]This has not been tested with Casualties: Together multiplayer mod ([url=https://github.com/Krokosha666/cas-unk-krokosha-multiplayer-coop]https://github.com/Krokosha666/cas-unk-krokosha-multiplayer-coop)[/url]. I would assume it would work, as long as each player has the mod, and the value in the configuration for each player is the exact same (but still, needs testing).[/size]



[size=4][b]Tools / Programs used:[/b][/size]

[color=#D4D4D8][size=3][b]Visual Studio (2026)[/b][/size][/color]
[list]
[*][color=#D4D4D8][size=3]Necessary to write any necessary code, and compile the mod DLL.[/size][/color][/*]
[/list][color=#D4D4D8][size=3][b]ILSpy[/b][/size][/color]
[list]
[*][color=#D4D4D8][size=3]Needed to check / decompile Casualties: Unknown's source code, and to figure out what specifically controls setting the Time Limit.[/size][/color][/*]
[/list][color=#D4D4D8][size=3][b]KoboldCPP +Â Qwen3-Nemotron-235B-A22B-GenRM.i1-IQ2_M (Large Language Model)[/b][/size][/color]
[list]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]...Yes, I (mostly) vibe coded this, don't judge me. I don't have the time (or brain cells) to fully learn how to program in C#. I still had to find the necessary values using ILSpy myself, and "hand hold" the AI model. I also manually checked, and cleaned up the code myself.[/color][/*]
[*][color=#D4D4D8]Fun fact: The LLM in question was run entirely on local hardware, no ChatGPT / OpenAI, or any other models were used.[/color][/*]
[/list]

Name: FixSeeCorpse
Description:
Overview

This mod completely reworks corpse detection mechanics. Instead of vanilla instant detection within a 7-tile radius (ignoring walls and lighting), players must now actually see the corpse

Realistic Visibility Checks

Â  Â  Field of view check (120Â° cone in look direction)
Â  Â  Line of sight check (corpses behind walls are not detected)
Â  Â  Camera bounds check (corpse must be on screen)
Â  Â  Lighting check (corpses in complete darkness are not noticed)
Â  Â Â 
Gradual Detection

Â  Â  Corpses must be looked at for a period of time before being noticed
Â  Â  Close corpses (â‰¤10m) are spotted quickly (~0.15s)
Â  Â  Distant corpses (â‰¥45m) take longer to notice (~1.5s)
Â  Â  Low consciousness adds up to +3.5s detection delay

Multiplayer Support

Â  Â  Full compatibility with KrokoshaCasualtiesMP mod
Â  Â  Works correctly for both host and clients

Installation

Â  Â  Install BepInEx 5.x
Â  Â  Place it in BepInEx/plugins/

Configuration

Currently no configuration file.

Description:
Shrapnel Overhaul completely transforms the way explosions and gunfights feel in Scav.

Tired of simple flashes and disappearing smoke? This mod replaces vanilla explosions with hundreds of real, physically simulated fragments that fly through the air, ricochet off metal, embed into walls, and seriously injure anyone caught in the crossfire.

Gunfights are equally brutal: bullets kick up massive directional dust clouds, rip chunks out of walls, and create blinding showers of sparks when hitting metal.

Everything is fully synchronized in multiplayer and heavily optimized so your game won't lag, even during massive chain reactions.

ðŸ’¥ Key Features
- Explosions that Matter: Real flying debris (metal, stone, wood) that can hurt players, enemies (spiders), and destroy turrets. Ground mines form an upward cone of death, while dynamite throws heavy, blunt rocks.
- Intense Gunfights (Updated for v1.0!): Bullets transfer kinetic energy through walls. Dust sprays backward from the impact, metal conducts energy creating spark showers, and guns emit a concussive gas blast from the muzzle.
- Dynamic Environments: The mod reacts to the world. Sand flies in the desert, steam hisses in the freezing cold. Hot metal glows in dark caves and sizzles if it lands in water.
- Watch Your Step: Shrapnel stays on the ground. If you step on sharp glass or metal barefoot, you'll bleed. Wear boots to protect yourself!
- Smooth Performance: Built on a custom "Zero-GC" particle engine. Even with thousands of particles on screen, your game won't freeze or drop frames.

ðŸŒ Multiplayer Compatibility
This mod is 100% compatible with the Co-op Multiplayer Mod. Every spark and shard is synced between players using a highly optimized, server-authoritative custom network protocol.
*(Note: It works perfectly in singleplayer too!)*

ðŸ› ï¸ Installation
1. Install BepInEx 5.4.21+ in your game folder.
2. Place ScavShrapnelMod.dll into CasualtiesUnknownDemo/BepInEx/plugins/
3. Launch the game! (A configuration file will generate automatically, allowing you to tweak particle counts, lifetimes, and more).

âŒ¨ï¸ Console Commands
Open the in-game console with `~`.
- `shrapnel_explode dynamite` - Spawns an explosion at your cursor.
- `shrapnel_shot rifle R -metal` - Tests the bullet impact effects.
- `shrapnel_clear` - Destroys all active shrapnel to clean up the map.
- `shrapnel_status` - Shows mod performance and network stats.

P.S: I know that my game screenshots are outdated, and I will try to update them as soon as possible...
Author: Scream034 or paralax034

Name: Player sprite replacer
Description:
[size=6][b]Casualties Unknown - Player Sprite Replacer[/b][/size]

A small hobby mod for Casualties Unknown that swaps your character's body and head sprites at runtime using BepInEx. Single-player, numpad-driven.

[size=5][b]Compatibility[/b][/size]

[list]
[*]Loader: [b]BepInEx 5.4.23.5[/b][/*]
[*]Game version: [b]Casualties Unknown demo v6.1[/b][/*]
[/list]
The game is still in demo â€” file layouts can change between versions, which may break the mod until it's updated.


[size=5][b]Permissions[/b][/size]

This is a hobby project I made for fun. If the game creator [b]Orsoniks[/b] does not want this mod public, please contact me and I will take it down.


[size=5][b]Installation[/b][/size]

[size=4][b]1. Prerequisite[/b][/size]

Install BepInEx 5 if you haven't already â€” official guide: [url=https://docs.bepinex.dev/articles/user_guide/installation/index.html]https://docs.bepinex.dev/articles/user_guide/installation/index.html[/url]

[size=4][b]2. The mod[/b][/size]

[list=1]
[*]Open the mod archive and look inside
[code]body-sprite-replacer/plugins[/code].[/*]
[*]Copy its contents into your game's
[code]BepInEx/plugins[/code]folder â€” the same folder used by other mods. You should end up with the Player Sprite Replacer DLL and a
[code]CustomSprites[/code]directory.[/*]
[*]Start the game.[/*]
[/list]

[size=5][b]Usage[/b][/size]

[list]
[*]In-game, press [b]Numpad 1 through 9[/b] to instantly switch between different character sprites.[/*]
[*]The mod looks for sprites in
[code]CustomSprites/st1[/code]through
[code]CustomSprites/st9[/code].[/*]
[*][b]No numpad?[/b]
[code]st1[/code]is loaded automatically on startup, so drop your preferred skin into
[code]st1[/code]and you're set.[/*]
[/list]

[size=5][b]Sprite credits[/b][/size]

[list]
[*][code]st1[/code]â€” [b]Mottrew[/b] by [b]beebigirl[/b]: [url=https://skin.cat-bot.de/?skinid=163&page=4#skin-163]https://skin.cat-bot.de/?skinid=163&page=4#skin-163[/url][/*]
[*][code]st2[/code]â€“
[code]st9[/code]â€” sprite files copied from GitHub user [b]Paili-16[/b]. (I could have extracted them from the game myself but wanted to save time.)[/*]
[/list]

[size=5][b]Making your own sprites[/b][/size]

[list=1]
[*]Back up the
[code]Body[/code]and
[code]Head[/code]folders inside the
[code]st1[/code]â€“
[code]st9[/code]slot you're going to modify.[/*]
[*]Replace the PNGs with your own art.[/*]
[*][b]Keep the file names exactly the same[/b] â€” sprites are matched by file name; renaming them breaks replacement.[/*]
[*]Try to match the original image dimensions. I haven't tested what happens when they don't, so YMMV.[/*]
[/list]

[size=5][b]Finding sprites[/b][/size]

Places to grab community sprites:

[list]
[*][url=https://skin.cat-bot.de/]https://skin.cat-bot.de/[/url] â€” note: this mod expects
[code]Body[/code]and
[code]Head[/code]as separate folders, so you'll need to split the downloaded files until I add single-file support.[/*]
[*][url=https://github.com/Paili-16/Scav-Prototype-The-Characters-Skins]https://github.com/Paili-16/Scav-Prototype-The-Characters-Skins[/url][/*]
[/list]

[size=5][b]Notes[/b][/size]

[list]
[*]You do [b]not[/b] need to edit
[code]sharedassets1.assets[/code]for this mod.[/*]
[*]Multiplayer support is planned for the future. For now, see the alternatives section below if you need to play in multiplayer.[/*]
[/list]

[size=5][b]Contributing & support[/b][/size]

GitHub: [url=https://github.com/dogederp/CasultiesUnknown_Player-sprite-replacer]https://github.com/dogederp/CasultiesUnknown_Player-sprite-replacer[/url]

[list]
[*]Pull requests welcome.[/*]
[*]Open an issue for questions or bug reports.[/*]
[*]Got screenshots or videos of your skins? Feel free to add them to this page â€” I'd love to see them.[/*]
[*]GitHub (primary) gets updates first, before NexusMods.[/*]
[*]Buy me a coffee: [url=https://buymeacoffee.com/dogederp]https://buymeacoffee.com/dogederp[/url][/*]
[/list]

[size=5][b]Looking for an alternative?[/b][/size]

If this mod doesn't fit your needs:

[list]
[*][url=https://www.nexusmods.com/scavprototype/mods/118]ChangeSkin Multiplayer[/url] â€” multiplayer-compatible skin replacer. Use this one if you need multiplayer support.[/*]
[*][url=https://github.com/05126619z/ChangeSkin]https://github.com/05126619z/ChangeSkin[/url] â€” single-player ChangeSkin by 05126619z.[/*]
[/list]

[/list]

Name: Custom Structures
Description:
[center][size=6][b]CU-Custom-Structures[/b][/size]
[size=4][b]BepInEx mod for Casualties: Unknown, adding custom user-generated structures
[/b][/size]
[img]https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/e5339102-374f-4d4d-904d-f3b21a762686[/img]
[url=https://github.com/jimmyking9999999/CU-Custom-Structures/raw/refs/heads/main/CustomStructures.zip][img]https://img.shields.io/badge/Download-v1.2.1-orange?logo=github[/img][/url][/center]

[size=5][b]Overview[/b][/size]
A [url=https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx]BepInEx[/url] mod for [url=https://orsonik.itch.io/scav-prototype]Casualties: Unknown[/url] that adds support for custom user-generated structures, including over 130 pre-made structures and a webtool to create and share your own.

[list]
[*]Adds >250 new custom structures to CU[/*]
[*]A tool to implement your own custom structures with any object in-game[/*]
[*]Easy editing and sharing of structures via .txt files on the online webtool[/*]
[/list]

[line]
[size=5][b]Installation[/b][/size]
Please follow these steps exactly to ensure your mod loads correctly!

[spoiler]
[size=4][b]Installing the Mod Loader (BepInEx)[/b][/size]
[list=1]
[*]Download [b]BepInEx v5.4.23.3[/b] (x64) from the official repository: [url=https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases/download/v5.4.23.3/BepInEx_win_x64_5.4.23.3.zip]Download BepInEx_win_x64_5.4.23.3.zip[/url][/*]
[*]Navigate to your [i]Casualties: Unknown[/i] game folder (the folder containing [font=Courier New]CasualtiesUnknown.exe[/font]).[/*]
[*]Extract the contents of the downloaded zip file directly into this folder.[/*]
[*]If prompted, choose to "Replace all files."[/*]
[*]Run the game once to generate the necessary configuration folders, then close the game.[/*]
[*]If you have a [font=Courier New]BepInEx/Plugins[/font] folder, you have done this correctly![/*]
[/list][/spoiler]

[spoiler]
[size=4][b]Installing CU-Custom-Structures[/b][/size]
[list=1]
[*]Navigate to your game folder and open the newly created path: [font=Courier New]BepInEx/Plugins[/font].[/*]
[*]Download the mod: [url=https://github.com/jimmyking9999999/CU-Custom-Structures/releases/]Download CustomStructures.zip[/url][/*]
[*]Extract the zip file directly into the [font=Courier New]BepInEx/Plugins[/font] folder.[/*]
[*]Launch the game.[/*]
[/list]
Your filesystem should look as follows:
[code]
CasualtiesUnknownDemo/
â””â”€â”€ BepInEx/
â””â”€â”€ plugins/
â”œâ”€â”€ CustomStructures.dll
â””â”€â”€ Structures/
â””â”€â”€ (your .txt structure files go here!)[/code][/spoiler]

[line]
[size=5][b]Webtool[/b][/size]
The structure editor webtool is hosted at [url=https://cu-structure-editor.web.app]cu-structure-editor.web.app[/url]. Create your own custom structures and share the .txt file with the world!
Â [img]https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d1b36828-1c37-4237-a98d-10fbc1dd28ac[/img]

[size=4][b]Keybinds[/b][/size]
[list]
- Middle mouse button to pick block & pan- â¨Shiftâ© to toggle Precision mode. Drag to adjust location.Â - Shift + Ctrlâ© to rotate, â¨Shift + Alt to scale- â¨Space to toggle foreground/background mode- â¨Ctrl + C / Vâ© to copy-paste[/list]

[line]
[size=5][b]Downloading Additional Structures[/b][/size]
[list=1]
[*]Visit the [url=https://cu-structure-editor.web.app/structures.html]structures page[/url] and download any structure .txt files you want.[/*]
[*]Move the files into [font=Courier New]BepInEx/Plugins/Structures[/font].[/*]
[*]Reload the game![/*]
[/list][img]https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/da769ece-e5b0-4aba-b919-fe0e2bb4c1a4[/img]

[line]
[size=5][b]Console Commands[/b][/size]
[list]
[*][font=Courier New]structure {id}[/font] â€” spawns a custom structure[/*]
[*][font=Courier New]locatestructure {id}[/font] â€” locates a custom structure[/*]
[*][font=Courier New]togglemorestructures {true/false}[/font] â€” enable/disable the mod until the command is run again[/*]
[*][font=Courier New]reloadstructures[/font] â€” reloads all .txt files and refreshes changes[/*]
[/list]

[img]https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d507e195-c90c-4390-8247-1251e4d11314[/img]

Name: QoL Unknown
Description:
[center][size=6][b]QoL: Unknown[/b][/size]
[size=4][b]Quality of Life & Discord Rich Presence Mod for Casualties: Unknown[/b][/size]
[img]https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/96fe38ed-0df2-4a25-95f6-498e4627938f[/img]
[url=https://github.com/jimmyking9999999/QoL-Unknown/releases/latest][img]https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/jimmyking9999999/QoL-Unknown?include_prereleases&color=orange&logo=github[/img][/url] [url=https://github.com/jimmyking9999999/QoL-Unknown/releases][img]https://img.shields.io/github/downloads/jimmyking9999999/QoL-Unknown/total?color=blue&logo=github[/img]Â  [/url][url=https://camo.githubusercontent.com/02043245c697f67c0c398fedbbb7011848594f50c1d40ff704c0be01790f0487/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f62616467652f47616d655f76657273696f6e2d76372e312e302d677265656e][img]https://camo.githubusercontent.com/02043245c697f67c0c398fedbbb7011848594f50c1d40ff704c0be01790f0487/68747470733a2f2f696d672e736869656c64732e696f2f62616467652f47616d655f76657273696f6e2d76372e312e302d677265656e[/img][/url]
[/center]
[size=5][b]Overview[/b][/size]
This mod is a general quality of life mod for [url=https://orsonik.itch.io/scav-prototype]Casualties: Unknown[/url], meant to introduce accessibility and QoL features without changing the core mechanics of the game.

This mod works alongside mosother public mods!
[line]
[size=5][b]
Features[/b][/size]

[spoiler]
[b]UI, UX, Visuals[/b]
[list]
[*]Automatic integrated Discord Rich Presence game support[/*]
[*]Adjustable luminosity and bloom settings[/*]
[*]Adjustable screen effect intensity[/*]
[*]Anti-flash/epilepsy toggle[/*]
[*]Added up/down arrow navigation to select different choices for autofill in the console, as well as other console improvements[/*]
[*]Console spawn command now autofills and has a visual guide[/*]
[*]Enhanced the crafting menu item preview, with autocraft and multi-craft additions[/*]
[/list]
[img]https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/21be03c0-8182-4666-a6ba-eb4b0c6f29b9[/img][/spoiler]

[spoiler]
[b]Inventory[/b]
[list]
[*]Quick-transfer for liquids and items via [b]Shift + Drag[/b][/*]
[*]Inventory auto-sort, prioritizing saved slots at low total weight, and lowest weight otherwise[/*]
[*]Scroll bars for containers that go offscreen[/*]
[*]Quick item pickup using middle-mouse button[/*]
[/list]
[img]https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3898e7f5-5c83-4aae-80cd-b4eab6437833[/img][/spoiler]
[spoiler]
[b]Gameplay[/b]
[list]
[*]Mid-layer saves that retain world generation, traps, enemies, etc...[/*]
[*]1-tile water no longer pushes the player, and water physics is more lenient[/*]
[*]Seeded runs for consistent worlds. (This syncs worlds down to the same keycode and items from drop pods!)[/*]
[*]Layer modifiers are able to be adjusted individually (e.g., enabling only dried layers)[/*]
[*]Difficulty sliders are no longer restricted[/*]
[*]Sound cannon indicator[/*]
[*]Extra settings to retain several v6 behaviour[/*]
[/list]
[img]https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/77c1b3b8-9edf-4496-80b6-30b3c9902a4c[/img][/spoiler]
[spoiler]
[b]System, Customization[/b]
[list]
[*]In-game options menu[/*]
[*]In-game custom keybind page[/*]
[*]Master volume & sound slider[/*]
[*]Controller support for Xbox/XInput-based controllers[/*]
[*]Individual entity density options. This can increase geysers amounts if you really like them, or remove spiders fully if you have arachnophobia[/*]
[*]Individual structure density options (if you really (don't) want lifepods or other structures)[/*]
[*]One-click locale download button[/*]
[*]Pre-run skill point allocation[/*]
[*]Framerate, resolution, window settings[/*]
[*]Individual sound overrides, audio safety toggles[/*]
[*]Instant radline toggle[/*]
[/list]
[img]https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7294c336-d216-4c1e-88ef-689341ba41fc[/img][/spoiler]

[b]Note:[/b] Almost all features listed above include toggles for customization.

[line]
[size=5][b]Installation[/b][/size]
Please follow these steps exactly to ensure your mod loads correctly!

[spoiler]
[size=4][b]Windows Installation[/b][/size]
[b]A: Installing the Mod Loader (BepInEx)[/b]
[list=1]
[*]Download [b]BepInEx v5.4.23.4[/b] (x64) from the official repository: [url=https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases/download/v5.4.23.4/BepInEx_win_x64_5.4.23.4.zip]Download BepInEx_win_x64_5.4.23.4.zip[/url][/*]
[*]Navigate to your [i]Casualties: Unknown[/i] game folder (the folder containing [font=Courier New]CasualtiesUnknown.exe[/font]).
On steam release, this isC:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\Casualties Unknown Demo[/*]
[*]Extract the contents of the downloaded zip file directly into this folder.[/*]
[*]If prompted, choose to "Replace all files."[/*]
[*]Run the game once to generate the necessary configuration folders, then close the game.[/*]
[*]If you have a [font=Courier New]BepInEx/Plugins[/font] folder, you have done this correctly![/*]
[/list]
[b]B: Installing QoL: Unknown[/b]
[list=1]
[*]Navigate to your game folder and open the newly created path: [font=Courier New]BepInEx/Plugins[/font].[/*]
[*]Download the mod DLL file: [url=https://github.com/jimmyking9999999/QoL-Unknown/raw/refs/heads/main/QoL%20Unknown.dll]Download QoL Unknown.dll[/url][/*]
[*]Place the [font=Courier New]QoL Unknown.dll[/font] file inside the [font=Courier New]BepInEx/Plugins[/font] folder.[/*]
[*]Launch the game.[/*]
[/list]
Your filesystem should look as follows:
[code]
Casualties Unknown Demo/
â””â”€â”€ BepInEx/
Â  Â  â””â”€â”€ plugins/

[spoiler]
[size=4][b]Linux Installation[/b][/size]
[b]A: Installing the Mod Loader (BepInEx)[/b]
[list=1]
[*]Install wine & winetricks:
[code]pacman -Sy wine winetricks[/code][/*]
[*]Install dependencies for Unity:
[code]winetricks dotnet40 dotnet48 dotnet8 dotnet9 dotnetcore3 dotnetcoredesktop3 dotnetdesktop8 dotnetdesktop9 arial corefonts[/code][/*]
[*]Download [b]BepInEx v5.4.23.4[/b] (x64) from the official repository: [url=https://github.com/BepInEx/BepInEx/releases/download/v5.4.23.4/BepInEx_win_x64_5.4.23.4.zip]Download BepInEx_win_x64_5.4.23.4.zip[/url]
(Note: You [b]must[/b] download the win_x64 version)[/*]
[*]Extract the contents of the downloaded zip file directly into the C:U game folder.[/*]
[*]Open up winecfg ([font=Courier New]winecfg[/font]), and add [font=Courier New]winhttp.dll[/font] as a dll override (Libraries tab).[/*]
[*]Launch the game and close it. If you have a [font=Courier New]BepInEx/Plugins[/font] folder, you have done this correctly![/*]
[/list]
[b]B: Installing QoL: Unknown[/b]
[list=1]
[*]Navigate to your game folder and open the newly created path: [font=Courier New]BepInEx/Plugins[/font].[/*]
[*]Download the mod DLL file: [url=https://github.com/jimmyking9999999/QoL-Unknown/raw/refs/heads/main/QoL%20Unknown.dll]Download QoL Unknown.dll[/url][/*]
[*]Place the [font=Courier New]QoL Unknown.dll[/font] file inside the [font=Courier New]BepInEx/Plugins[/font] folder.[/*]
[*]Launch the game.[/*]
[*]To enable Discord RPC, read [url=https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/wiki/Enabling-Discord-Rich-Presence]here[/url].[/*]
[/list][/spoiler]
[line]
[size=5][b]FAQ[/b][/size]
[b]My game doesn't show the mod![/b]
[list]
[*]There is a known BepInEx issue where mods may fail to load if your path to the game folder contains Unicode characters. If so, try moving the game folder.[/*]
[*]Otherwise, you may have messed up the BepInEx or mod installation. Please follow the installation instructions carefully.[/*]
[*]If you still don't see the mod, ensure this mod supports your game version, and send me a ping in the Discord thread![/*]
[/list]
[b]What versions does this mod work on?[/b]

[i]Mod Version : Game Version[/i]
[list]
[*](v1.0.4.4): `v7.1`[/*]
[*](v1.0.4.3): `v6.1`[/*]
[*](v1.0.4.1): `v5.1.0, v6.0, v6.1` (Steam release) [/*]
[*](v1.0.3.2): `v5.0.3`, `v5.0.2`, `v5.0.1`, `Version 5 Pretest 5` (Itch.io)[/*]
[/list]
[b]Where are my saves kept?[/b]
[list]
[*]All saves and autosaves are kept in [font=Courier New]%appdata%/LocalLow/Orsoniks/CasualtiesUnknown/[/font] as .sv files[/*]
[/list]
[b]Does this work mid-run? Will my saves be deleted?[/b]
[list]
[*]Migrating from a non-modded run to a modded run works normally! (and vice versa)[/*]
[*]All your save data will stay the same, and you can continue off from where you left off.[/*]
[/list][b]Does this support multiplayer?
[/b]
[list]
[*][list]
v1.0.4.1 and above support multiplayer! Yay! Not all players need the mod for it to work, and it functions independantly.[/list][/*]
[/list]

Name: Cursor Character Drag (OUTDATED)
Description:
[b]WARNING: The mod is not compatible with the newest version of the game![/b]

I probably should have written this earlier, but yeah, the newest version broke the mod completely. The short answer to why is that this mod has aÂ ManagerObject dependency at its core, and this dependency breaks it in the newest version of the game. I tried to make a completely new mod from scratch that wouldn't requireÂ ManagerObject dependency, but I failed each time. So yeah, there's a good chance that this mod will not be coming back for a good while. I will try again when the game updates a bunch, in hope to find some solution to implement a cursor drag mechanic again.

As it stands now,Â Cursor Character DragÂ is only compatible with theÂ [b]V5 pre-testing 5[/b]Â version of the game. Sorry, everyone!

How to install it:
InstallÂ BepInEx in the game's executable folder.
Navigate to the folder:Â BepInEx > plugins, and extractÂ SandboxDragMode.dll there.

How to use it:
You can enable and disable SandboxDragMode by pressing F1 in-game (activation is only visible in console ~, there's no UI to indicate that the mode is active).
When SandboxDragMode is active, your attack is disabled, and you can drag your character around with your mouse however you like.
When SandboxDragMode is disabled, you can attack again.

If you encounter any issues, it's very likely that I won't be able to fix them.

If some actual coder wants to take my mod, improve it and reupload it, go ahead, don't even ask for my permission. Consider this piece of code a public domain.
